Description:
You call upon Abhaaldrac, the World Destroyer who drifts beyond the Far Cosmos, and invite a fragment of its attention to fall upon the world. The moment its presence presses against reality, the Loom begins to tear.

Across the affected region, existence destabilizes violently. Gravity bends toward impossible vectors, structures shear and realign, and the Veil fractures as alien geometries intrude. The land does not merely break, it is rewritten according to principles that do not belong to this world.

Anomalous entities and cosmic hazards manifest spontaneously, acting without pattern, allegiance, or mercy. Spellcraft within the area becomes dangerously unstable as Abhaaldrac’s influence distorts intent and outcome, causing magic to misfire, mutate, or recoil upon its caster.

At its highest invocation, Abhaaldrac does not withdraw. The descent completes, and the region becomes a permanent scar upon reality, a place where the Far Cosmos presses close and the world forever remembers that it was once seen by something vast enough to end it.
Scaling: - If cast at 10th level, the effect becomes permanent, transforming the area into a quarantined anomaly site.
Modifier Details: spellcraft within the area becomes dangerously unstable as Abhaaldrac's influence distorts intent an

Description:
You draw a momentary aegis of compressed force tight against your body. Until the start of your next turn, missiles, bolts, and hurled weapons strike as though the air itself resists them, their impact dulled before reaching you.

Those who attempt to wound you from afar feel the backlash of their own momentum, as the rebounding force snaps back along the path of the attack, bruising flesh and rattling bone.
Scaling: 1d6 per level above 2nd
Modifier Details: missiles, bolts, and hurled weapons strike as though the air itself resists them, their impact dulle

Foundry Tag: Modifies AC, Uses Bonus Action, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Modifies AC, Uses Bonus Action, Grants Advantage
Description:
You overwhelm the senses of up to six creatures within range with a hallucinated scent so vivid and strange that it threatens their grip on reality. The scent can be chosen by you upon casting and changes the spell’s effect. You may also choose whether the scent feels directional or all- consuming. Affected creatures must make a Constitution saving throw or suffer the selected effect.

At the end of each of their turns, affected creatures must repeat the saving throw. On two failed saves, the target becomes Stunned until the end of their next turn as the olfactory hallucination overwhelms their nervous system.

Choose one scent effect upon casting:

Rot and Filth: Targets that fail their save are Poisoned and suffer disadvantage on saving throws against charm and fear effects for the duration .

Sweet Perfume: Targets must succeed on a Wisdom save or be Charmed by you until the end of that turn.

Burnt Ozone: On a failed save, targets cannot take reactions until the start of their next turn and The creature has disadvantage on Perception and Insight checks.

You may use a bonus action on your turn to change the scent’s directionality (e.g., from everywhere to a specific direction).

The illusion lingers for 1 minute, and affected creatures must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns. On a success, the effect ends for them.

Condition Synergy: If a target is already Frightened, Charmed, or Poisoned when affected, it has disadvantage on the initial saving throw.

Obscured Battlefield: While under the spell’s effect, affected creatures are considered heavily obscured to others—they constantly shift position in reaction to the scent, creating a surreal mirage- like presence. This effect ends if the scent illusion ends.

Additionally, while the spell is active, you may use an action to cause the scent to mimic a recognizable compound—such as smoke, fuel, or blood—potentially triggering scent- based detection systems or distracting creatures with scent- based senses.
Flavor:
In the city of Concord, Alerrim's Solitude is outlawed, for it was once used by the cult of the Silent Maw to unmake a senator in broad daylight. Witnesses swore they saw his shadow scream before vanishing entirely.

Those who cast Alerrim's Solitude often find that they dream differently afterward. They dream of corridors that stretch into infinity, doors that lead to places older than time.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw

Description:
You summon a localized, magical downpour of harmless frogs in a 10- foot radius area within range. The frogs immediately cover the ground, creating difficult terrain for the duration. They persistently croak in gentle harmony, producing mild distraction but no other direct harm.

When the spell ends, the frogs disperse naturally, leaving no lasting effect.
Clarification: Clarifications:- The frogs are non- hostile and cannot be used for damage.- The difficult terrain affects all creatures equally, allies and enemies alike.
Table:
| Spell Slot Level | Radius of Effect |
|----------------------- |-------------------- |
| 1st-level | 10- foot radius |
| 2nd-level | 20- foot radius |
| 3rd-level | 30- foot radius |
| 4th-level | 40- foot radius |
| 5th-level or higher | 50- foot radius |
Modifier Details: produces mild distraction but no other direct harm

Description: You touch a crafted object or man-made structure and command it to forget its shape. Wood, stone, and metal separate cleanly into their original components, collapsing without shattering or violence. A blade divides into hilt and edge, a building falls back into beams, nails, and stone. The materials retain the memory of their former arrangement and may be restored without loss if reassembled within 48 hours. If rebuilt incorrectly, the components resist, forcing themselves back toward their original design.
Anomaly Effect: - If misused, the materials may refuse to be reassembled, taking on forms of their own choosing.
Range: Touch (up to 10 feet of crafeeted material)

Description:
You overstimulate a creature’s autonomic responses, flooding the body with involuntary signals divorced from intent or desire. Heart rate accelerates, skin flushes, pupils widen, and sensory thresholds spike as the body reacts without the mind’s consent.

While affected, the creature suffers disadvantage on Stealth checks as involuntary motion and heightened awareness disrupt subtlety. It gains advantage on checks relying on touch, hearing, or taste, but has disadvantage on concentration checks due to sensory overload. Creatures without nervous systems, or those immune to charm effects, are unaffected.

The reaction may be mistaken for excitement, panic, or agitation depending on context, often leading to social misinterpretation. The effect is purely physiological, a surge of reflex without command or emotion.
Clarification:
Optional Roleplay Effects:

The response may resemble nervousness, excitement, or panic depending on context. Social misinterpretations are common and potentially useful (or disastrous).

Notes for Tables:
- Reflavor it for espionage, interrogation, or psychic interference if you want to avoid overtly sexual interpretation.
- Mechanically, it behaves like a stimulant- overload or magic caffeine shot — sensory boost, motor disruption.
Modifier Details: suffers disadvantage on Stealth checks

Description: You exhale a puff of grayish smoke, which spreads in a small cone before quickly vanishing. Creatures in the area must make a Constitution saving throw or briefly choke on the acrid air. On a failed save, a creature cannot take reactions until the start of its next turn. This effect does not work against creatures that do not need to breathe.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw

Description:
You drive a needle of unstable ruin into the target, an invisible barb that seeds their body with volatile decay. On contact, the target takes 6d10 radiation damage and becomes contaminated for the duration. A faint, glowing scar shaped like a hooked barb marks the point of impact.

While contaminated, the target bleeds destructive energy in a 5-foot radius. Any creature that starts its turn within that radius, excluding you, takes 1d6 radiation damage. At the start of each of the target’s turns, it takes 1d4 radiation damage as its body struggles to contain the ruin. The contamination spreads to any creature that ends its turn grappling or touching the target.

If the target receives magical healing, the contamination ends for that creature, though the scar remains unless removed by Arcane Sterilization or Sigil of Restoration.
Flavor:
> “The sting doesn’t kill you. It kills your presence—makes you the danger.” 
> — Whispered at the ASTRAL quarantine labs
Anomaly Effect: On a 15 or higher when casting: The sting pulses again one round later, forcing the target to make a new Constitution saving throw or take an additional 2d10 radiation damage.
Modifier Details: target takes 6d10 radiation damage

Description:
A hovering writ of binding law manifests around you, forming an unseen but oppressive obstruction. For the duration, whenever a creature targets you with an attack, it must choose one of the following options:

• Submit a Petition: The creature forfeits its next action, doing nothing on its following turn.
• Endure the Queue: The creature’s movement speed is reduced by 10 feet for that turn.
• Defy the Writ: The creature makes the attack roll with disadvantage.

This effect persists for the spell’s full duration or until you are incapacitated.
Flavor: The spell was originally created by the Department of Magical Compliance to protect mid- level clerks from workplace aggression. It remains surprisingly effective.
Modifier Details: makes the attack roll with disadvantage

Foundry Tag: Modifies AC, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Modifies AC, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Description:
You awaken a heightened state of martial clarity, flooding the target with supernatural speed, awareness, and instinct.
	•	The target’s speed doubles.
	•	They gain a +2 bonus to AC.
	•	They have advantage on Dexterity saving throws.
	•	On each of their turns, they gain one additional action (Attack, Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use an Object).

When the spell ends, the surge collapses. The target’s body rebels against the sudden loss of heightened function, leaving them unable to move or take actions for one full round, overwhelmed by the aftershock of borrowed perfection.
Modifier Details: +2 bonus to ac
Component Description (Material): a data uplink or HUD visor

Description:
When a creature who has dealt necrotic damage in the past 24 hours enters Sanctified Terrain, you may call upon the terrain itself to reject them.

They must make a Constitution saving throw or take:- 6d6 chiaric damage- Become Incapacitated until the end of their next turn if they fail by 5 or more

On a success, they take half damage and are not incapacitated.
Modifier Details: fail by 5 or more
Trigger: when a creature enters sanctified terrain

Description:
You momentarily sync out of step with the immediate flow of time, creating a minor delay in perception.
 
Until the start of your next turn:
- Creatures that would notice you must make an Intelligence saving throw or fail to recall any details about your presence until you interact with them directly.
- If you attack or speak, the effect immediately ends for that creature.
Modifier Details: creatures must make an Intelligence saving throw or fail to recall any details about your presence

Description:
A kiss imbued with phantasmal delirium seeps into the target’s mind, unraveling their thoughts into surreal hallucinations.
 
- The target becomes heavily intoxicated, seeing phantom lights and distorted visions.
- They suffer disadvantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Dexterity checks.
- If they fail a Wisdom save, they believe their deepest desires have become real, embracing illusions as if they were flesh.
Modifier Details: suffers disadvantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Dexterity checks

Description:
You rupture the boundaries of cell cohesion in a targeted area, causing violent outgrowths of alien flesh and rapid, cancerous mutation.
All creatures in the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure:
- They take 8d10 biologic damage.
- They are Mutated for the duration.
- Difficult terrain forms as cancerous masses spread across the ground.

On a success, creatures take half damage and are not mutated.

If cast in an urban area, strange organic material fuses with infrastructure, leaving behind semi-living architecture.
Modifier Details: takes 8d10 biologic damage on failure

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Grants Advantage
Description:
You conjure and fling a dense glob of swamp muck at a creature within range. The bolt arcs with a disgusting squelch, trailing algae, peat, and mystery goo.

Make a ranged spell attack against a creature. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 impact damage and must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be coated in stinking mire for 1 minute.

While affected:- The target has disadvantage on Charisma checks involving persuasion, performance, or decorum.- The effect ends early if the creature spends an action to scrape or wash off the muck.
Flavor: This spell is often used less for harm and more for humiliation. The smell lingers longer than the magic.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Charisma saving throw

Description: You create a roiling burst of scalding liquid at a point you can see within range. Creatures within the area take 3d6 fire damage, or half as much on a successful Dexterity saving throw. The area becomes difficult terrain until the start of your next turn.
Modifier Details: takes half damage on a successful Dexterity saving throw

Description:
You accelerate the growth of parasitic flora hidden in the bones of the land.
- Choose a point on the ground within 30 ft. A twisting vine- bloom erupts in a 10 ft radius.
- Enemies that enter or start their turn there must succeed a Strength save or be restrained until the end of their next turn.
- The vines writhe as if remembering something.
Modifier Details: enemies must succeed a Strength save or be restrained
Component Description (Material): a root knot in the shape of a skull

Description: You twist the flow of possibility, stealing fortune from the immediate future. You gain advantage on the next attack roll you make against a creature you can see within range before the end of your next turn. Once the attack is resolved, the target gains advantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on the next attack roll

Description:
You urge a creature to draw briefly beyond its natural cadence, pulling strength and clarity from moments that have not yet arrived. The target’s body moves ahead of its own shadow, muscles and thought aligning into a single urgent flow.

For the duration, the target gains increased speed, heightened physical control, and sharpened focus, allowing swift movement and rapid repositioning that borders on precognition. Their steps are lighter, reactions faster, and decisions arrive without hesitation.

When the spell ends, the borrowed momentum collapses. The creature pays the cost of acting ahead of itself, gaining one level of exhaustion as time and flesh reclaim what was taken.
Modifier Details: gains increased speed

Description:
You seize a departing soul at the final threshold and wrench it back into flesh that has already begun to fail.

By laying your hand upon the fallen, you force life to resume in a body that has already crossed the brink. The creature gasps back into the world with a single flicker of vitality, yet something is wrong.

Its movements are stiff, breath shallow, and pulse irregular, as if its life is being held in place by will rather than truly restored. Until the body is allowed to fully reconcile with life through rest, it bears the weight of having died, dragging exhaustion behind every action.

Priests whisper that this rite does not revive the dead so much as delay the truth.
Modifier Details: movement and actions are stiff, breath shallow, and pulse irregular

Description:
You lay your hand upon stone, concrete, or reinforced architecture and attune yourself to the slow awareness of what has been built. For the duration, you share a muted communion with the structure, sensing movement within it up to 60 feet from your location.

Through fractured impressions and weighty memories, the structure answers simple inquiries. You may learn what last moved within its bounds, what forces have recently scarred it, or whether living beings currently shelter inside. Its responses are never words, but sensations of pressure, absence, and strain.

Once during the duration, you may impose your will upon a 10-foot portion of the structure, either coaxing it to mend itself, restoring up to 30 hit points, or commanding it to weaken and fracture, dealing 6d6 force damage as cracks race through its body.

In cities and ruins alike, the built world listens, remembers, and endures, and for a brief time, it answers only to you.
Modifier Details: restores up to 30 hit points
Component Description (Material): a cracked stone with a spiral drawn in chalk

Description:
You tear open a forbidden wellspring of corruptive power, flooding the land with ruinous emanations that cling to flesh and stone alike.

All creatures in the affected area are scorched by the unleashed radiance, taking 8d10 radiant corruption damage (Constitution save for half).

Creatures that fail the saving throw become Tainted for 1 minute.

While Tainted:
	•	The creature exudes a sickly, unnatural glow.
	•	Any creature that ends its turn within 10 feet of a Tainted creature takes 1d4 radiant corruption damage, stacking with repeated exposure.
	•	A Tainted creature cannot benefit from invisibility, concealment, or stealth of any kind.
	•	Each Tainted creature has a 10% chance to manifest a unique anomaly or corruption, determined by the GM. These manifestations may be physical, spiritual, or metaphysical in nature.

The corruption lingers in the air, crawling from body to body like a curse given form, and even those who survive the blast may never fully escape its mark.
Modifier Details: takes 8d10 radiant corruption damage (Constitution save for half)

Description: You trigger a violent release of raw, unshaped power at a point within range. The detonation warps gravity and space in a 20-foot-radius sphere. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 12d10 impact damage and is hurled up to 30 feet away. On a success, it takes half damage and is not pushed. For 1 minute after the blast, the area bears visible scars of distortion, with colors bending and weight behaving unpredictably.
Modifier Details: each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw
Component Description (Material): an unstable anomaly, a crushed blueprint soaked in rainwater
Acquisition Note: Due to its instability, use of this spell without a permit is classified as an extreme containment risk.

Description:
You snap your fingers through folded chronology, sending a burning ripple backward into a wound that never had time to close.
Choose one creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 pyric damage (a combination of radiant and fire), and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or suffer Disadvantage on their next Concentration check before the end of your next turn.
Flavor:
- 5th level – 2d6 damage
- 11th level – 3d6 damage
- 17th level – 4d6 damage
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Constitution saving throw

Description: A barely visible eye- like shape appears on a ceiling, wall, or corner of a room. The illusion remains for the duration but does not actually perceive anything. Creatures unfamiliar with the spell must make a Wisdom (Perception) check to determine that it is not real. If they fail, they instinctively avoid looking at it or standing beneath it.
Modifier Details: creatures unfamiliar with the spell must make a Wisdom (Perception) check

Description:
With a single chant, a surge of prideful energy wraps around your chosen target. This incantation shields them with an aura of indomitable will, reducing the severity of blows that might otherwise pierce their resolve.

Choose one creature within range. The target gains resistance to critical hits for the duration of the spell—any critical hits against them are instead treated as normal hits.
Flavor:
The phantom ship *Elder’s Lament* was last seen adrift in the Void Sea, its captain having invoked Cerulean Incantation of Pride in a desperate bid to escape the Hunt. The ship is still seen, sometimes, but never in the same place twice.

The very air around Cerulean Incantation of Pride seems to recoil, as if reality itself wishes to avoid acknowledging its existence. It is a spell that should not be, yet is.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: reduces the severity of blows that might otherwise pierce their resolve, treating critical hits as n

Description:
Your senses sharpen with uncanny focus as your tongue tingles and your stomach hums like a divining rod. For a brief moment, you are aware of all edible food items within 30 feet—whether fresh, packed, rotten, conjured, or stolen.

You instantly know the location, type, and state of freshness or edibility of every meal, ration, snack, feast, or bite within range—even if it is hidden, obscured, or magically disguised.
Scaling:
At Higher Levels

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you also learn:- Whether any food is poisoned, cursed, or tampered with- Whether any food is enchanted, illusory, or anomalous- Whether a meal was prepared with love, bitterness, or obligation (at DM’s discretion)
Flavor: Early A.S.T.R.A.L. field manuals refer to this spell as Gastro- Thaumic Detection Protocol 4B. However, agents mostly call it The Lunch Sniff. Rumors persist of a rogue variant that once identified a sandwich as “sentient but indifferent.”

Description:
You shatter the containment of pyric forces, unleashing a cataclasmic surge that liquefies matter and collapses lesser workings into silence.

The blast hammers bodies and structures alike, its radiance so intense that enchantments falter and unravel beneath it. Survivors stagger beneath the weight of the exposure, their strength leeched by lingering sickness, while the terrain itself bears the mark of having been briefly judged unfit to endure.
Modifier Details: enchantments falter and unravel beneath it

Description:
You inscribe a sigil of absolute temporal authority, binding a creature, object, or structure within a knot of frozen moments.

Choose one creature, object, or structure within range. It is sealed in perfect stasis for the duration.
	•	While sealed, the target does not age, move, decay, or change in any way.
	•	It is immune to all damage and conditions.
	•	Attempts to touch, move, or affect it pass through harmlessly, as though interacting with an illusion, though the target remains visibly present.
	•	Time cannot be perceived from within the seal, nor can the sealed entity act or react in any way.

When the spell ends, the target resumes existence exactly as it was at the moment of sealing, with no awareness that time has passed.

Freezing a sentient creature strains the fabric of fate itself. Those who wield this magic too freely risk backlash from the deeper currents of time, as moments resist being denied their flow.
Component Description (Material): a corporate time- freeze contract, sealed within a wristwatch stuck at midnight
Acquisition Note: Corporate contracts must be notarized and signed before casting.
Arcane Strain: Freezing a sentient entity in time induces Moderate Arcane Strain.

Description: A faint shimmer of embers rises around you, warding off the cold. Until the start of your next turn, you ignore the first 5 cold damage you would take. If you are in an area of magical darkness, the veil glows faintly, revealing your silhouette to creatures within 5 feet.
Modifier Details: ignores the first 5 cold damage

Description:
You encrypt a message into a magical cipher, ensuring that only the intended recipient can read or hear it. Any unauthorized entity attempting to intercept the message sees random gibberish or hears static interference.
 
The recipient receives the message via a paranormal medium (a flickering holoscreen, whispered transmission, or ghostly text). If the message is not received within 7 days, it self- destructs.
Component Description (Material): a sealed letter or digital passkey

Description:
You carefully re-align the tangled strands of a willing creature’s mind, smoothing distortions where memory, fear, or cognition have been bent out of true. Fractured thoughts knit together, intrusive echoes fall silent, and clarity returns like breath after suffocation.

This spell restores lost memories, clears mental fog, and stabilizes neurological corruption caused by psychic overload, anomaly exposure, or cognitive decay. It removes the following conditions if they originate from such disturbances:
	•	Paranoid
	•	Frightened
	•	Stunned
	•	Compelled
	•	Aroused, if the effect was imposed through enchantment rather than emotion or will

The spell does not dispel active enchantments or magical charm effects. Instead, it halts the spread of long-term mental deterioration, such as mnemonic rot, forbidden sigil exposure, or Loom-scarring caused by containment breaches.

The mind is not rewritten. It is reminded of how it once held itself together, and given the strength to do so again.
Component Description (Material): a data storage crystal or memory chip

Description:
You draw the hues and textures of worked stone and crowded streets across your form.

Your body subtly adopts the tones of masonry, shadowed alleys, and weathered surfaces.
	•	You gain advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks in settlements, ruins, or constructed environments.
	•	You may pass through crowds and busy spaces without drawing notice, so long as you do not act violently or create sudden noise.

To the casual eye, you are merely another shape among many, a presence lost in the geometry of civilization.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks

Description: Summons an enchanted chessboard where the pieces move themselves. Unfortunately, the game always ends in a stalemate.

You overwhelm a creature with powerful urges of lust and romance. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or become compelled by you for the duration, deeply distracted by intimate thoughts and feelings.

Roll 1d6 immediately upon casting to determine a specific additional effect:

Roleplay Potential:
- This spell can cause considerable confusion and social chaos in delicate or diplomatic settings.
- Cultures sensitive to romantic propriety might ban or heavily regulate its use.
Table:
| d6 | Random Effect (Love or Lust?) |
|---- |--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | The target falls hopelessly in love with the nearest inanimate object, becoming protective and affectionate toward it. |
| 2 | The target becomes incredibly awkward for 1 hour, imposing disadvantage on all Charisma checks and saves. |
| 3 | The target is overcome by romantic impulse and spontaneously proposes marriage to you. |
| 4 | The target gains a +2 bonus to AC due to heightened adrenaline but is unable to attack any creature they find attractive. |
| 5 | The target takes 1d4 psychic damage from embarrassment as they recall every humiliating moment of their romantic past. |
| 6 | A whimsical Cupid- like being appears, openly judging and criticizing the target’s romantic choices for 1 minute. |
Flavor: An incomplete footnote in a partially corrupted A.S.T.R.A.L. record vaguely describes a “linguistic corruption phenomenon” causing test subjects to irreversibly blur intimacy, enchantment, and compulsion. The rest of the archive is suspiciously absent.
Component Description (Material): a rose petal dipped in honey

Description:
You cast a luminous veil over a creature, causing their appearance to elevate dramatically in status and confidence. Their clothes become elegant or fashion- forward (appropriate to the culture), their grooming perfect, and their posture striking. Even their aura suggests wealth, success, or nobility.

For the duration:- The target is perceived as wealthier, more influential, or better connected than they actually are.- They gain advantage on Charisma (Persuasion, Performance, or Deception) checks made to impress, manipulate, or gain favor from upper- class individuals.- Creatures of lower social standing may act deferential or assume the target outranks them.
Scaling: When cast with a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you may affect up to three additional creatures within range. Each creature may choose a different glamor (e.g., fashion mogul, noble, executive, avant- garde performer).
Flavor:
Originally misfiled as a peasant- upgrade joke, Disguise Glow- Up has since been used to bypass velvet ropes, crash board meetings, and seduce high- ranking officials into leaking sensitive documents.

One Concord fashion blogger noted: “It’s not fake. It’s curated.”
Modifier Details: gains advantage on Charisma (Persuasion, Performance, or Deception) checks

Description:
You enforce a miraculous ceasefire between all present minds. Creatures of your choice within range must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be overcome with a temporary wave of shared understanding and empathy.
- Affected creatures cannot attack, cast harmful spells, or willingly cause damage.
- They may still speak, act, or flee. If harmed by an external source, they may re- attempt the save at the end of their turn.
- Creatures under this spell see brief flashes of each other’s memories, chosen by the GM.
Flavor: For one impossible moment, everyone saw everyone else. And the fighting stopped.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Charisma saving throw

Description:
You summon a trivial but deeply irritating inconvenience to plague a creature or small area within range. No damage is dealt, and no serious harm is inflicted—just mild frustration.

Roll a d10 and consult the Chaos Table below to determine the exact annoyance:
Clarification: Clarifications:- The inconvenience is purely cosmetic, causing no damage or severe disadvantage.- The DM can creatively adjust minor details based on the narrative scenario.
Table:
| d10 | Minor Annoyance (Chaos Table) |
|----- |----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | A sock disappears from the target’s foot, never to be found again. |
| 2 | A sudden, unbearable itch appears exactly where the target can't reach. |
| 3 | The target’s shoelaces immediately untie themselves repeatedly for 1 minute. |
| 4 | A random book or document the target is holding becomes incorrectly labeled, causing confusion. |
| 5 | The target’s keys or similar small celebrityl item vanish momentarily, reappearing only after thorough searching. |
| 6 | An annoying dripping sound emanates nearby but has no identifiable source. |
| 7 | The target’s clothes become slightly uncomfortable—too itchy, tight, or loose—for exactly one minute. |
| 8 | A persistent and unexplained humming noise surrounds the target, audible only to them. |
| 9 | Every surface the target touches for the next minute feels oddly sticky. |
| 10 | A harmless but persistent fly or insect buzzes around the target’s face, impossible to swat or catch. |

Description:
You whisper the Old Words into the void, and something answers.
 
- A succubus or incubus materializes in your presence, bound to fulfill the caster’s desires, but at an unknown cost.
- Each interaction with the entity requires a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the caster’s body weakens (- 1 Constitution permanently).
- If the entity interacts with another person, they too must make the saving throw.
- The succubus/incubus remains until dismissed but will never leave empty- handed.
Flavor: Ancient texts warn that too many summons attract the attention of something greater.
Modifier Details: on failure, the caster's body weakens (-1 Constitution permanently)

Description:
You bind the target beneath a covenant that delays Morena’s claim. For the next 24 hours, if the creature dies, death is momentarily stayed and the Loom forcibly reasserts their thread. The creature is immediately restored to life with half its maximum hit points.

The return is not without cost. The Loom records the intervention as a debt of consequence, owed to the unseen power that enforced the deferral. How and when that obligation is collected is left to fate, ambition, or the Crown’s notice.

Foundry Tag: Requires Sight, Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Teleportation, Plane Shifting
Unnamed: 5: Requires Sight, Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Teleportation, Plane Shifting
Description:
You conjure binding sigils of obligation that lash out toward a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 3d6 impact damage and become bound in place.

While bound, the creature’s speed becomes 0, and it cannot teleport, shapeshift, or plane shift. Each turn, the creature must spend its action struggling against the covenant, making a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC. On a success, the sigils fracture and the creature breaks free.
Scaling: At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the initial damage increases by 1d6 per slot level above 2nd.
Flavor: Subjects report the sensation of being buried in paperwork—stamped by entities they cannot name. Many escape only after metaphorically signing something they don’t remember
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw

Description: You weave a subtle illusion into the mind of a creature within range, causing them to feel an unnatural crawling sensation on their skin. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, the creature spends its next bonus action, if it has one, brushing at unseen shapes or shifting uncomfortably. This effect ends early if they take damage.
Modifier Details: must make an Intelligence saving throw

Description:
You channel the latent narrative forces of the multiverse to manifest an entirely new occult practice, ritual, or thaumaturgic procedure. The spell you create becomes part of your personal occult catalog, possibly inscribed into anomalous archives, grimoires, or SCP containment protocols. The form, effects, and limits of the new spell are theoretically unbounded, but the DM is the final arbiter of legality, scope, and drawbacks.

Guidelines for Created Spell Level:
- Cantrips & 1st–3rd: Minor effects, situational utility.
- 4th–6th: Major combat or utility with risks.
- 7th–8th: High-tier control, transformation, or resurrection-tier with severe costs.
- 9th: Reality-warping effects akin to Wish.

Drawbacks: Memory loss, exhaustion, psychic damage, anomaly contamination, permanent ability loss, or SCP-style containment breaches.
Limitations: Only 1 created spell can be stable in your catalog at a time; others require containment. Backlash possible on failure.
Component Description (Material): A sentient quill or anomalous recorder worth at least 5,000 gp, consumed

Description:
You proclaim a sweeping decree that asserts royal authority over an entire district. All creatures within range must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, they are compelled to remain in place, believing compliance to be their lawful duty. Affected creatures cannot initiate hostilities unless attacked and automatically suppress panic and fear responses in favor of obedience.

Throughout the area, structures of order fall silent as conduits of control shut down or enter enforced dormancy, responding only to authority recognized by the Crown. When invoked at its greatest height, the decree persists until undone by equally potent magic, binding all who failed their save to the stillness of sanctioned order.
Scaling: - If cast at 10th level, the effect lasts until dispelled, and creatures who fail their save cannot willingly leave the area.
Modifier Details: must make a Charisma saving throw

Description:
You call upon a sovereign reckoning that does not punish, but clarifies standing. Up to six creatures of your choice are momentarily measured beneath the unseen scales of the Crown and found worthy.

For the duration, each affected creature gains advantage on saving throws against fear, charm, and coercion. Once per round, when a creature misses an attack or fails an ability check, it may add 1d10 to the result, as resolve hardens under scrutiny.

If an affected creature would be reduced to 0 hit points, it instead drops to 1 hit point and cannot die until the start of its next turn, held upright by recognition rather than force. The weighing does not promise victory, only that endurance will not go unnoticed.
Flavor: Your worth is not in your success. It is in your endurance.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on saving throws against fear, charm, and coercion
Component Description (Material): a balance scale and a sliver of gold

Description:
You invoke Tiqyzeus, god of artisans and perfected form, and consecrate a workspace to his exacting attention. Flame is rendered unnecessary as disciplined, purposeful heat settles into the forge, workshop, or crafting station, guided not by combustion but by intent and design.

While the crucible endures, Tiqyzeus’ influence reshapes matter through ordered transformation:
	•	Metals yield to the hand, bending, fusing, or refining without tools. If the intended form violates the material’s true nature or balance, the metal forcefully rejects the change, snapping back or releasing a violent backlash at the Weaver’s discretion.
	•	Composite and synthetic materials merge into deliberate hybrids, forming structures that feel intentional rather than accidental, as if following a hidden blueprint.
	•	Bone, ivory, and ceramic drawn into the crucible align with nearby metal, assembling into coherent artisan constructs reminiscent of sacred tools or reliquaries.
	•	Items crafted under Tiqyzeus’ gaze remain warm for 72 hours and emit a faint, steady resonance, the echo of a design affirmed.

While working within the crucible, smiths and crafters gain advantage on crafting checks involving metal, bone, or composite materials. Items forged here may bear subtle marks of divine craftsmanship at the Weaver’s discretion, such as unnatural balance, resistance to mundane corrosion, or a quiet hum that responds to skilled hands.

Overuse invites permanence. The workspace itself may begin to assume Tiqyzeus’ ideals, tools curving toward perfect grip, surfaces aligning into functional symmetry, and mechanisms resisting crude or careless use. Such sanctification can be slowed, but never fully undone.
Anomaly Effect: Overuse may alter the workstation itself. Vents weep mercury, engines resist standard inputs, and tools develop unexpected curvatures. A successful DC 17 Arcana or Engineering check halts the corruption—for now.
Modifier Details: smiths and crafters gain advantage on crafting checks involving metal, bone, or composite materials
Range: Touch (1 forge, anvil, or crafeeting station)
Component Description (Material): a coal that has never burned

Description: You disrupt sound waves in a localized area. Until the start of your next turn, all spoken words within range are slightly distorted, causing messages to come out garbled and difficult to understand. If cast near a radio or intercom, the device crackles with static for one round.
Modifier Details: all spoken words within range are slightly distorted, causing messages to come out garbled and diffi

Description:
You impose a compulsion of misplaced authority upon a humanoid within range. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they become convinced that the matter before them lies beyond their standing and must be addressed by a superior power.

While affected, the creature gains the Paranoid condition and refuses to engage with the current conflict or task, insisting it falls outside their jurisdiction. In combat, they take the Dodge action unless directly attacked, spending their turns muttering about violations of protocol, improper authority, or the need for escalation.

The creature may repeat the saving throw only at the end of each hour, or immediately if confronted by an entity they genuinely recognize as holding higher authority. On a successful initial save, the target resists the compulsion but suffers disadvantage on their next ability check or attack roll as doubt briefly undermines their resolve.
Flavor: I’m afraid I’m not authorized to deal with that. You’ll need to speak with management.
Modifier Details: must make a Wisdom saving throw

Description: You designate a target and whisper a temporal lullaby encoded with latent enchantment. The spell does not take effect immediately—instead, you set a delay of 1, 2, or 3 rounds (your choice when casting). When the timer ends, the effect detonates.- The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or fall unconscious for the duration.- While unconscious, they can be awakened by taking damage or being shaken awake.- If the target is already asleep when the spell activates, they instead experience vivid hallucinations and speak in an unknown language. For the next hour, they have disadvantage on Insight checks and occasionally murmur anomalous phrases.- On a successful save, the target resists the effect and instead gains one level of fatigue for 1 hour, as their internal clock momentarily collapses.
Scaling: When cast using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you may affect one additional creature per level beyond 3rd. All targets must be within 30 feet of one another.
Flavor: Survivors of the spell’s effects often complain of waking up in a body that feels slightly… borrowed.
Modifier Details: must make a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You call upon the fractured laws of probability, summoning a swirling rain of polyhedral objects—tiny magical dice—within a 10- foot radius centered on you. The clattering projectiles slam into everything nearby.

Each creature of your choice in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 2d6 impact damage. On a successful save, they take half damage. The damage is nonlethal unless otherwise specified.

Chaos Surge:

Immediately after casting, roll a d20. On a natural 20, the dice manifest as larger and denser projectiles (e.g., d20s the size of coconuts). Choose one of the following critical effects, or let the DM improvise:- The target is knocked prone.- One object in the area is crushed or broken.- A random magical effect is triggered (see Wild Magic surge table).- The area becomes difficult terrain until the end of your next turn (covered in slippery dice).- You regain 1 hit point (the dice land just right to boost your morale).
Scaling:
The spell’s damage increases as you gain levels:
- 5th level: 3d6
- 11th level: 4d6
- 17th level: 5d6

At 11th level and above, the storm includes d12s and d20s. Damage remains nonlethal, but now causes loud, disorienting clatter (creatures have disadvantage on Stealth checks until the end of their next turn).
Flavor: Originally cataloged under “d1.ce” by A.S.T.R.A.L., this anomaly manifested during a failed probability containment test. The storm is still considered a Class- 1 hazard in casinos, board rooms, and office game nights.
Modifier Details: each creature of your choice in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw

Description: You focus your senses, allowing the pulse of magic to resonate through the terrain around you. This pulse reveals the optimal path forward, guiding your movements with precision. Whether in dense forests, city streets, or endless hallways, the flow of magic sharpens your awareness of the shortest and safest route.- Effect:- For the duration of the spell, you gain the ability to automatically find the shortest path to your desired destination within range. This can be a physical route, avoiding obstacles like walls or difficult terrain, or identifying pathways hidden or obscured by illusion magic.- You do not become disoriented or confused by labyrinths, mazes, or other disorienting environments.
Scaling:
- 5th Level: You can now detect multiple routes (such as secondary exits or alternate pathways) and choose the safest or most efficient.
- 11th Level: The range of the spell increases to 120 feet, and you gain the ability to perceive hidden or magical paths, such as secret doors or enchanted routes.
Flavor: The flow of energy pulses through the stone beneath your feet, and your senses expand beyond your immediate surroundings. As the pulse of magic spreads outward, the shifting walls of a labyrinth reveal their true layout, and you are guided with an unnatural certainty toward your destination.
Modifier Details: gains the ability to automatically find the shortest path

Description: You release a low, resonant tone that only the dying can hear. Any creature within range that is at 0 hit points and making death saving throws automatically stabilizes if it succeeds on its next death saving throw. If an undead creature is within range, it takes 1 cognitive damage as the resonance disrupts its form.
Modifier Details: automatically stabilizes on next death saving throw

Description: You attune your senses to latent traces left behind by recently departed creatures. For the duration, you can perceive invisible bio- trails, residual aura smudges, or paranormal displacement fields that linger behind living beings.- Within a 60- foot radius, you can detect the most recent trace left by any creature that passed through in the last hour.- The trace manifests as a faint shimmer, footprint, or scent only you can perceive.- On close inspection (action), you can learn one of the following:- Species or general creature type (humanoid, beast, aberration, etc.)- Time since presence- Anomalous conditions, such as illness, magical residue, or emotional trauma
Scaling: When cast using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can:- Analyze up to 3 traces simultaneously, or- Gain additional information such as diet, injury patterns, or arcane corruption.
Flavor: A censored note in Zachary Rogers’ Black Site logs reads: “We used to follow footprints. Now we follow echoes. But the echoes are starting to echo back.”
Trigger: when an object is thrown at you

Description: You unleash a storm of warped aetheric vibrations and shrieking resonance that overwhelms thought and balance. The surge erupts in a 10-foot-radius sphere centered on a point within range, battering minds and senses alike. Each creature in the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 5d8 sound damage and is stunned until the start of its next turn. On a success, it takes half damage and suffers no stun. The air itself seems to hum with lingering discord as the surge fades.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw

Description:
You implore the dark corners of reality to place an unseen burden within a creature’s body—not a pregnancy, but an unnatural gestation, a thing that grows, waits, and feeds.
 
- The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, something begins to gestate inside them, though it is not a living creature as they understand it.
- Each week, roll a d6 to determine the nature of the thing growing within:
- 1 | Bone Nesting – The target’s bones begin splitting, reforming, and growing redundantly, fusing in painful, alien configurations.
- 2 | Chitinous Hatchery – Their flesh hardens into a shell, and something moves beneath it. It will hatch, but no one knows what.
- 3 | Hollow Incubation – The target’s organs begin to disappear, one by one, yet they remain alive, feeling the empty space within.
- 4 | Blood Evolution – Their blood is no longer theirs, but something else’s, moving independently, making them a host for an unseen parasite.
- 5 | Neural Infestation – The target begins hearing a second set of thoughts inside their mind. These are not theirs. They whisper secrets.
- 6 | Aberrant Bloom – Strange tendrils or appendages occasionally emerge from the target’s body, retreating before they can be seen.
 
 Ending the Spell:
- If left untreated, at the end of three months, the thing inside them will be born. The result is not predictable, and it may not be survivable.
- The only way to remove the gestation is through surgery or another, more powerful anomaly.
Anomaly Effect: - If multiple creatures are affected by this spell in the same location, their gestating entities begin to sync, forming a single, distributed intelligence between all hosts.
Modifier Details: target must make a Constitution saving throw
Component Description (Material): a handful of grave dirt, pressed into the skin

Description: You extract fragments of knowledge from the target’s mind. They must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, their spell save DC and Intelligence- based ability checks are reduced by 1d4 for the duration.
Modifier Details: reduces spell save DC by 1d4 for the duration

Description:
You release a thin pulse of sound, inaudible to the living, but receptive to echoes of the past.
 
For the duration, you can hear vague, distorted murmurs of events that occurred in the area within the past hour. These sounds are indistinct and fragmented but may provide clues about recent conversations, movement, or disturbances.

The clarity of the echo depends on an Intelligence (Investigation) check made against your spell save DC:
Modifier Details: Intelligence (Investigation) check made against your spell save DC

Description: Time fractures briefly around a small object of your choice within range. A flickering afterimage of the object appears where it was exactly one second ago, causing momentary confusion. A creature interacting with the object must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or be unable to distinguish which version is real until the start of your next turn.
Modifier Details: must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw

Description:
This spell channels a forbidden soporific charm through intimate proximity, forcing the target into an immediate and unnatural slumber upon physical exchange, such as a kiss or sustained touch. The magic seeps directly into the target’s vital rhythms, overwhelming conscious resistance before the mind can react.

The target remains unconscious for the duration unless physically harmed or deliberately awakened by an external force. Ordinary sounds, movement, or environmental noise do not disturb this sleep, as the enchantment anchors itself to the body rather than the senses.

A reversed application of this spell, known as Biometric Reboot, forcibly restores wakefulness to a creature suffering from magical sleep. When used in this manner, the effect is instantaneous and ignores the remaining duration of the original enchantment.

Drowsing Embrace is commonly employed in discreet medical rites, pleasure cults, and covert interrogations, particularly in circles where subtlety is valued over restraint. Its use is tightly controlled in most civilized regions, though it thrives in black markets and clandestine traditions.
Component Description (Somatic):
The caster draws the target close and places two fingers lightly at the base of the jaw or along the throat, where pulse and breath converge. As contact is made, the caster exhales slowly and traces a subtle sigil with the thumb, a motion so slight it can be mistaken for an affectionate caress. The gesture must be performed with deliberate calm and uninterrupted focus, allowing the enchantment to flow through touch rather than force.

The magic relies on intimacy and timing rather than precision or complexity. Any abrupt movement, visible tension, or break in contact disrupts the spell, causing the energy to dissipate harmlessly. Observers rarely recognize the motion as spellcasting, as it mirrors common gestures of comfort, trust, or desire.

Description: You infuse a creature with a protective ember shield. The first time they take damage within the duration, flames lash out at the attacker, dealing 2d6 pyric damage. Additionally, the target has resistance to pyric damage for the duration.
Scaling: At 4th level, the retaliatory pyric damage increases to 3d6.
Flavor:
The last known user of Duinir’s Ember Aegis, the warlock Darsil Veil, was found with his eyes hollowed out and his veins scorched black. His journal spoke of 'the whispering ones' who demanded a price for forbidden knowledge.

There are words that should not be spoken, thoughts that should not be held. Duinir’s Ember Aegis is one such secret—a spell that fractures reality just enough for something else to listen.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: deals 2d6 pyric damage
Component Description (Material): A coal from a long- dead hearth

Foundry Tag: Grants Temporary Hit Points, Creates Light
Unnamed: 5: Grants Temporary Hit Points, Creates Light
Description:
A flicker of protective warmth, traced from forgotten runes and smoldering remnants of a scholar’s last memory, pulses outward from your hands. You call upon Duinir’s ward—a memory, a flame, a sanctuary encoded in contradiction.

Choose up to 3 creatures within range. Each target gains 2d4 temporary hit points and becomes immune to the charmed condition for the duration.

While under this spell’s effects, affected creatures emit a faint, heatless ember- glow from their chest when they are emotionally manipulated or magically influenced—visible only in dim light or darkness.
Flavor:
Before the disaster at Hollowwatch, scribes documented the sudden, inexplicable disappearance of a scholar who had been researching Duinir's Respite of Flames. The books on his desk were found neatly stacked, except for one—a tome that had no title, and no pages inside.

Ancient tablets unearthed beneath the ruins of Gilded Hollow show early inscriptions of Duinir's Respite of Flames, yet none can determine the language it was written in. The characters shift when no one is looking.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher:
Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen. The air smells faintly of burnt parchment and oranges.
Modifier Details: gains 2d4 temporary hit points

Description: A sudden gust of dust rises from the ground, swirling around you in a blinding veil. The particles obscure your form, making it harder for enemies to land a blow.- Effect:- When you are attacked, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll against you for the duration of the round.- The dust is drawn from the terrain, and the effect is strongest in areas where the environment is gritty or decaying (such as crumbling concrete).
Scaling:
- 5th Level: The dust cloud lasts for 2 rounds instead of 1.
- 11th Level: The radius of the cloud increases to cover a 10- foot area around you, granting disadvantage on all attack rolls against creatures within the area.
Flavor: As you step, the ground beneath you cracks and a cloud of dust erupts into the air, swirling around your form in a cloud of obscurity. The air feels thick with sand as your enemies struggle to focus through the haze, their vision distorted by the fine particles swirling in every direction.
Modifier Details: imposes disadvantage on attack rolls against you

Description:
You speak a cursed word in a language not meant for mortals, invoking the forbidden knowledge of Dyrnar’s Doom. Choose one creature within range. The target becomes marked for death. Lines of black sigils appear on the target’s hands and neck, and the target learns that they will die in 4d8 + 12 hours unless certain conditions are met. They know how much time they have and how to avoid the doom:
 1. Curse Removal: The target can make a successful Wisdom saving throw at the end of each day to break the curse.
 2. Kill a Humanoid: The target must kill a humanoid within the time frame to delay the curse. The humanoid must be a creature capable of being killed by the target.

If the conditions are not met, the target dies when the time expires.

The sigils continue to spread as the time runs out, covering the target’s body in dark ink. If the conditions are met or the curse is removed, the sigils vanish, and the target is freed from the impending doom.

Saving Throw: Wisdom save to resist being marked for death.
Flavor:
The last known user of Dyrnar's Doom of Despair, the warlock Darsil Veil, was found with his eyes hollowed out and his veins scorched black. His journal spoke of 'the whispering ones' who demanded a price for forbidden knowledge.

There are words that should not be spoken, thoughts that should not be held. Dyrnar's Doom of Despair is one such secret—a spell that fractures reality just enough for something else to listen.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a roll of 15 or higher, reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen. This subtle shift in the balance may leave its marks on the world, though it is not always clear what effect it will have.
Modifier Details: Wisdom save to resist being marked for death

Description:
You exhale a shriek that does not come from your lungs. It comes from somewhere much deeper—a place beneath time and flesh. The wail drags the psychic residue of forgotten horrors into the minds of those who hear it.

Each creature in a 30- foot cone must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is frightened for 1 minute. If the creature fails by 5 or more, it also suffers 5d6 cognitive damage as ancestral screams claw through its mind.

A frightened creature may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
Scaling: At Higher Levels. When cast using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the range increases to a 60- foot cone.
Flavor:
In the city of Concord, Dyrnar’s Tormentous Wail is outlawed, for it was once used by the cult of the Silent Maw to unmake a senator in broad daylight. Witnesses swore they saw his shadow scream before vanishing entirely.

Those who cast the wail often find that they dream differently afterward. They dream of corridors that stretch into infinity, doors that lead to places older than time.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher:
Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: on a failed save, suffers 5d6 cognitive damage
Component Description (Material): A desiccated tongue wrapped in silk

Description: You summon an anomaly- born specter that fights for you. It has AC 16, HP 60, flight, and resistance to nonmagical damage. It attacks with cognitive tendrils (2d10 damage) and can phase through solid surfaces. It obeys only direct commands and disappears if ignored.
Modifier Details: has AC 16
Component Description (Material): a memory fragment from an erased soul

Description:
While this spell is active, your aura reflects the memory of sanctified or desecrated ground. For the duration:- Your Aura of Sanctity applies even if the terrain is not currently Holy or Unholy.- You gain a faint glowing aura (or a flickering shadow) that reveals your allegiance.- If you cast Consecrate or Corrupt during the duration, the effect becomes instantaneous and cannot be interrupted by damage.

Note: This spell allows you to “prime” a battlefield without needing the terrain benefit immediately, making it more tactical.

You briefly attune yourself to lingering impressions of the past. You hear a single whispered sentence in a language you understand, spoken by a creature that was in the area within the past 24 hours. The sentence is random, chosen by the DM, and may or may not be useful. If no creature was in the area within that time, you instead hear a distant, meaningless murmur.
Table:
| DC Range | Effect Description |
|---|---|
| 10 or lower | Incoherent murmurs, difficult to interpret. |
| 11–15 | Disjointed words or partial sentences. |
| 16+ | A full phrase or clear statement relevant to the past event. |

Description: By attuning to the residual vibrations in the air, you briefly hear a snippet of a conversation that took place in the area within the last 10 minutes. The words are often out of context, distorted, or incomplete, requiring interpretation.

You fracture the veil of memory and call forth the voices of the long- dead—those whose names were erased, whose deaths were forgotten. In a 20- foot- radius sphere centered on a point within range, spectral murmurs begin to rise, growing louder until they echo with unbearable presence.

Each creature in the area must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they are frightened and deafened for the duration. While frightened in this way, they perceive haunting whispers describing their worst regrets, lost memories, or alternate lives that could have been.

A creature that ends its turn in the area must repeat the saving throw.
Scaling: At Higher Levels. When cast with a spell slot of 7th level or higher, affected creatures also take 3d6 cognitive damage at the start of each turn they remain in the area.
Flavor:
The last known user of Echoes of the Forgotten, the warlock Darsil Veil, was found with his eyes hollowed out and his veins scorched black. His journal spoke of 'the whispering ones' who demanded a price for forbidden knowledge.

There are words that should not be spoken, thoughts that should not be held. Echoes of the Forgotten is one such secret—a spell that fractures reality just enough for something else to listen.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher:
Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: each creature in the area must make a Wisdom saving throw
Component Description (Material): A whisper trapped in a glass vial

Description: You fracture the timeline around you, surrounding yourself in rippling echoes of potential futures and discarded pasts. While the spell is active, time fractures and repairs itself in chaotic pulses.
Table:
| d6 Roll | Temporal Effect |
|--------- |------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 1–2 | Slowed Flow – Enemies within range have disadvantage on attack rolls against you until the start of your next turn. |
| 3–4 | Accelerated Momentum – You may take a second bonus action this turn. |
| 5 | Echo Step – You teleport up to 15 feet to a location you occupied on your last turn. |
| 6 | Temporal Mirror – A flickering afterimage of yourself performs the same action you just did (repeating your action once, targeting a different creature if possible; no additional cost). |
Scaling:
At Higher Levels.
When cast using a 9th-level spell slot, you may roll twice each turn and choose the result.
Flavor:
The rogue artificer Veyl Renshaw designed an enchanted device that could replicate Echoing Pulse of Time. She activated it only once—her house was later found standing, but all its doors opened into an endless void.

To cast Echoing Pulse of Time is to invite a presence that does not belong. The words taste of static, the gestures feel like echoes of hands not your own.
Anomaly Effect:
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Component Description (Material): A watch with a cracked face

Description:
You stomp your foot with great force, sending a shockwave reverberating through the wooden floor beneath you. The sound echoes with a force that temporarily disrupts your enemies.
- Effect:
- All creatures within a 10- foot radius must make a Constitution saving throw.
- On a failed save, creatures lose their reaction until the start of their next turn, stunned by the reverberation of the stomp.
- Creatures that fail the saving throw also take 1d6 sonic damage as the shockwave disrupts their senses.
Scaling:
- 5th Level: The radius of the shockwave increases to 15 feet.
- 11th Level: The damage increases to 2d6 sonic damage.
Flavor: You stamp your foot firmly, and the floor trembles as the shockwave ripples outward, vibrating through the wood like the crack of thunder. The deafening echo unsettles your foes, leaving them dazed and vulnerable, as the ground beneath them seems to tremble with your power.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw

Description: You trace an arcane sigil over a creature’s skin, leaving behind a barely perceptible shimmer. The next time the target takes chiaric damage before the start of your next turn, the damage is reduced by 1d4.
Modifier Details: reduces damage by 1d4

Description: You channel the Imperatum’s highest mandate, imposing unquestionable authority drawn from the Throne of Ambition. Creatures must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be rendered non-actors, unable to initiate hostile actions. All magic, supernatural effects, and anomalies are suppressed beneath imperial decree. Summoned beings and external manifestations are expelled as unlawful presences.
Modifier Details: creatures must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description: When a creature within 60 feet attempts to cast a spell, you pronounce an Imperatum edict that arrests its ascent. The spell is bound mid-utterance. Roll 1d4. After that many rounds, the restrained spell collapses, expending its original resources without effect. The caster is aware that their magic has been judged and stayed. The bound spell counts as ongoing for concentration. You may only enforce one imperial suspension at a time.
Modifier Details: rolls 1d4 to determine how many rounds the restrained spell collapses
Trigger: When a creature casts a spell

Description:
You invoke the authority of the Throne of Ambition to strike a name from the ledgers of dominion. The target is severed from all structures of recognition, becoming a sovereign null where authority once applied.

All systems of claim and record fail to acknowledge the target. Attempts to identify, bind, judge, or pursue them collapse as if no rightful subject exists. Deeds, transgressions, and obligations attached to the target lose standing, leaving no recognized trace of culpability or status.

Once the edict is enacted, the target cannot reclaim standing, title, or recognized identity without powerful magical intervention capable of reasserting dominion over their existence.
Component Description (Material): an official government form with the target’s name redacted, stamped with an erasure approval seal
Acquisition Note: Requires government approval and processing, costing 500 credits. Unauthorized attempts may trigger forensic restoration protocols or counter- investigations.
Arcane Strain: Due to its permanent nature, casting this spell inflicts Major Arcane Strain.

Description:
You impose a sovereign pause upon causality itself, compelling time to await permission before proceeding. Choose an action, spell, or attack within range. Its effects do not resolve immediately. Instead, the moment is seized and held in suspension, unfolding exactly one round later as though time itself had been restrained by decree.

Those who witness the delay feel a palpable resistance in the air, as if reality hesitates under an unseen mandate. The delayed effect occurs normally once the moment is released, with no alteration to its outcome, only its timing.
Component Description (Material): a stamped bureaucratic delay request, attached to a clock frozen at the moment of submission
Acquisition Note: Requires a corporate- issued authorization stamp. Unregistered delays may be flagged for administrative review.
Arcane Strain: Using this spell more than once within 24 hours inflicts Moderate Arcane Strain.

Description:
You saturate the target’s nervous system with supernatural excess, overloading their body with pleasure, euphoria, and dreamlike sensations.
 
- They gain the Aroused condition. 
- They suffer disadvantage on Intelligence and Wisdom saving throws. 
- Their movement speed is halved as they drift and stagger in a dreamlike trance. 
- They begin to hallucinate, seeing fleeting images of former lovers, unfulfilled desires, or paranormal entities disguised as temptations (chosen by the GM).

If the target fails the saving throw by 10 or more, they lose all grip on reality—treating illusions as truth and mistaking allies for figments or phantoms. They become Paranoid and they cannot willingly target these hallucinated allies with hostile actions unless they are harmed first.
Anomaly Effect: - If a creature fails by 10 or more, they become unable to tell reality from illusion, mistaking allies for dreamlike apparitions.
Modifier Details: suffers disadvantage on Intelligence saving throws
Acquisition Note: See Blood Seal rules for procurement.

Description:
You place an enchanting kiss upon a humanoid creature you touch, compelling it to regard you as an authoritative presence worthy of obedience. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw at disadvantage. On a failed save, the target becomes charmed, treating you as a trusted authority figure, willing to comply with most non- hostile requests and instructions.

While charmed, the target experiences a strong compulsion to remain within 10 feet of you. At the end of each of its turns, the target may repeat the Wisdom saving throw (without disadvantage); success allows it to move freely until the start of its next turn.

The charm persists indefinitely until it is dispelled, you become hostile toward the target, or the target takes damage.
Scaling: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you may affect one additional humanoid for each slot level above 1st. Each target must be kissed separately within the same action.
Flavor: Common in corporate negotiation tactics and underground consent override procedures.
Modifier Details: target must make a Wisdom saving throw at disadvantage

Description: You alter the way a surface registers footprints. For the duration, a touched floor, rug, or dirt path erases any traces of movement within a 5- foot radius. Creatures attempting to track someone through this area must make a Wisdom (Survival) check against your spell save DC.
Modifier Details: creatures attempting to track someone must make a Wisdom (Survival) check against your spell save DC

Description: Up to 6 creatures of your choice in range become immune to pain, fear, and exhaustion for 1 hour. This spell does not remove injuries but suppresses all negative physical sensations.

You disrupt the ordered patterns by which thought is structured, overwhelming the target’s mental lattice. The creature must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, its nervous system seizes as cognition collapses inward, dealing 8d8 damage and leaving it incapacitated until the end of its next turn. If the creature fails the saving throw by 5 or more, its sight is lost in cascading overload for 1 minute.

At greater invocations, the fracture propagates more violently, increasing the damage as deeper layers of ordered thought are torn apart.
Scaling: At Higher Levels. When cast using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 per level.
Modifier Details: must make an Intelligence saving throw

Description:
You inscribe a declaration, signature, or binding mark directly into the Loom itself, affixing it permanently to a nonliving object. The inscription cannot be erased, defaced, burned, or altered by mundane means, persisting as long as the object exists.

Only reality-altering magic, sovereign intervention, or severe disturbance of the Loom can remove or overwrite the edict. To all who perceive it, the mark carries an undeniable weight, as though the world itself insists the message has always been there.

You release a sudden eruption of Everbright radiance and concussive force at a point within range. Creatures caught in the burst must make a Constitution saving throw or be overwhelmed by searing light and thunderous impact, leaving them blinded and deafened until the end of your next turn.

The flare is brief but absolute, collapsing vision and sound into a single violent instant before fading, leaving those affected struggling to reorient themselves as the world rushes back in.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw

Description:
You exhale a surging cataract of Everbright radiance braided with bleeding shadow. The light is not warm nor pure, but judging, refracted through impossible angles as it tears across a wide cone before you.

Creatures caught within must withstand the brilliance or suffer searing chiaric harm, their sight overwhelmed as the world fractures into afterimages. Stone, steel, and air alike shimmer with residual distortion, bending and resisting passage as the light lingers for a breath after the surge has passed.
Modifier Details: creatures caught within must withstand the brilliance or suffer searing chiaric harm
Component Description (Material): a broken projector bulb smeared with soot

Description:
You force a target to become a stranger to themselves.
 
- The target must make a Charisma saving throw (DC 20) or lose all memories of who they are, what they believe, and what they have done.
- They retain skills and knowledge but have no celebrityl attachment to any of it.
- The effect can only be undone by tracing their past through physical records or using a Wish spell.
Modifier Details: must make a Charisma saving throw (DC 20)
Component Description (Material): a mirror that has never reflected the caster

Description: For a fleeting moment, a creature within range instinctively hesitates, doubting its own authority. If the target is attempting to enforce a rule, issue a command, or exert influence over another creature, it must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or momentarily second- guess itself, delaying its action until the start of its next turn.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Charisma saving throw

Description:
The caster places a lingering, whispering kiss upon the target, sapping their very essence.
 
- The target loses 2d4 Strength for 24 hours, their muscles wilting as if touched by decay.
- For 1d10 turns, the target is completely helpless, collapsing under an unseen weight, as if an entity watches from behind their eyes.
- If the target is already affected by a Necromancy spell, it takes extra 1d6 chiaric damage at the start of its next turn.
 
 Those struck by this curse claim to see their own reflection smile at them, long after the caster has left.
Modifier Details: extra 1d6 chiaric damage

Description: By momentarily altering a minor cognitive detail, you cause a creature to misremember something trivial. It might forget setting its keys down, misplace an item it was just holding, or recall an insignificant interaction that never happened. The effect is fleeting, lasting only a few seconds before the mind corrects itself.

You attune your senses to the fading echoes of life. You become aware of the heartbeat of any creature within range, even if they are hidden or invisible, as long as they are not undead or a construct. You do not learn their exact location, only their presence.

You press your fingers against the target’s abdomen and whisper an incantation that only hunger understands. The body responds, but not in the way it should. Something takes root within the target’s form, but it is not life—it is absence made flesh.
 
- The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure:
- They become host to an Unborn Hunger, a parasitic void that feeds upon them instead of growing.
- Each long rest, they lose 1d4 maximum hit points, their flesh pulling taut over their bones.
- Their appetite vanishes—no matter what they eat, their body refuses to digest it.
- They can feel it moving inside them, shifting with breath, curling beneath the ribs.
- If reduced to 0 HP while afflicted, they do not die. Instead, their body becomes an empty husk, and something else uses their form.
 
 Ending the Spell:
- If the host wishes to remove it, they must cut it out manually, taking 4d6 tearing damage and making a DC 15 Constitution check to remain conscious.
- A failed removal attempt may cause the entity to take full control, wearing the host like a suit of skin.
Anomaly Effect: - If multiple creatures are afflicted at once, they begin to crave each other, drawn together by an instinct to consume the unborn within one another.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw
Component Description (Material): a shard of cracked bone, buried in the target’s flesh

Description:
A subdermal neurological impulse is implanted in the target’s cognitive prioritization center, making them incapable of focusing on anything but the caster.

The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save:

- The affected target follows the caster wherever they go, unable to take their eyes off them.
- If attacked, the target enters a berserk fury, attacking anyone who obstructs their pursuit of the caster (+1 to hit,- 1 AC).
- The target may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
Modifier Details: +1 to hit
Component Description (Material): a biometric signature of the target

Description:
A touch laced with the essence of decay invades the target’s body, unseen and insidious. The rot festers quietly beneath the surface, waiting to unravel its host.- The target becomes infected with a random disease that is linked to the natural decay of the body. This affliction is dormant for three days, during which the target feels no ill effects.- After three days, the disease awakens. Symptoms manifest suddenly, accompanied by whispers of secrets and memories of decay that take hold of the target’s mind. Feverish visions and murmured words plague the afflicted, distorting their perception of reality.- A successful Medicine check reveals nothing unusual at first glance. The symptoms appear natural, as though the disease were always meant to be a part of the target’s life. No magical traces of the rot can be detected without specialized magic or knowledge.

Effect:
While the disease is dormant, the target remains unaware of the affliction. Upon the disease’s awakening, they suffer from both physical and psychological effects, with hallucinations and fever overtaking them. The whispers may speak of forgotten memories, lost opportunities, or cryptic warnings—each whisper an unsettling reflection of the decay inside them.

The disease is not immediately fatal but can lead to a slow decline in health, eventually debilitating the target unless it is cured. Cure Disease or similar magical healing can remove the affliction.
Modifier Details: reveals nothing unusual at first glance with a successful Medicine check

Description:
