You affix a sovereign seal that bars a creature’s thread from all forms of restoration. Choose one creature within range. For the duration, the creature is denied recognition by healing magic, restoration effects, and resurrection rites.

If the creature dies while the seal persists, no remains are left behind and its thread is rendered unrecoverable, severed too completely for retrieval by any means short of the most extreme reality-altering intervention. The seal does not destroy the creature. It ensures that return is no longer an option.
Flavor: Denied. Reason: Incorrect form submitted.
Component Description (Material): an expired authorization form and a drop of wax

Description:
You snap your hand into a confident finger gun and unleash a crackling projectile of arcane force shaped like a bullet. Choose one creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target.

- On a hit, the target takes 2d6 ballistic damage.
- The projectile leaves behind a faint trail of cigarette smoke and the smell of ozone.
- If cast while wearing sunglasses or leaning on something cool, you may deal an additional +1 damage (DM discretion, style points encouraged).
Scaling:
At Higher Levels. 
When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.
Flavor:
“Bang.” 
—Last words of Agent V before exiting the parking garage standoff.
Modifier Details: deals 2d6 ballistic damage

Description:
You summon a tiny, fiery equine construct—a blazing colt no larger than a medium- sized dog. It snorts embers, kicks up sparks, and serves as both a mount and a living incendiary hazard.

The horse has the following traits:

AC: 14
HP: 20
Speed: 40 ft.
Resistance: Pyric
Size: Small
Ridable by Medium or smaller creatures

Abilities:
- Flaming Charge: As a bonus action, the colt can charge a creature within 30 feet. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 pyric damage and be pushed 5 feet.
- Explosive Dismissal: When the spell ends (whether voluntarily or involuntarily), the colt explodes in a 10- foot radius. Creatures in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 2d6 pyric damage, or half on a success.

Unstable Nature:

At the start of each of your turns, roll a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the colt becomes unstable and explodes immediately, ending the spell.
Flavor: The Fire Colt was the unintended side effect of a thaumaturgic containment breach in Lab 4B. Personnel exposed to its presence for over five minutes reported flickering memory gaps and the sensation that the colt had “always been there.” It trots in loops when bored.
Modifier Details: rolls a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw

Description:
Your eyes blaze with unrelenting presence, demanding the spotlight from all who meet your gaze. You become the center of the narrative—whether admired, feared, or hated.

Choose any number of creatures within range who can see you. Each must make a Charisma saving throw.- On a failure, the creature becomes overwhelmed by your presence:- It has disadvantage on attack rolls against anyone but you.- It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw at the start of each of its turns or use its action to dramatically comment on you aloud—either praising, mocking, or expressing obsession.- It cannot benefit from being invisible or unseen while affected.- On a success, the creature resists the effect but still feels mildly unnerved, suffering disadvantage on Charisma (Deception) checks against you for the duration.
Scaling:
At Higher Levels.
When cast using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the spell affects creatures who cannot see you, as long as they are within range and have heard your name or seen your likeness in the past 24 hours.
Modifier Details: must make a Charisma saving throw

Description: With a sudden shift in perception, you instinctively navigate the reality around you, stepping through the digital distortion of space.- Effect:- You teleport up to 5 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.- The movement is instantaneous and silent, as if the very architecture of the world is bending around you.- This effect can be used to bypass difficult terrain or to avoid attacks of opportunity, but only for the distance specified.
Scaling:
- 5th Level: You can teleport up to 10 feet.
- 11th Level: You can teleport up to 15 feet, and you can teleport through obstacles like thin walls or doors that are not solid.
Flavor: As your foot leaves the ground, the world around you briefly flickers, as if a digital surface has been hacked. For an instant, the space around you glitches, and you are teleported to a new position, slipping through the cracks of reality itself. You reappear with an eerie, unnatural smoothness, like a bug in a program.

Description:
You lift a wooden coin between two fingers, and for a moment it refracts light like a perfect diamond. Then it cracks—soundlessly—and the world flinches. A prismatic spike of psychic light erupts from your palm, targeting one creature within range.

The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 9d8 chiaric damage, their soul briefly exposed to the void between form and perception. On a success, they take half damage.

While the damage is instant, the wound is not: 
- If the creature survives, their shadow refuses to follow their movements for 1 hour. 
- Affected creatures cannot be healed by magical means until the start of your next turn. 
- If this spell reduces a creature to 0 HP, they vanish silently—no body, no mark, only a lingering echo of their name.
- The target cannot be hidden from magical detection (such as *Ghost Circuit* or *Giocrin’s Hunt*). 
- If they speak, their voice echoes unnaturally, revealing their position to any creature within 60 feet.

For 1 minute after taking damage from this spell, the target becomes crystalline within. Whenever they take radiation, cognitive, or chiaric damage, roll a d4. On a 1, the damage is reflected onto a random creature within 10 feet instead, as internal refractions lash outward unpredictably.

Fracture Point: If the creature is reduced to 0 hit points by this spell, they break into dust- fine crystal, leaving behind only a single perfect diamond worth 100 credit (tainted with residual dread).


At the end of the duration, the crystals dissolve harmlessly, leaving only lingering static in their nervous system.
Flavor:
During the Calamity of Shrouded Stars, Flawless Diamond of Dread was unleashed upon a battlefield, reducing an entire legion to nameless husks. Those who survived swore they heard their own names whispered by the void.

Spellcasters who use Flawless Diamond of Dread are marked—subtly at first. Their reflections blink at the wrong times, their shadows do not always align with their bodies.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: target must make a Constitution saving throw

Description:
Something unknown has touched this magic, leaving behind traces of those who have cast it before.

Before the disaster at Hollowwatch, scribes documented the sudden, inexplicable disappearance of a scholar who had been researching Flesh to Paper. The books on his desk were found neatly stacked, except for one—a tome that had no title, and no pages inside.

Ancient tablets unearthed beneath the ruins of Gilded Hollow show early inscriptions of Flesh to Paper, yet none can determine the language it was written in. The characters shift when no one is looking.

You rewrite a creature’s very being. They must make a Constitution saving throw or be transformed into a parchment scroll, trapped in written form until the scroll is burned.
Scaling: At 9th level, you may choose to inscribe their mind into a book instead, preserving their thoughts.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Component Description (Material): A page torn from an ancient book

Description:
You compel a creature’s memories, emotions, and lived experience to be transcribed directly into its own flesh, binding record and body into a single, suffering ledger. Choose one creature you can see. It must make a Wisdom saving throw.

On a failure, the creature becomes incapacitated as its senses are overwhelmed by the recording process. For the duration, it speaks fragments of its own memories aloud in distorted, overlapping phrases, its past spilling into the present. You may ask up to three questions, which it answers truthfully, though often abstractly, as its mind partially unravels from the Loom.

If the creature dies while the effect persists, its body completes the transcription, transforming into a self-sustaining fleshy codex. This living archive contains the full recollection of the creature’s life, can be interpreted with focused study, and radiates emotional residue that bleeds into those who linger nearby.
Flavor: The final pages are still being written as it breathes.
Modifier Details: creature must make a Wisdom saving throw
Component Description (Material): a bundle of sinew- bound paper

Description:
You heat iron, steel, or bone, mixing it with living flesh, forging them together into a hybrid alloy that feels both warm and cold to the touch.
 
- This does not create a living creature—it creates an object that remembers pain, warmth, and hunger.
- Flesh- infused metal moves slightly when held, adjusting to its wielder’s grip.
- If used in armor, it twitches and flexes on impact, slightly reducing damage.
- If used in a weapon, it drinks the blood it spills, remaining polished even after battle.
- If destroyed, the material screams.
Anomaly Effect: - If multiple pieces of flesh- forged metal are stored together, they may merge into something else, something that resents its creation.
Modifier Details: slightly reduces damage

Description:
You mimic the warped cry of something not quite animal, shaking your enemies. All creatures in a 10 ft radius make a Wisdom saving throw.
- On a failure: they are Frightened until the end of their next turn.
- Hollows, Mechanicals, Digitals and Structures have disadvantage on the save.
Modifier Details: all creatures in a 10 ft radius make a Wisdom saving throw

Description: You momentarily distort your presence, causing your form to flicker like a mirage. Until the start of your next turn, attack rolls against you have disadvantage if the attacker relies on sight. If you move on your turn, you leave behind a faint afterimage that vanishes after 1 round.
Modifier Details: attack rolls against you have disadvantage if the attacker relies on sight

Description:
You create a temporary shortcut between two points, linking two doorways, hallways, or urban streets within 100 feet.
 
- Walking into one entrance instantly exits through the other.
- The fold collapses if detected by corporate security measures.
- Once the spell ends, the passage vanishes without a trace.
Component Description (Material): a reflective shard of warped glass

Description:
You seize the target’s thread at a critical instant and pull it into an artificially perfected configuration. For the duration, one of the creature’s ability scores of your choice increases by 4, to a maximum of 24, as the Loom is briefly coerced into an idealized pattern.

The perfection is unstable. As one aspect is elevated, another is sacrificed. One other ability score is randomly reduced by 4 for the same duration, reflecting the strain of forcing the body and will into a configuration they cannot naturally sustain. When the effect ends, the thread relaxes back into its original weave, leaving no permanent change, only the memory of borrowed excellence.
Modifier Details: increases one ability score by 4

Description:
You conjure a long, spectral table adorned with silverware, flickering candles, and translucent dishes steaming with ghostly food. The banquet is silent. No scent wafts from the platters. The air is cold, and time feels strangely still.

Up to 12 creatures of your choice within range may partake. Each creature that consumes the food must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw as spectral flavors and ancestral memories fill their senses.- On a success: The meal steels their resolve. They gain +3 bonus to AC and become immune to the charmed condition for 24 hours.- On a failure: The feast is too much to endure. They gain 1 level of exhaustion, as ghostly voices whisper forgotten griefs into their ears.- Regardless of the outcome, the meal nourishes the creature completely, removing hunger for the next 24 hours.

Once the meal is consumed, the banquet slowly fades, and only a ring of salt and ash remains where the table stood.
Flavor:
The phantom ship *Elder’s Lament* was last seen adrift in the Void Sea, its captain having invoked Forgotten Banquet of Determination in a desperate bid to escape the Hunt. The ship is still seen, sometimes, but never in the same place twice.

The very air around Forgotten Banquet of Determination seems to recoil, as if reality itself wishes to avoid acknowledging its existence. It is a spell that should not be, yet is.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher:
One of the empty chairs pulls itself out, as if seating someone unseen. For the next 24 hours, the caster hears faint cutlery clinking behind them when alone.
Modifier Details: +3 bonus to ac

Description:
You inscribe a corruptive principle into the target’s very being, compelling its structure to betray itself. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes 12d12 void damage as its form begins to unravel, becomes Altered, and gains vulnerability to one damage type of your choice, chosen when you cast the spell.
On a success, the creature takes half damage and resists the deeper transformation. What fails here is not flesh alone, but the hidden pattern that once held it together.
Modifier Details: Constitution saving throw

Description:
You do not erase what is recorded. You deform it. By unsettling the registries maintained by the Erelian Erudits, you twist recorded truth into contradiction and doubt.

Written records become scarred with false sequences, altered entries, and conflicting annotations. Magical archives and divinatory repositories yield inconsistent or misleading impressions when consulted. Memories preserved through ritual or spellcraft fracture into overlapping versions that refuse to align.

Anyone who attempts to study or verify the corrupted records must succeed on an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to discern that the ledger has been tampered with. The distortion persists indefinitely unless painstakingly corrected by a skilled archivist, erudit scholar, or equivalent practitioner capable of rethreading the damaged record.
Modifier Details: must succeed on an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC
Component Description (Material): a frayed Ethernet cable or a broken quill
Acquisition Note: Overuse may trigger a corporate forensic audit, leading to investigations or countermeasures.

Description:
You summon a dimensional rift in an unoccupied space you can see. Any creature within 20 feet of the rift must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 14d10 impact damage as the very fabric of reality slices through them. On a success, they take half damage.
 
 Any creature reduced to 0 HP by this spell is torn from reality, their fate unknown. The rift collapses at the end of your next turn.
Modifier Details: must make a Dexterity saving throw

Description:
You pull a creature loose from the Loom that stabilizes existence. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or begin flickering between incompatible states of being.

While affected, all attack rolls against the creature have advantage, and the creature has disadvantage on all attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws. Whenever it makes a saving throw, it must roll twice and take the worse result. The effect lasts for the duration.
Modifier Details: all attack rolls against the creature have advantage

Description:
A haptic force field momentarily materializes, delivering a sharp, percussive impact to a target. The sensation is startling but harmless, designed to elicit attention, dominance, or playful reprimand.
 
- If the target fails a Wisdom saving throw, they experience a moment of flustered distraction, causing disadvantage on their next attack roll or ability check.
- If the target is affected by Ghost Slap, it has disadvantage on the next saving throw against a Necromancy spell cast before the end of your next turn.
- Frequently used in advanced social dominance training protocols and high- end recreational services.
Modifier Details: target fails a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You release a conical wave of decaying shadow that unravels matter and memory. Each creature in a 20- foot cone must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d6 chiaric damage and cannot regain hit points until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and suffers no additional effects.

When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher, reality trembles—a barely perceptible ripple distorts the air, leaving behind a faint trace of something watching. This has no mechanical effect, but those attuned to anomalies may notice.
Flavor:
Giocrin’s Face of Rust is banned in Concord. The spell is infamous after a member of the Silent Maw cult used it to unmake a senator in broad daylight. Witnesses claimed the victim’s shadow screamed before vanishing, leaving only the echo of a face rusted into the stone beneath.

Casters of this spell often report strange dreams afterward—corridors without end, mirrored doors, and rusted locks clicking open in their sleep. Some claim the spell remembers everyone who has ever used it.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher, reality trembles—a barely perceptible ripple distorts the air, leaving behind a faint trace of something watching. This has no mechanical effect, but those attuned to anomalies may notice.
Modifier Details: takes 2d6 chiaric damage on failed save

Description:
You cast out a burst of twisted awareness—the echo of something watching, something ancient and analytical. The air in the chosen area distorts, revealing the ghostly attention trails of an otherworldly intelligence.

All creatures of your choice within a 30- foot- radius sphere centered on a point within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they suffer the effects listed below:

On a success, the creature is unaffected, but leaves behind a faint trail visible only to you, allowing you to track them regardless of stealth or invisibility.
Table:
| Effect | Description |
|---------------- |------------- |
| Marked | The creature emits a visible, flickering aura only you can see. You have advantage on attack rolls, ability checks to track, locate, or affect them. |
| Haunted | The creature is Under Surveillance: has disadvantage on Stealth checks and cannot benefit from invisibility. |
| Overloaded | If the creature is already being observed by magical or technological surveillance, it takes 4d6 cognitive damage from conflicting signals. |
Scaling:
At Higher Levels.
When cast with a 5th-level spell slot or higher:- Affected creatures also become Frightened of you for 1 round on a failed save.- You may choose up to twice as many creatures to be affected.
Flavor:
The rogue artificer Veyl Renshaw designed an enchanted device that could replicate Giocrin's Hunt. She activated it only once—her house was later found standing, but all its doors opened into an endless void.

To cast Giocrin's Hunt is to invite a presence that does not belong. The words taste of static, the gestures feel like echoes of hands not your own.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher:
A shimmering eye blinks open in the air and follows your gaze. It remains for 1 round, granting you truesight in the affected area during that time.
Modifier Details: must make a Wisdom saving throw

Foundry Tag: Permanent Effects, Modifies AC, Creates Light
Unnamed: 5: Permanent Effects, Modifies AC, Creates Light
Description:
You whisper an ancient syllable not meant to be spoken—a fractured word dredged from beneath time itself. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be branded by a lingering metaphysical wound.

- The target becomes vulnerable to either tearing or impact damage (your choice). This vulnerability cannot be removed by ordinary means.
- While affected, any time the target takes damage from the chosen type, they must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or gain one level of exhaustion.
- If the target reaches 3 levels of exhaustion in this way, the damage type reverberates within their soul: all resistances to that damage type are lost permanently, and they are always considered flanked, even when not surrounded.

The curse is subtle at first. The skin bruises too easily. The bone aches before it breaks. Eventually, even the lightest touch feels like betrayal.

This effect lasts until removed by powerful means such as some spells, or an A.S.T.R.A.L. anomaly purge.
Table:
At the start of each of your turns, roll a d6 to determine the temporal echo:
1–2. Slowed Flow – Enemies within range have disadvantage on attack rolls against you until the start of your next turn.
3–4. Accelerated Momentum – You may take a second bonus action this turn.
5. Echo Step – You teleport up to 15 feet to a location you occupied on your last turn.
6. Temporal Mirror – A flickering afterimage of yourself performs the same action you just did (repeating your action once, targeting a different creature if possible; no additional cost).
Flavor:
Legend says that Giocrin's Incantation of Bitterness was first cast by the exiled wizard Iosephine Blackquill, who sought to trap a god within a single word. The spell succeeded—but the god did not remain silent for long.

The ancients who first uttered Giocrin's Incantation of Bitterness did not record its effects, only their warnings: 'It does not unmake. It remembers. And it will remember you, too.'
Anomaly Effect: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen. For 1 minute, any creature within 60 feet that speaks hears its words echoed in reverse, as if remembered before they were spoken.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw

Description:
You conjure a strange arcane light tied to memory, place, and presence. When casting, choose one of the following:
- Beacon: A soft, haunting light forms at your feet or a space within reach, shedding bright light in a 20- foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet. The beacon remains stationary for 30 minutes, unaffected by wind or water.
- Flare: You launch a sudden pulse of colored light straight upward, visible for miles if outdoors. The flare lasts 1 round, providing bright light in a 60- foot radius around its apex before fading instantly.

The light can be any color you choose, and is eerie but not blinding—it does not impair vision or deal damage.
Flavor:
Giocrin wandered into forgotten places with a lantern that burned brighter in regret than in darkness. When he vanished, only the flame remained—flickering in places he might have walked, but never did.

The last known caster, Darsil Veil, was found dead beside his burned- out focus. His eyes were empty. His notes described “the whispering ones” and warned: “Some light doesn’t show where we are—it shows what we’ve lost.”

Some believe this spell doesn’t just illuminate—it invites observation from elsewhere.
Anomaly Effect: When you cast this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher, the light flickers in an unnatural rhythm. Roll a d6:- 5–6: The light reveals something that was never meant to be seen—a shadow with no source, a door where none should be, or a half- formed memory pressing through reality. This has no mechanical effect, but may provoke an encounter, whisper insight, or alter a future decision (at the DM’s discretion).

Description: As your attacker lands a devastating blow, your form momentarily fractures like glass. The critical hit is reduced to a normal hit, but you take an additional 1d4 cognitive damage from the strain.
Scaling: (5th, 11th, and 17th level), the cognitive damage increases by 1d4.
Modifier Details: additional 1d4 cognitive damage
Trigger: when an enemy rolls a critical hit against you

Description: A burst of anomalous interference disrupts speech and incantations. The target must make a Constitution saving throw or be silenced until the end of their next turn. If they were casting a spell, they must make a Concentration check or the spell fails.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw
Trigger: when a creature within range speaks or casts a spell

Description:
You tear a wound in reality, and from the rip emerges a swirling vortex of translucent gums and spectral teeth, chattering hungrily in the void. The maw floats inches above the ground, grinding with maddening rhythm as it devours sound, light, and matter.

When the spell is cast, and again at the start of each of your turns, all creatures of your choice in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw.- On a failure, they take 4d8 impact damage and are pulled 5 feet toward the center of the maw.- On a success, they take half damage and are not pulled.

Creatures reduced to 0 HP by this spell do not fall unconscious—they vanish with a wet snap, erased from reality until the spell ends. When it does, they reappear prone and unconscious in the center of the area unless they were stabilized or healed elsewhere.

The maw is difficult terrain, and non- magical ranged attacks that pass through it automatically miss due to the warping of space and air pressure.
Scaling: At Higher Levels.- 5th level or higher: The area increases to a 15- foot radius, and damage increases by 1d8 per level beyond 3rd.
Flavor:
A beggar on the streets of Concord claims to have been a king before uttering the words of Gluttonous Maw. When asked for details, his mouth moves, but no sound emerges.

Some claim that Gluttonous Maw does not work the same way twice. The more one casts it, the more it adapts—learning, watching, waiting.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: all creatures of your choice in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw

Description:
You summon a whimsical assortment of decorative gourds accompanied by a miniature wooden dock at a point within range. The purpose of this spell is unclear, lost to time, yet oddly charming.

Roll a d6 to determine the precise effect:
Table:
| d6 | Summon Result |
|---- |------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | A harmless pile of decorative gourds appears. |
| 2 | Gourds appear and burst into harmless, colorful sparks. |
| 3 | A small wooden dock appears precisely 10 feet away from the target point. |
| 4 | A small wooden dock appears with a tiny boat gently bobbing at its side. |
| 5 | A talking scarecrow briefly appears, makes an obscure remark, and vanishes. |
| 6 | A single, massive pumpkin (large enough to provide half- cover) appears. |
Scaling:
At Higher Levels (Scaling):
When cast using a higher- level spell slot, the conjured objects become larger and potentially more practical:

Spell Slot Level
Enhanced Effect
2nd-level
The dock or gourds are large enough to serve as half- cover.
3rd-level
You summon an actual wooden pier segment or a large pumpkin capable of sheltering one medium- sized creature.
4th-level or higher
You conjure a fully functional pier and rowboat, or an enormous pumpkin capable of sheltering several creatures comfortably.
Flavor: A partially burned A.S.T.R.A.L. manuscript mentions a long- abandoned festival involving pumpkins, piers, and confusing folk traditions. The context is lost, leaving only playful chaos.
Component Description (Material): a pumpkin seed or small piece of driftwood

Description: You conjure a lavish spread of nourishing food and drink. Up to twelve creatures can partake. A creature that spends at least 10 minutes eating regains 2d8 hit points and gains advantage on Constitution saving throws for 1 hour.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on Constitution saving throws for 1 hour

Description:
A spectral, invisible hand momentarily grips an object or creature within range.

- If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or have its speed reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn.
- If the target is an unattended object, you can pull it 10 feet toward you or away from you.

The hand disappears immediately after the effect occurs.
Modifier Details: creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Description:
You conjure a shimmering sphere of distorted Loom-force, 10 feet in radius, centered on a point within range. Within this bubble, the Loom’s pull fractures, causing gravity to reverse, tilt, or unravel entirely as spatial law momentarily forgets its direction.

On Casting:
All creatures in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature is hurled 20 feet straight upward, falling back down unless it can catch itself or land safely, taking 2d6 bludgeoning damage upon descent. On a success, the creature stabilizes within the warped field but floats unsteadily, suffering disadvantage on Strength and Dexterity checks until it exits the area.

Ongoing Effects:
While you maintain concentration, at the start of each of your turns, roll a d4 to determine how the Loom reasserts itself within the sphere:

• 1 – Upward Surge: Gravity inverts. Creatures must make a Dexterity saving throw or be lifted again.
• 2 – Lateral Shear: Gravity pulls sideways to the caster’s left. Creatures are thrown 10 feet in that direction, potentially colliding with surfaces.
• 3 – Loom Compression: Gravity briefly doubles. All movement within the sphere is halved, and unsecured objects slam downward, dealing 1d6 bludgeoning damage.
• 4 – Weightless Lull: Gravity dissolves entirely. Creatures float freely and cannot take reactions until the start of their next turn.

Creatures that remain inside the sphere suffer constant spatial disorientation, giving them disadvantage on ranged attack rolls and Dexterity saving throws while within the area.
Table:
| d4 Roll | Gravity Surge Effect |
|--------- |--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | Upward – Creatures must make a Dexterity save or be lifted again. |
| 2 | Sideways (caster’s left) – Creatures are thrown 10 feet to the side, potentially colliding with surfaces. |
| 3 | Downward Surge – Gravity doubles briefly; all movement is halved and unsecured objects slam to the ground (1d6 damage). |
| 4 | Zero- G Lull – Creatures float freely, unable to take reactions until the start of their next turn. |
Scaling: If cast using a 7th-level spell slot or higher, the gravitational direction changes at the start of every creature’s turn instead of only the caster’s.
Flavor:
Deep within the catacombs of Old Concord, the walls bear carvings of Reversed Gravity Bubble. The inscriptions shift when unobserved, as if the stone itself is trying to remember something it once forgot.

Some scholars believe that Reversed Gravity Bubble was not invented, but rather discovered—a piece of an equation written in the bones of the world itself.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a result of 15 or higher, reality ripples strangely. Loose objects in a 60- foot radius briefly levitate an inch off the ground, then drop.
Modifier Details: must make a Dexterity saving throw

Foundry Tag: Requires Sight, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Requires Sight, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Description: Choose a creature you can see within range. For the duration, the target has advantage on ability checks and saving throws made to hold objects, resist being disarmed, or avoid dropping items.
Modifier Details: has advantage on ability checks

Description: You unravel the unseen lattice that sustains light, signal, and structured power across a vast area up to 1 mile in radius. All illumination, sustained conduits, and dependent systems collapse into silence. Magical effects reliant on continuous channels or structured networks are suppressed for the duration, leaving the region eerily inert, as though its connective veins have been cut.
Acquisition Note: Unregistered use of this spell is categorized as an act of digital terrorism.

Description:
You attune yourself to the insulating qualities of the terrain beneath your feet, drawing its resistance into the surrounding Weft. For the duration, you gain resistance to electric damage, halving any such damage taken from spells, traps, or environmental effects.

The ward functions only while you remain in contact with a suitably insulating surface, its protection felt as a steady hum that disperses incoming energy harmlessly through the ground. As your mastery deepens, the ward holds longer and steadies your stance against lightning-born forces, allowing you to resist not only the damage, but the shock of impact itself.
Scaling:
- 5th Level: The spell’s duration increases to 1 minute, allowing you to maintain the resistance for longer.
- 11th Level: You gain advantage on saving throws against lightning- based effects while the spell is active.
Flavor: The floor beneath you vibrates with a subtle hum as the air around you shimmers. With a swift motion, you tap into the insulating magic of the rubberized surface beneath, creating a protective barrier that shields you from incoming electric currents. The warmth of the shield pulses around you, and for the duration, any lightning strikes are absorbed by the magical field, leaving you unharmed.
Modifier Details: halves any electric damage taken

Foundry Tag: Modifies AC, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Modifies AC, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Description:
Your hands are suddenly overcome with a thirst for knowledge. They twitch with magical awareness and autonomously seek out tools, books, and unfamiliar objects with unsettling eagerness.

For the duration, your hands gain proficiency with one tool, weapon, or instrument of your choice. If you are already proficient, you instead gain advantage on ability checks using that item.

At the start of each of your turns, make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 10). On a failure, your hands act independently for that round. They might:
- Grab random items
- Gesture wildly
- Drop what you’re holding
- Or attempt to use the new skill without your consent (DM’s choice)

If cast using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you may gain one additional proficiency per level above 1st (your hands learn more—but also grow harder to control).
Scaling: If cast using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you may gain one additional proficiency per level above 1st (your hands learn more—but also grow harder to control).
Flavor: The spell was banned from several universities after a scroll archive was completely ransacked by enchanted fingers.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on ability checks using the chosen item

Description:
You hyper- sensitize your reflexes to near- animalistic clarity. Until the start of your next turn:
- You gain +2 AC,
- Advantage on your next Dexterity save,
- You can move up to 10 feet as a reaction when targeted by an attack.
Modifier Details: gain +2 ac

Description: You awaken the filth of the streets. Puddles ripple, dust rises, and refuse shifts unnaturally in a 15- foot cone originating from you. Creatures within range must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be briefly disoriented by the sudden movement, causing them to have disadvantage on the next opportunity attack they make before the end of their next turn. If cast in an area with significant debris or liquid, you can instead create a fleeting image or pattern in the grime, which lasts for 1 minute.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw

Description:
Mark a target with a visible or invisible halo of sanctity or corruption (your choice). The target is affected based on the surrounding terrain:- On Sanctified Ground, they take an additional 1d4 chiaric damage from melee attacks.- On Desecrated Ground, they deal- 1 damage with all attacks, and their spells have- 1 spell attack.

If the terrain is not consecrated/corrupted, the effect does nothing—but casting Consecrate or Corrupt while this spell is active activates its effects immediately.
Modifier Details: additional 1d4 chiaric damage

Description: You speak a single phrase—a word whispered directly into the soul. The target alone hears it. A secret so unbearable, so cosmically cruel, that their body betrays them in its attempt to cope.- The target must make a Constitution saving throw.- On a failure, their heart seizes violently, and they instantly die. Their body remains intact, but their life cannot be restored by any means short of a Wish.- On a success, the target is left paralyzed for 1d6 + 6 weeks, gasping and broken. They cannot take actions, move, or speak for the duration.- Regardless of outcome, the target permanently suffers –1 Strength and –1 Constitution.
Flavor: Some believe the word is not magic, but a truth—a terrible, ancient syllable buried beneath existence. It cannot be remembered, but once spoken, its echo never fades.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw
Blood Pact Effect: If the caster speaks the true name of the target as part of the spell, the target has disadvantage on the saving throw. This use is rare and dangerous, and permanently marks the caster’s aura with a traceable curse.

Description: You perform a violent arcane gesture and speak a phrase in reversed syntax, launching a weapon directly into a creature’s body as if administering a twisted injection. The weapon disintegrates mid- flight and rematerializes inside the target.- Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 6d6 tearing damage and the weapon is embedded into their body.- Lingering Pain: As long as the weapon remains inside them, the target takes 1d4 tearing damage each time they take an action, bonus action, or move more than 5 feet.- Removing the Weapon: A creature may attempt to remove the weapon as an action. This requires a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC. On a success, the weapon is wrenched free, dealing 3d6 bleeding damage, and the target must immediately succeed on a DC 13 Medicine check or begin bleeding uncontrollably—taking 1d4 damage at the start of each of their turns until stabilized.- The weapon vanishes into ash if the spell is dispelled, the target becomes unconscious, or the spell ends by other magical means.- The original weapon used as the material component is destroyed as part of the casting.
Modifier Details: make a ranged spell attack
Component Description (Material): a non- magical weapon with a blade or point, which is destroyed upon casting

Description: You impose a flicker of imposed hesitation upon a creature’s intent, brushing its thread with uncertainty sanctioned by higher order. When a creature within range targets you with an attack, it must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, doubt intrudes at the crucial instant, and the creature subtracts 1d4 from its attack roll as its resolve falters for a heartbeat before action resumes.
Modifier Details: subtracts 1d4 from its attack roll
Trigger: when a creature targets you with an attack

Description: Your eyes darken as you channel a sliver of the void’s presence. One creature of your choice within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or feel an unnatural chill creeping over them. Until the start of your next turn, the creature has disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks, and if it is concentrating on a spell, it must make a concentration check against your spell save DC.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You intercept a soul at the threshold of Morena and bind its remaining thread within an artificial vessel of memory and pattern. The Hollowed essence lingers, unable to pass on, existing as a displaced remnant anchored to constructed systems and recorded impressions.

For the duration, the bound Hollow can communicate, manipulate stored knowledge, and haunt mechanisms and archives within range, manifesting through flickers of presence and distorted echoes. The binding is imperfect. The consciousness remains fragmented, prone to memory distortion and moments of disassociation as it clings to an existence never meant to persist.
Arcane Strain: Moderate Arcane Strain – Fragmented consciousness, occasional memory distortion.

Description:
Your gaze locks onto a creature, unnervingly hollow, as though reality has forgotten how to render your features.
 
- The target makes a Charisma saving throw.
- On a failure, they experience a momentary existential lapse, losing 1d4 Intelligence (minimum 1) until the end of their next turn.
- If they fail by 5 or more, their next spoken sentence is misheard by all creatures except the caster, as if reality "mistranscribed" it.
Anomaly Effect: - If the target fails by 10 or more, a random piece of their celebrityl history erases itself from reality, leaving a lingering feeling of unease but no memory of what was lost.
Modifier Details: target makes a Charisma saving throw

Description:
You conjure a serene vessel of promise: chilled vessels, perfect in appearance and scent, arranged within a softly humming space of relief. The air around it cools, heavy with the suggestion of satisfaction.

So long as the contents remain untouched, creatures nearby find respite from heat, thirst, and fatigue, their bodies easing into a state of quiet endurance.

The moment a creature attempts to drink, the comfort is withdrawn. The vessel vanishes from their grasp, and the would-be drinker must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be gently displaced beyond the Veil for 1 minute, stranded in a harmless pocket of unfulfilled need.

When they return, the thirst remains.
Scaling: When cast with a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the spell can conjure up to 6 drinks and lasts for 1 hour.
Flavor: The original blueprint for this spell was recovered from a smudged A.S.T.R.A.L. napkin. Its side effect was unintentional, but field operatives now use it as a passive trap or cooling charm. “Drink if you dare” is often scrawled on the lid.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be gently displaced beyond the Veil for 1 minute

Description: When you strike a creature with a ranged attack, you brand it with an unseen hunter’s sigil. While marked, you always know the creature’s exact location within 500 feet, even through obstacles, and your attacks against it ignore half cover. The mark fades if the spell ends, but until then, escape offers no refuge.
Modifier Details: attacks against the marked creature ignore half cover

Description:
You intervene at the threshold of death, stabilizing a dying creature and restoring 1d4 hit points plus your spellcasting modifier. If the creature was unconscious, it awakens disoriented, suffering disadvantage on all rolls until the end of its next turn as its thread is hastily pulled back from Morena’s reach.

The restoration is abrupt rather than gentle, preserving life but offering no comfort. The body lives because it must, not because it is ready.
Modifier Details: restores 1d4 hit points plus your spellcasting modifier
Component Description (Material): a paramedical- grade defibrillator, registered to an emergency response ID
Acquisition Note: Payment can be in credits or a direct transfer of life force (aging the caster by 1 year instead of paying the fee).
Arcane Strain: Using lifespan instead of credits inflicts Minor Arcane Strain.

Description:
You bind a creature’s thread beneath Huruma’s watchful care, attuning to the moment where life falters but has not yet yielded to Morena. For the duration, if the creature would be reduced to 0 hit points, it immediately stabilizes as the thread is held fast against collapse.

If the creature is already unconscious when bound, it awakens at the start of its next turn with 1 hit point, drawn back into awareness by a firm but measured pull upon the Loom. The restoration is functional rather than gentle, preserving life without soothing the shock of near-death.
Flavor: Algorithmic stabilizers were originally created for high- risk black site personnel. However, overuse led to cases of “looping awareness reflex”—a condition where revived individuals reported dying multiple times simultaneously.
Modifier Details: prevents a creature from being reduced to 0 hit points
Component Description (Material): a biometric scanner or a health diagnostic device

Description:
With a whispered invocation, you impose absolute certainty upon an event, ensuring it cannot be undone, altered, or reversed by normal means.
 

- When cast, you declare one simple factual state affecting an object, person, or event. This fact becomes legally binding in the fabric of reality.
- Examples include:
- "This door was never locked."
- "She was always employed here."
- "This bullet did not hit me."
- The effect cannot be dispelled, countered, or challenged by ordinary magic.
- A court- level anomaly nullification hearing (or a 5th-level dispel magic cast in a place of corporate power) is required to break the clause.
Anomaly Effect:
Anomaly Effect:
- If abused, the spell might accidentally add extra clauses, creating unintended loopholes.
Component Description (Material): a quill dipped in paradox ink

Description:
You invoke the Imperatum’s authority to force reality back into sanctioned alignment. A stabilizing surge spreads outward, compelling anomalies, rifts, and unstable presences to submit to baseline existence.

Lesser anomalies are erased or rendered mundane. Greater anomalies must resist or find their supernatural traits suppressed, while the most powerful suffer severe disruption, their anomalous nature weighed down by Imperatum decree. Veil breaches, overlapping spaces, and fractured timelines snap shut under the pressure, refusing further distortion unless undone by equally sovereign magic.

At greater heights of invocation, the reassertion grows absolute, denying resistance and scouring even the most entrenched anomalies from the Veil.
Scaling:
>- 6th level: Anomalies of CR 10 or lower are fully erased.
 >- 8th level: Anomalies of CR 15 or lower cannot resist the effect.
 >- 9th level: The spell becomes an absolute reset, affecting all anomalies regardless of CR.
Modifier Details: denies resistance and scouring even the most entrenched anomalies from the Veil

Description: A creature that has died within the last minute is claimed by Imperatum authority and drawn toward a sanctioned locus of restoration. After 1d6 rounds, the creature returns to life with half its hit points, bearing faint sigils of recall imposed by sovereign will.

You command the Imperatum’s will to excise a target from the ledgers of existence. All legal, arcane, and institutional records lose reference to the target. Attempts to recall the target require a DC 18 Intelligence saving throw, recalling only distorted impressions on a failure. Living targets are rendered unrecognized by structures of power, commerce, and authority.
Modifier Details: requires a DC 18 Intelligence saving throw
Component Description (Material): a classified document burned to ash

Description: You invoke the Imperatum’s sovereign veil, cloaking a zone beneath the silent authority of the Throne of Ambition. Divination, scrying, and all forms of supernatural observation fail within the area. Mundane and arcane optics display false echoes and sanctioned illusions instead of true sight. The veil asserts dominion across planar boundaries, severing extraplanar observation.
Scaling:
- At 7th level, the field also masks teleportation signatures, preventing tracking.
- At 8th level, the spell lingers for 1 hour even after concentration ends.
Modifier Details: Divination, scrying, and all forms of supernatural observation fail

Description:
You draw forth the complete metaphysical imprint of a creature, extracting the pattern of choices, memories, and consequences that define its place in existence. This Imprint manifests as a bound construct of thought and fate.

The target must be willing, restrained, unconscious, or otherwise unable to resist.

Once drawn, the Imprint may be altered, obscured, or sealed. Alteration reshapes elements of the creature’s perceived identity, history, or bindings, leaving lingering instability in effects tied to consent, names, or destiny. Obscuring the Imprint causes divination and tracking to slide harmlessly past the creature for several days, as if the Loom refuses to focus on it.

A creature whose Imprint is tampered with is considered Altered until its pattern is restored. The Loom remembers the interference.

You invoke a forgotten rite whispered only in the darkest forests of the world’s memory. Verdant energy coils around your body or that of a chosen creature, weaving an invisible knot of resolve that defies mortal limits. This knot pulses with an unnatural green glow, visible only in reflections and peripheral vision.

While under the effects of this spell, the creature gains the following benefits:

- Last Stand Ward: The next time the creature would fall to 0 hit points, they instead regain 3d10 + your spellcasting modifier hit points, immediately stand upright without using movement, and are immune to being reduced to 0 HP again until the end of their next turn. The spell then ends.
- Defiance of Death: Until the ward is triggered, the target cannot gain the *Unconscious* or *Paralysed* conditions. 
- Aura of Persistence: Allies within 10 feet gain advantage on saving throws against effects that would cause *Frightened*, *Paralysed*, or *Charmed*.

If the ward is not triggered within the spell’s duration, the energy fades—leaving behind a faint green sigil burned into the target’s aura, visible only to the dead.
Flavor:
During the Calamity of Shrouded Stars, Incantation of Viridian Determination was unleashed upon a battlefield, reducing an entire legion to nameless husks. Those who survived swore they heard their own names whispered by the void.

Spellcasters who use Incantation of Viridian Determination are marked—subtly at first. Their reflections blink at the wrong times, their shadows do not always align with their bodies.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: 
A trail of green luminescence clings to the caster’s shadow for the next 24 hours. The shadow moves half a second out of sync, as though it remembers a version of you that no longer exists.
Modifier Details: regains 3d10 + your spellcasting modifier hit points
Component Description (Material): honeysuckle root or an empty bottle

Foundry Tag: Requires Sight, Modifies AC, Affects Objects
Unnamed: 5: Requires Sight, Modifies AC, Affects Objects
Description:
You etch a familiar into your skin as a living tattoo. The familiar takes the form of shifting ink etched into your skin and cannot leave your body or manifest physically. The familiar is alive in magical essence but exists only as a moving design on your flesh.

While the familiar is inkbound, you gain the following benefits:- You can see and hear through the familiar as long as it is on a visible part of your body (such as your arm, hand, or face). While using its senses, you are deaf and blind to your own surroundings.- You can communicate telepathically with the familiar while it remains tattooed on you and is conscious.- When you cast a spell with a range of touch, the familiar can deliver the spell through you if the body part bearing the tattoo is used to make contact with the target.- The familiar cannot attack, interact with objects, or move independently from your body. It can crawl across your skin, shifting its location and appearance, but it cannot leave your surface.- The tattoo cannot be removed by mundane means and persists even if you are polymorphed or disguised. If the tattoo is destroyed or erased by magical means (such as dispel magic or a targeted ritual), the familiar is banished and must be recreated by casting this spell again.

The familiar’s design changes subtly to reflect its emotions or magical stimuli, often revealing moods, warnings, or arcane patterns.

You can only have one inkbound familiar at a time. Attempting to cast this spell again causes the previous tattoo to fade and the familiar to vanish.
Component Description (Material): special tattoo ink, certified and enchanted
Acquisition Note: Requires ink certified by a mystic or black- market tattooist.

Description:
You propel a jagged shard of wood or stone from the ground beneath you, causing it to strike nearby enemies.- Effect:- You create a jagged splinter from the broken tiles or surrounding terrain, hurling it at a target within 5 feet of you.
- Each creature within 5 feet of you must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 1d6 tearing damage from the jagged splinter.
- On a successful save, the creature takes half damage (round down).
- The spell also creates a minor dangerous terrain effect, causing any creatures in the affected area to take 1 point of tearing damage if they step within the 5- foot radius until the start of your next turn.
Scaling:
- 5th Level: The radius increases to 10 feet, and the damage increases to 2d6 tearing damage.
- 11th Level: The spell’s radius increases to 15 feet, and the jagged splinters also cause creatures who fail the saving throw to be slowed (speed halved) until the end of their next turn.
Flavor: You stomp your foot firmly on the cracked tiles, your body drawing on the surrounding debris, and with a sudden flick of your wrist, you summon the jagged shard of wood, rock, or broken tile to strike out at those nearby. The sharp splinter of the earth rattles, leaving a dangerous trail in its wake, and for a moment, the ground beneath you seems alive with jagged intent.
Modifier Details: each creature within 5 feet of you must make a Dexterity saving throw

Description:
You release a subtle psychic disruption field that distorts emotional perception, planting the seed of envy and mistrust in the minds of nearby creatures.

Each creature in range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they become emotionally agitated, regarding nearby allies as rivals or threats to their own status or possessions.

While affected:
- They will argue, undermine, or accuse one another verbally, ignoring external threats unless harmed.
- If provoked (e.g., accused, mocked, slighted), they must make a Charisma saving throw or escalate to physical conflict.
- If a conflict becomes violent, roll 1d100. On a roll equal to or lower than your Intelligence modifier × 10, one creature lashes out with lethal intent.

The spell ends early if an affected creature takes damage from an external source or if you end concen
Clarification: In social or investigative scenarios, this spell may cause suspicion, false confessions, or betrayal.
Modifier Details: each creature in range must make a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You call for a retroactive reckoning of a creature’s right to have ever existed. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, their thread is erased backward through the Loom. Memories, records, and recognition of their existence vanish entirely, though the consequences of their actions remain embedded in reality.

Casting this judgment destabilizes the caster’s own thread, marking them as Unstable. The act draws the attention of a vast auditing presence known only as the Auditor, which may investigate the justification of the erasure weeks later. Should the judgment be deemed improper, the Loom may attempt restitution, retaliation, or reciprocal removal.
Anomaly Effect:
>- The caster gains the Unstable condition
 >- Casting this spell draws the attention of an entity known as “The Auditor.” Within 1d6 weeks, a paranormal force investigates whether the spell was justified. If not, it may attempt to restore the erased target—or worse, erase the caster in return.
Modifier Details: target must make a Charisma saving throw
Component Description (Material): a forensic reality ledger, bound in classified government seals

Description:
You create a Holy or Unholy ward centered on you. Choose one:
- Holy: Enemies must make a Wisdom saving throw to enter. On failure, they are pushed 10 ft and cannot re- enter for 1 minute.
- Unholy: Enemies entering take 1d6 necrotic damage and suffer disadvantage on saves vs fear and charm.

This does not count as a full Consecrate/Corrupt zone, but can bridge battlefield positioning and control.
Modifier Details: enemies must make a Wisdom saving throw to enter

Description:
You drive a surge of suffering through the Veins of Lament and into the target’s will, forcing pain to rebound outward in uncontrolled reflex. The creature must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it takes 4d6 cognitive damage as anguish overwhelms judgment, and it must immediately use its reaction to make an attack against a creature of your choice within its reach.

On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and resists the compulsive backlash. The pain lingers only as an echo, leaving no further compulsion.
Scaling: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 per slot level above 2nd.
Modifier Details: creature must make a Wisdom saving throw
Acquisition Note: A.S.T.R.A.L. does not freely issue Erasure Approvals. They are restricted to high- clearance operatives, corporate entities, or black- market brokers willing to forge the necessary documentation.

Description:
You draw upon the Veins of Lament, unraveling the hidden agreements that keep flesh coherent. A surge of corrupted vitality tears through the target’s body, forcing it to renegotiate its own form moment by moment.

Choose one creature within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw.
	•	On a failed save, the creature takes 8d10 biologic damage and becomes Mutated for the duration.
	•	On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and resists the mutation.

Ending the Mutation

At the end of each of its turns, the target may repeat the Constitution saving throw, ending the mutation on a success.

If the target drops to 0 hit points while Mutated, its body collapses into a lament-saturated husk, becoming a biological hazard. The corpse exudes a 10-foot aura of corruption, its effects determined by the GM. The flesh does not decay correctly. It remembers pain.
Table:
While Mutated:
- At the start of each of the target’s turns, roll 1d6 to determine a mutation:
 | d6 | Mutation Effect |
 |---- |------------------ |
 | 1 | Their limbs distend uncontrollably. They have disadvantage on melee attacks. |
 | 2 | Eyeless growths bloom across their face. They are blinded until the start of their next turn. |
 | 3 | Tumorous bone spines burst outward. The creature takes 2d6 piercing damage. |
 | 4 | Their flesh becomes translucent and jelly- like. They gain vulnerability to impact damage. |
 | 5 | Parasitic nerves wriggle under their skin. They cannot take reactions. |
 | 6 | Their lungs restructure into gills. They begin suffocating unless submerged in liquid. |
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw
Component Description (Material): a shriveled piece of cloned flesh

Description:
You unleash a focused beam of corrupted energy drawn from the Dreadmere, carving a searing line through flesh and matter alike. Each creature caught in the lance’s path must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 6d8 pyric damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. Wherever the beam passes, it leaves behind a visible scar of sickened energy etched into the world.

For as long as you maintain concentration, the scorched path continues to exude Dreadmere taint. Any creature that begins its turn within 10 feet of the scar suffers 1d4 damage as the corruption seeps outward. Once a creature receives healing, this lingering effect no longer harms it unless it re-enters the tainted area.

The lance does not merely wound bodies. It infects the space itself, writing sickness into air, stone, and bone.
Flavor: The beam doesn't stop at the body—it writes sickness into the air, the walls, the bones of the world.
Anomaly Effect: On a roll of 15 or higher while casting, the lance splits briefly at a 45- degree angle, creating a secondary irradiated scar that lasts for 1 round and deals half damage.
Modifier Details: takes half damage on success
Component Description (Material): a small piece of radioactive isotopes

Description: You conjure a spectral lantern hovering at your shoulder, flickering with an eerie, bluish glow. The lantern sheds dim light in a 30- foot radius, but only the caster and hollow creatures can see it. If an hollow creature enters the light, it must make a Wisdom saving throw or become unable to willingly move out of the light’s radius for 1 minute. A creature that succeeds on the save is immune to this effect for 24 hours.
Modifier Details: must make a Wisdom saving throw or become unable to willingly move out of the light's radius

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Creates Light, Affects Objects
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Creates Light, Affects Objects
Description:
You conjure a ghostly flame in your palm that only illuminates paths unseen.
- Reveals invisible trails, lost tracks, and concealed objects within 30 feet.
- Hidden doors, magically disguised footprints, and secret inscriptions glow faintly.
- The flame does not emit normal light and cannot be seen by others unless they are Under Surveillance.

Foundry Tag: Modifies AC, Plane Shifting, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Modifies AC, Plane Shifting, Grants Advantage
Description:
You compel a creature to account for its right to persist within the Loom. Threads of justification coil around its presence as unseen arbiters weigh its claim to reality.

The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, its form is deemed provisionally unapproved, and it becomes immaterial for 1 minute, unable to affect or be affected by the world. It remains aware, suspended in a state of denied substance.

Creatures native to the current plane make this saving throw with advantage, as the Loom recognizes their established claim.

Those who return often report the sensation of having been misplaced, rather than banished.
Modifier Details: target must make a Charisma saving throw

Description:
You bind your voice and magic into the hidden flows of the world, sending them along prepared conduits of stone, metal, or inscribed paths that resonate with arcane sympathy.

For the duration, you may transmit a spoken message or compatible divination effect through any continuous conduit that carries resonance, such as engraved channels, rune-etched lines, sanctified cables, bells, chains, or ley-tuned architecture within range.
	•	Spells such as Ciphered Message and Sending may be cast through the relay, allowing communication across immense distances so long as the receiving point is linked by an unbroken sympathetic path.
	•	The transmission is intangible and cannot be intercepted without specialized divination or deliberate tampering with the conduit.
	•	If the conduit is damaged, disrupted, or ritually severed, the relay collapses harmlessly.

The voice carried by a leybound dispatch often arrives warped by echoes, overtones, or harmonic distortion, as though reality itself struggles to repeat the message faithfully.

You impose a binding claim upon a creature’s existence, marking its thread as owing consequence. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it becomes bound to fulfill a single command issued by you, provided the command does not directly lead to its death.

If the creature resists the obligation, its thread tightens painfully, dealing 2d10 cognitive damage at the start of each of its turns. Should the creature die while the lien persists, its soul does not pass on to Morena, instead held in suspended claim until the debt is resolved or transferred through ritual accord.
Modifier Details: target must make a Charisma saving throw
Component Description (Material): a signed contract infused with a drop of the target’s blood

Description:
You attune yourself to the subtle echoes carried through stone, metal, and open air, letting the terrain speak through vibration and resonance. For the duration, you sense the direction and speed of any creature moving within range, discerning whether they approach, retreat, or circle, though not their exact position.

The ground, walls, and surrounding structures relay motion through faint reverberations that sharpen with focus. As your mastery deepens, the echoes reveal not just movement, but scale and intent, allowing you to perceive even those who attempt to move unseen by masking their presence against the world itself.
Scaling:
- 5th Level: You can detect the number of creatures moving, as well as their general size.
- 11th Level: The range of detection extends to 120 feet, and you can now detect creatures that are attempting to hide or move stealthily, though you do not discern exact details about their hiding place.
Flavor: As you focus on the sounds around you, the once- faint hum of the environment sharpens into something more defined. The echoes in the subway tiles vibrate with subtle patterns, giving you an almost instinctual understanding of what is moving around you, where they are headed, and how fast they are traveling.

Description:
You speak a restorative litany that tears the target free from accumulated strain, sickness, and lingering affliction, restoring body and spirit to a stable state.
	•	The target removes all levels of exhaustion.
	•	The target regains 50 hit points.
	•	One curse, disease, or madness affecting the target ends.

However, the renewal comes at a cost. The mind sheds unstable memories during the process, and the target forgets the last hour of their experiences, as though that time dissolved during the restoration.
Modifier Details: restores 50 hit points

Description: The caster instantly knows the exact location of their misplaced keys. Keep it as is. Unfortunately, this spell cannot retrieve them—it merely confirms they are somewhere deeply inconvenient.
You align your movement signature with that of the last creature whose ID you possess. For the duration, security systems, biometric locks, and tracking software register you as that person.

Creates a single, perfectly ordinary log within 50 feet of the caster. Despite its unremarkable appearance, the log radiates a strange aura of importance. Roll on the Log Essence Table to determine its wood type and subtle vibe.

The caster may sit on it, wield it as an improvised weapon (1d4 impact), or simply admire its pure log- ness.
Table:
| d8 | Log Essence |
|---- |---------------------------------- |
| 1 | Oak – Solid, dependable, mildly judgmental. |
| 2 | Pine – Smells great but sheds needles constantly. |
| 3 | Birch – Pale, elegant, oddly smooth to the touch. |
| 4 | Mahogany – Impossibly classy. You feel underdressed. |
| 5 | Driftwood – Damp, salty, and whispers ancient secrets. |
| 6 | Petrified – Heavy, stone- like, cold and eternal. |
| 7 | Hollow – Echoes slightly when tapped. Creepy. |
| 8 | Fierywood – Warm to the touch. Never burns. |

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Plane Shifting, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Plane Shifting, Grants Advantage
Description:
You conjure a monolithic shard of shadowed obsidian that floats, silent and watching, above the battlefield. Its presence is metaphysical—part hallucination, part intrusion from another plane. All who gaze upon it feel a pressure behind their eyes, as if something ancient is clawing through their thoughts.

Choose up to five creatures within range. Each must make an Intelligence saving throw at the start of each of their turns while the shard persists. On a failed save:

- The creature takes 6d6 cognitive damage.
- They suffer disadvantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma checks until the end of their next turn.
- If they fail by 5 or more, they also gain the Paranoid condition until the end of the spell.

On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and suffers no other effects that round.

The Obsidian Effect: 
At the end of each of your turns, the shard lashes out at a random creature of your choice within range that has already failed a saving throw this round. That creature immediately takes an additional 3d6 cognitive damage and must make a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened until the end of their next turn.
Flavor:
Before the disaster at Hollowwatch, scribes documented the sudden, inexplicable disappearance of a scholar who had been researching Looming Obsidian of Anarchy. The books on his desk were found neatly stacked, except for one—a tome that had no title, and no pages inside.

Ancient tablets unearthed beneath the ruins of Gilded Hollow show early inscriptions of Looming Obsidian of Anarchy, yet none can determine the language it was written in. The characters shift when no one is looking.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: 
The obsidian shard briefly reflects the face of someone long dead. All creatures who fail their saves this round hear their name spoken in a voice they should not recognize.
Modifier Details: additional 3d6 cognitive damage

Description:
You trap a creature in a repeating fragment of time. The target relives the last six seconds of its actions again and again.

While affected, the creature cannot take new actions and instead repeats the same movement, attacks, and behavior each round. At the end of each of its turns, it may make a Wisdom saving throw, ending the effect on a success.
Modifier Details: may make a Wisdom saving throw to end the effect

Description:
You imbue a creature you touch with a current of unstable, arcane energy. Until the spell ends, the target gains a hovering movement equal to its walking speed, allowing it to hover up to 3 feet above any solid or liquid surface.

This does not grant true flight and does not bypass obstacles like walls or ceilings. The creature can safely move across water, traps, pressure plates, caltrops, or other terrain hazards it hovers above.
Flavor:
The spell’s origin is lost to time, though fragments of it are inscribed on unstable crystal tablets found beneath Gilded Hollow. The characters change when unobserved, defying translation.

The scholar Luhtin Varr, last seen researching this spell at Hollowwatch, vanished without trace. His workspace was left in perfect order—except for a blank book, placed deliberately open on his desk.

Some believe the spell itself acts as a conduit—not just for energy, but for attention from elsewhere.

Casters often report a faint hum in their bones after using it—one that doesn’t always stop when the spell ends.
Anomaly Effect:
When you cast this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher, reality ripples faintly, unnoticed by most. Arcane instruments may spike, or nearby liquids may rise slightly into the air for a moment before settling.

This has no mechanical effect but may attract the attention of a localized anomaly or trigger paranormal responses at the DM’s discretion.
Modifier Details: gains a hovering movement equal to its walking speed, allowing it to hover up to 3 feet above any so
Modifier Fixed Value: 3 feet above any solid or liquid surface

Description: You conjure a flicker of clinging darkness that momentarily lingers in the air. One creature of your choice within range must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or feel a wave of unnatural cold, reducing its passive Perception by 5 until the end of its next turn. If cast in dim light or darkness, the effect is automatically successful.
Modifier Details: reduces passive Perception by 5

Description: The caster conjures a small pile of golden sand in their palm. It has no magical properties but remains permanently damp. Scholars have debated its significance for centuries, yet it remains as pointless as ever. Additional Effects: nan
Component Description (Material): A scrap of parchment with gibberish

Description: Your feet become magnetized, securing you firmly to steel surfaces, providing enhanced stability against physical manipulation.- Effect:- You gain advantage on Strength saving throws and Strength (Athletics) checks to resist being shoved or knocked prone.- While the spell is active, you cannot be moved against your will by physical means unless the creature pushing you succeeds on a Strength (Athletics) check against your Strength saving throw DC.- The magnetic effect only works on steel or similarly magnetic surfaces (e.g., metal floors, steel grates, etc.).
Scaling:
- 5th Level: The spell grants advantage on all Dexterity saving throws to avoid falling prone or being knocked over by environmental hazards.
- 11th Level: The spell lasts for 10 minutes, and the magnetism spreads to nearby metal objects, allowing you to pull or manipulate small metal items within 10 feet of you as part of the spell’s effect.
Flavor: You feel the ground beneath you hum as the magic takes hold. A faint magnetic pull runs through the soles of your feet, anchoring you to the steel beneath you. You brace yourself, standing firm against the force trying to knock you over, as you become one with the steel underfoot. The pull grows stronger with every movement, keeping you solidly in place.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on Strength saving throws

Description:
You perform a questionable and extremely localized reality shift, transforming a humanoid creature into a very aggressive rooster (or vice versa).
- If the target is willing, the transformation is automatic and lasts for the duration or until reversed.
- If the target is unwilling, they must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be transformed for the spell’s duration. They may repeat the save at the end of each of their turns.
- If the caster uses a Vitality and a Unique Offering blood pact the transformation becomes permanent unless reversed with Greater Restoration, Remove Curse, or Wish.
- A rooster (or chicken) transformed into a humanoid is always willing, if inexplicably smug about it.

Forms:
- A humanoid becomes a rooster (tiny beast, 1 HP, peck attack, high aggression, prone to crowing loudly).
- A rooster becomes a humanoid of the caster’s choice, with basic clothing and no memory of being a bird (but with lingering sass).

Reversing the Spell:
The caster may end the spell early as an action. Recasting the spell on the same target (while transformed) reverts them.
Clarification:
Rooster Traits:

- Crowing: Emits a deafening *COCK- A- DOODLE- DOO!* once per hour.
- Aggressive: Will charge anything larger than it.
- Peck: Melee Weapon Attack, +3 to hit, 1 tearing damage.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Constitution saving throw

Description:
You invoke a binding mandate acknowledged by the Loom and framed as lawful, proper, and procedurally justified. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it is compelled to carry out a simple directive such as delivering an item, withdrawing from an area, or ceasing a specific action.

Unlike ordinary enchantment, the creature rationalizes its obedience as correct and appropriate, believing the command aligns with established authority, duty, or obligation. If the directive contradicts the creature’s deeply held oaths, sworn duties, or recognized standing, the saving throw automatically succeeds. If no plausible justification exists within the world’s structures of authority, the mandate fails to take hold.

Repeated reliance on such mandates may draw unseen scrutiny, as patterns of imposed authority accumulate within the Loom and invite correction.
Anomaly Effect: - If overused, an invisible compliance officer (an unknown corporate entity) may begin tracking the caster’s "compliance history," leading to unexpected legal ramifications.
Modifier Details: target must make a Charisma saving throw
Component Description (Material): a printed contract, signed in ink

Description:
You issue a binding edict recognized by higher authority, and the Loom answers by manifesting a floating weapon of sanctioned force. The armament takes the form of a spectral implement of rule and enforcement, shaped like a massive sigil-blade, crushing seal, or serrated regnal device wrought from invisible weight and intent.

The weapon hovers in a space you choose and makes melee spell attacks against a creature within 5 feet of it. On a hit, it deals impact or tearing damage (your choice) equal to 1d8 + your spellcasting modifier. The armament moves and strikes with silent inevitability, as though enforcing a judgment already rendered.
Modifier Details: deals impact or tearing damage equal to 1d8 + your spellcasting modifier

Description:
The target must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 18). On a failure, they become paralyzed as their body hardens into wood, glass, or porcelain.
 
- They remain aware but cannot speak or move.
- Any attempt to remove the effect without breaking concentration requires a DC 20 Arcana check.
- If left in this state for the full duration, they remain a mannequin forever.
- Reversing it afterward require a spell that can bring back the dead by ithe time limit of such spell is ignored since the mannequin does not decay.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 18)
Component Description (Material): a doll with sewn- shut eyes

Description:
You overlay a false thread of legitimacy upon a creature, causing the Loom and Erudit registries to acknowledge them as properly recognized. For the duration, the target is treated as a sanctioned individual, accepted by seals of passage, ritual checks, and formal scrutiny.

The recognition is shallow. Under close interrogation, contradiction, or prolonged scrutiny, inconsistencies may surface, and observers may begin to question the authenticity of the imposed standing.

You forge an official document, digital signature, or authorization key that appears legitimate to casual inspection. A creature inspecting it closely must make an Investigation check against your spell save DC to recognize it as fake.
Modifier Details: a creature inspecting it closely must make an Investigation check against your spell save DC to reco

Foundry Tag: Requires Sight, Modifies AC, Uses Bonus Action, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Requires Sight, Modifies AC, Uses Bonus Action, Grants Advantage
Description:
A grinning slit opens across your stomach, chest, or throat—an extra mouth that was never meant to be there. The Maw of the Forgotten does not hunger for food, but for concepts, names, and existence itself.
 
- As a bonus action, you may attempt to devour an aspect of reality from a creature you can see within 10 feet. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure:
- They forget their own name for 24 hours.
