- Any titles, contracts, or legal records referring to them become unreadable, as though they were never written.
- They suffer disadvantage on all identity- based interactions, including deception and persuasion.
- Each time the Maw feeds, it grows larger, whispering secrets only you can hear.

 Ending the Spell:
- If the caster does not feed it at least once per minute, the Maw becomes hungry, forcing the caster to make an Intelligence saving throw or forget their own past.
- Once the spell ends, the mouth does not fully disappear—it remains as scar tissue, aching whenever something forgotten lingers nearby.
Anomaly Effect: - If cast on the same target twice, they may cease to exist entirely, their body remaining but their presence erased.
Modifier Details: target must make a Charisma saving throw
Component Description (Material): a swallowed tooth, not your own

Foundry Tag: Requires Sight, Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Creates Light
Unnamed: 5: Requires Sight, Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Creates Light
Description:
You conjure a radiant barrier that judges life at the moment of passage, offering mercy to some and ruin to others.

A luminous wall of living sigils manifests in a space you can see, humming softly like a great heart heard through stone. You name a single kind of being when the gate is formed, attuning it to that nature.
	•	Those who match the gate’s decree feel warmth, clarity, and renewal as they pass through, emerging bolstered and briefly fortified.
	•	All others feel the gate turn cold and hollow, siphoning vitality as they cross.

The gate accepts passage only in measured beats, becoming solid after each crossing before opening again, as though enforcing a rhythm that must not be rushed. Each transit weakens the gate’s resolve, and once it has judged enough souls, it dissolves into fading light.

To walk through it is like stepping across a line drawn by fate itself, one that decides whether you are meant to endure or be diminished.
Scaling:
At Higher Levels.

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the wall is 10 feet wider for each slot level above 2nd
Flavor: The light hum of the wall pulses like a heartbeat. Passing through feels like walking through sunlight after rain — warm, clear, and briefly perfect.
Modifier Details: those who match the gate's decree feel warmth, clarity, and renewal as they pass through, emerging b

Description: With a confident declaration of “I know a guy,” you tap into the power of connections and influence. For the next few moments, you can easily slip past social barriers, gaining a temporary edge in negotiations, or simply causing others to lower their guard. A subtle aura of calming energy surrounds the caster, creating an almost magical atmosphere of ease.- Effect:- The caster can bypass most social obstacles with ease, whether it’s convincing someone to lend you a hand, smoothing over a tense situation, or gaining access to a restricted area. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the target’s defenses are momentarily lowered, leaving them more susceptible to your influence.
Table:
| Roll | Side Effect Description |
|------ |------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | Nearby creatures become unusually philosophical, pondering the mysteries of the universe. |
| 2 | The caster’s presence hums softly, almost like a soothing melody. |
| 3 | A small cloud of smoke appears, lingering for a moment before dissipating. |
| 4 | Strange moths appear, fluttering around the caster or target. |
| 5 | Time seems to slow in the immediate vicinity, making everything feel more relaxed. |
| 6 | A ghostly voice whispers, “Chill,” calming the target even further. |
Scaling: - 5th Level: The duration of the influence extends to 1 minute, allowing for prolonged conversations or more complex social maneuvers.- 11th Level: You can target up to 3 creatures within range, with the same effects applying to all.
Modifier Details: target must make a Charisma saving throw

Description:
You draw a thin veil across recent suffering, allowing pain to pass without leaving a scar upon the thread. Choose up to five creatures within range. Each target forgets a single traumatic experience, moment of intense suffering, or emotionally searing event that occurred within the last 24 hours.

The memory is not erased violently. Instead, it is gently omitted from conscious recall, leaving behind only a vague sense of time passed or exhaustion endured. Emotional wounds tied directly to the forgotten moment ease accordingly, though deeper patterns of fear or habit may remain.

This effect does not dispel magical charm, domination, or ongoing supernatural influence. It merely releases the mind from the immediate weight of remembered pain, allowing the Loom to settle without tearing.

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Grants Advantage
Description:
You conjure an illusory, impossibly attractive individual tailored to a creature’s preferences—a glowing vision of flirtatious perfection. The phantom makes eye contact, winks, poses, and speaks with a sultry or suave voice. Only the target can hear its words.

When the illusion appears, all creatures within 10 feet of it must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, they are flustered until the start of their next turn, suffering disadvantage on Insight and Charisma (Persuasion or Deception) checks.

The phantom lingers for 1 minute, continuing to flirt—but vanishes immediately if the target accepts a date.

If the target refuses, ignores, or reacts negatively to the offer during the spell’s duration, they immediately gain 1 level of exhaustion as their heart and sleep are cursed with regret, longing, and dramatic dream interludes.

Restless Charm:

If the target accepts the date, the illusion disappears with a delighted gasp—but returns during their next long rest, whisking them away on a surreal magical outing.
- The long rest is interrupted.
- The target returns in the morning emotionally drained, gaining a level of exhaustion.
- Additionally, they suffer disadvantage on Wisdom saving throws until their next long rest, as reality feels disappointingly dull compared to their spectral suitor.

If the target refuses the date, the illusion returns every long rest until they accept, interrupting sleep and causing 1 level of exhaustion each time. The illusion is more dramatic every time it returns.

Upon the 6th refusal the illusion will sip the beauty and vitality from the target incuring in the death of the target.
Flavor: Some scholars argue the phantom is a low- grade romantic fey. Others think it’s just a magical thirst trap with abandonment issues.
Modifier Details: suffers disadvantage on Charisma saving throws

Description:
You form a sharpened shard of mental force—an arrow of intent, a spike of memory, or a piercing thought—and hurl it toward a creature within range.

Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 cognitive damage.

The shard is invisible to those without psychic sensitivity, leaving no physical trace. To mundane observers, the effect may resemble the target momentarily flinching or losing focus.

Thematic Delivery

You choose the form your psyslings take. Examples include:
- A crystalline memory dart
- A shimmering synaptic spike
- A phrase spoken backward that drills through thought
- A fractal glyph burned across the air

The number of shards you manifest increases as you gain levels.
Each shard can target the same or different creatures. You make a separate attack roll for each.
Table:
### Psysling Cantrip Progression

| Character Level | Damage | Number of Shards |
|----------------- |-------- |------------------ |
| 1st | 1d8 | 1 |
| 5th | 1d8 | 2 |
| 11th | 1d8 | 3 |
| 17th | 1d8 | 4 |
Modifier Details: make a ranged spell attack

Description:
You overload the thoughts of all creatures in range. They must make an Intelligence saving throw.
- On a failed save, they take 8d6 cognitive damage and are stunned until the end of their next turn as their thoughts collapse inward, echoing with names they shouldn’t know. 
- On a successful save, they take half damage and resist the mental collapse.
Scaling:
At Higher Levels. 
When cast using an 8th-level spell slot or higher, the radius of the detonation increases to 40 feet.
Flavor:
During the Calamity of Shrouded Stars, Mindspike Detonation was unleashed upon a battlefield, reducing an entire legion to nameless husks. Those who survived swore they heard their own names whispered by the void.

Spellcasters who use Mindspike Detonation are marked—subtly at first. Their reflections blink at the wrong times, their shadows do not always align with their bodies.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher:
A chorus of whispered names pulses outward. For 1 minute, your voice overlaps with an unknown one, slightly out of sync.
Modifier Details: must make an Intelligence saving throw
Component Description (Material): A fragment of obsidian implanted in the skin

Description: You press your lips against the sleeping target, whispering a glamour that blurs the truth and smooths away inconvenient memories.- The target forgets up to 10 minutes per caster level from the past 24 hours. You choose which moments are erased.- The spell only works on unconscious or willingly sleeping creatures.- If this spell is cast on the same target more than once within a week, their memory begins to unravel:- They suffer disadvantage on Intelligence checks for 24 hours,- And 1 level of exhaustion if they fail a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw.
Flavor: Those who cross Mirabel rarely remember why they fear her. Only that something precious was stolen while they slept.
Modifier Details: fails a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You press a lingering, soul- bound kiss onto the target—searing an immortal affliction into their very being. The kiss binds them not to life, but to endless suffering.
- The target contracts a disease or madness of your choice (DM discretion or chosen from existing rules).
- They cannot die by any means—not from wounds, fire, aging, poison, or deprivation.
- When reduced to 0 hit points, the target falls into a state of paralyzed consciousness: aware, feeling pain, but unable to move or speak.
- They do not regenerate, heal, or lose hit points unless healed or damaged magically.
- Only a Greater Restoration or Cure Disease cast by a Cleric of higher level than the caster can remove the affliction.
Flavor: Creatures cursed in this way have been found years—sometimes centuries—later, whispering forgotten apologies into the void.
Modifier Details: only a Greater Restoration or Cure Disease cast by a Cleric of higher level than the caster can remo
Component Description (Material): a vial of black salt, dissolved in a lover’s tear

Description:
A brief biometric transfer stimulates the target’s central nervous system, instantly eliminating exhaustion, hangovers, and sleep deprivation.

- The target gains the benefits of a long rest (except for spell slot recovery).
- Their next Constitution saving throw gains advantage.
 
 Often employed by high- level executives and underground performance- enhancing specialists.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on their next Constitution saving throw

Description: You cause a single nonmagical item no larger than a book to appear misplaced or absent to those unfamiliar with it. For the duration, creatures other than you must succeed on an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to notice the object. This effect ends if the item is touched directly.
Modifier Details: creatures other than you must succeed on an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell sa

Description:
You tangle the creature’s passage through the Loom, scattering false impressions and contradictory threads in its wake.

For the duration, all attempts to track, locate, or divine the target’s position or identity produce misleading or fabricated results. Scrying, telepathic pursuit, omen-reading, and similar effects slide past echoes instead of the true thread.

If a creature is already following the target when this spell is cast, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or lose the trail entirely, convinced it has reached a dead end or false conclusion.

At higher invocations, the Veil may be widened to cloak an entire group, their shared passage dissolving into overlapping false routes.
Scaling:
- At 5th level, the obfuscation lasts 8 hours.
- At 7th level, the effect extends to an entire group (up to 6 creatures).
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or lose the trail entirely

Description:
You induce a momentary flaw in the Erelian Erudits’ registries, causing a document, card, or written record within range to appear subtly misaligned with its proper place in the Loom. Labels blur, markings seem inconsistent, and the record feels faintly out of order.

Any creature attempting to reference or rely upon the affected record must succeed on an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to notice the inconsistency. The disturbance is fragile. Once examined closely or deliberately scrutinized, the misthread resolves and the record settles back into its proper form.
Modifier Details: any creature attempting to reference or rely upon the affected record must succeed on an Intelligenc

Description:
You fortify the mind of a creature against forced memory alterations, cognitive tampering, and external data manipulation.
 
- The target becomes immune to Memory Fragmentation, A.S.T.R.A.L. Redaction, and similar effects.
- Any attempt to modify their memory fails outright, and the caster of the effect must make a Wisdom saving throw or suffer 5d8 cognitive damage.
- The target also remembers everything perfectly for 24 hours, with a near- photographic recall of events.
Scaling: - At 9th level, this effect becomes permanent unless dispelled.
Modifier Details: suffers 5d8 cognitive damage
Component Description (Material): a glass bead filled with silver ink

Description: Target one creature you can see within range. It must make a Wisdom saving throw or forget the last 10 minutes of its memory. The effect is permanent unless restored by greater restoration, a cognitive anomaly, or direct exposure to the same event again.

You call in a debt from the shadow economy of the city, invoking an unspoken pact older than law. A hardened enforcer manifests nearby, broad-shouldered and scarred, wearing a long coat heavy with stitched sigils of obligation. Gold rings mark their fingers. Their eyes weigh you like currency.

They say nothing at first. Then, after a pause: “You paid. So I’m here.”

The enforcer is a capable but self-interested combatant. They fight efficiently, without heroics, and without mercy. Their loyalty is purely transactional, bound by coin and the invisible ledgers of the city’s underworld.

The Gilded Enforcer
	•	Acts once in combat, immediately after your turn
	•	Makes melee spell attacks, dealing 1d6 + your Charisma modifier impact damage
	•	Prioritizes protecting you or nearby allies, but will not sacrifice themselves
	•	If reduced to half their hit points, they begin fighting defensively

Coinbound Quirks
	1.	Refers to the caster as “credit”, “asset”, or “investment”
	2.	Casually references unnamed debts, favors, or broken oaths
	3.	Will threaten, maim, or intimidate enemies without hesitation
	4.	Shows no interest in ideology, morality, or long-term plans
	5.	Vanishes once the spell ends, leaving behind the faint impression that something is still owed

Additional Effects
	•	The enforcer demands payment in gold or valuables upon manifestation
	•	If unpaid or shorted, the GM may introduce future complications tied to reputation, lost favors, or shadow reprisals
	•	When the spell ends, the enforcer disappears silently, as if folded back into the city itself, leaving behind a sense of unresolved obligation

This is not a summoning.
It is a transaction.
Modifier Details: deals 1d6 + Charisma modifier impact damage

Description: You slam your foot down onto the ground, sending a controlled shockwave rippling through the asphalt, knocking foes back and disrupting their balance.- Saving Throw: Each creature within 5 feet of you must make a Strength saving throw.- On a failed save, the creature is pushed 5 feet away from you.- On a successful save, the creature remains unaffected by the shockwave.- Additionally, any creature that fails the saving throw takes 1d6 sound damage as the shockwave disturbs their equilibrium.
Scaling: - 5th Level: The push distance increases to 10 feet, and the damage increases to 2d6.- 11th Level: The push distance increases to 15 feet, and the damage increases to 3d6. The radius of the shockwave also increases to 10 feet.
Flavor: You slam your foot onto the solid asphalt beneath you, a tremor spreading out in all directions. The ground rumbles briefly before an invisible wave of force ripples outward, sending those nearby stumbling backward, unbalanced by the shock. The sound of the wave echoes briefly in the air, leaving a brief ringing in the ears of those it affects.
Modifier Details: each creature within 5 feet of you must make a Strength saving throw

Description: A small, glowing orb resembling a moth flutters within range, pulsing dim light in a 10- foot radius. The orb moves slowly (10 feet per round) in a direction of your choice. If it enters a creature’s space, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw or be briefly mesmerized, reducing its movement speed by half until the start of its next turn.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw

Description:
You whisper defiant syllables into the Weave, anchoring a sphere of uncooperative momentum into the world. Within the zone, tension hangs in the air—every movement feels optional, replaceable, or rebellious.

While within the sphere, each creature may choose at the start of their turn:
- Forego their movement speed for the turn to gain one of the following effects until the start of their next turn:
- +1 bonus to AC
- One additional weapon attack as part of the Attack action
- Advantage on the next saving throw they make

These choices must be made verbally—even whispered—causing faint echoes to ripple in the air.
Flavor:
A beggar in Concord once claimed to be a king undone by this spell. When asked for details, his mouth moved, but no sound emerged.
Those who cast Muttering Solstice of Indignation say it never feels the same way twice. Each use leaves a trace—on the caster, on the ground, on the timeline.
Anomaly Effect:
When you cast this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher, reality trembles slightly:

For the next minute, illusory duplicates of the caster flicker into being around them, mirroring their movements with a one- second delay. They offer no mechanical benefit—but are extremely unsettling.
Modifier Details: +1 bonus to ac
Area: Sphere/15 ft (centered on a point you can see)
Component Description (Material): a piece of a fence post

Description:
You conjure a small, ancient mussel in your hand—enchanted, annoyed, and tightly sealed shut. If anyone tries to pry it open, it vanishes with a faint salty pop, leaving behind only a briny scent.

If spoken to gently and respectfully, the mussel may whisper a cryptic phrase in an ancient tongue or forgotten accent. These utterances are rarely useful and often nonsense, though occasionally they align with bizarre truths.

Once conjured, the mussel lasts for 1 minute or until it disappears. It may be placed on surfaces, used as a very passive familiar, or simply admired.
Table:
| d6 | Whispered Phrase |
|---- |------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | The seventh bell tolls sideways. |
| 2 | Ask the chimney, it remembers. |
| 3 | You were almost someone else. |
| 4 | Don’t trust the paperclip. |
| 5 | Salt forgets, but pepper never forgives. |
| 6 | It’s already behind you. |
Component Description (Material): A scrap of parchment with gibberish

Description:
You summon choking fumes that blur vision.
- A 10- foot- radius sphere becomes heavily obscured.
- Creatures inside must succeed a Constitution save or have disadvantage on attacks and lose reactions for 1 round.
- The area smells faintly of burnt plastic and rust.
Modifier Details: must succeed a Constitution save

Description:
You draw a thin veil between a creature and its own sensations, allowing pain, fear, and emotional shock to arrive late rather than all at once. For a brief time, the body moves while the mind waits.

The target continues to act as though unharmed, unafraid, and unburdened, their reactions eerily calm and delayed. Heat does not burn yet, poison does not sting yet, and terror feels distant, muffled, and unreal. Observers may notice the target moving with unsettling detachment, as if their spirit has stepped half a pace behind their flesh.

When the veil collapses, all postponed sensations rush back in a single overwhelming wave, leaving the creature reeling as their body and mind struggle to reconcile what was ignored.
Scaling: At Higher Levels. When cast using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you may target one additional creature for each slot level above 2nd.
Modifier Details: no postponed sensations rush back in a single overwhelming wave, leaving the creature reeling as the

Description: You exist in two places simultaneously, shifting between locations each round. You can take actions from either location, but your form is flickering and unpredictable. Any attempt to track, capture, or observe you results in incomplete or contradictory reports.
Modifier Details: your form is flickering and unpredictable

Description:
You issue a sanctioned directive that targets the body itself—its fluids, its functions, its very architecture. The spell sends a binding sigil through the bloodstream of one creature within range, turning their own vital systems against them.

The target must make a Constitution saving throw.

- On a failure, their blood ignites with administrative intent, warping to fulfill the directive. They take 10d10 biologic damage, and for the duration, they are affected by Internal Rebellion (see below).
- On a success, the target takes half damage and is not otherwise affected.

Internal Rebellion: 
For the duration of the spell, the creature suffers the following effects:
- Hemorrhagic Pulses: At the start of each of their turns, roll 1d6. On a 1–3, they take 3d6 biologic damage as their blood attempts to exit through eyes, ears, and pores. 
- Command Override: The caster may use a bonus action on their turn to force the target to use half their movement in a direction of the caster’s choosing, as their own muscle tissue spasms against them. 
- Hostile System Rejection: If the target attempts to cast a spell or take a reaction, they must first succeed on a Constitution saving throw or the action fails and they take 2d6 psychic damage.

If this spell reduces a creature to 0 hit points, their body implodes into mist and splinters of bone, leaving behind no remains.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw
Component Description (Material): a black file folder stamped “TOP SECRET”

Description: You mark a creature as obsolete, and reality enforces the correction. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes 10d10 impact damage and suffers disadvantage on all attack rolls and ability checks for 1 minute. If the creature has legendary resistances, it must expend one to automatically succeed.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw

Description: You cause a dazzling flash of light to reflect off a nearby surface, like polished marble, momentarily blinding and disorienting any ranged attacker.- Saving Throw: Each creature within a 30- foot radius who makes a ranged attack on their turn must make a Constitution saving throw.- On a failed save, the creature suffers disadvantage on their next ranged attack.- On a successful save, they are unaffected by the dazzling light.
Scaling:
- 5th Level: The radius of the effect increases to 40 feet, and the disadvantage lasts for 1 additional round.
- 11th Level: The radius of the effect increases to 50 feet, and creatures that fail their saving throw are also blinded until the end of their next turn.
Flavor: The marble gleams brightly in the light as you invoke the magic, reflecting the sunlight or torchlight in an intense flash. The sudden burst causes ranged attackers to squint, their aim faltering as the brilliance momentarily overwhelms their senses. Those who fail to resist the burst of light find themselves momentarily disoriented, struggling to regain focus before releasing their projectile.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Creates Light
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Creates Light
Description:
You conjure a glowing, humming orb of soft pastel light that floats 5 feet off the ground and emits gentle music and phrases like:- “Let’s not do this.”- “Violence is exhausting.”- “Hugs not hits.”- “Come on, talk it out, champ.”

Each creature of your choice within a 10- foot radius of the sphere must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become non- hostile for as long as they remain within the sphere’s area.

They won’t initiate attacks, cast offensive spells, or shout mean things while inside—but they may still pout, sulk, or engage in passive aggression.

The Save DC is equal to: 10 + the creature’s Challenge Rating (rounded down)
This means tougher creatures are less likely to resist the sphere’s charm, because even ancient dragons want to be understood.

While active:- The sphere follows its original position and cannot be moved.- Creatures who exit and re- enter must re- roll their save.- Creatures who are damaged immediately shake off the effect.
Table:
| d6 | The Sphere says... |
|---- |--------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | "Let’s take a deep breath." |
| 2 | "I’m hearing a lot of anger right now." |
| 3 | "Let’s find common ground... maybe brunch?" |
| 4 | "It’s not about winning." |
| 5 | "I’m on your side. All of your sides." |
| 6 | *Plays gentle ukulele chords and sighs* |
Modifier Details: DC is equal to: 10 + the creature’s Challenge Rating (rounded down)

Description: You channel the corrosive forces of decay into your strike, touching a creature’s armor or weapon with a touch imbued with necrotic energy. The target suffers both chiaric damage and the physical degradation of their gear.- Melee spell attack: Make a melee spell attack against a target. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 chiaric damage.- Armor Degradation: If the target is wearing armor, their AC is reduced by 1 for 1 minute due to rust and corrosion weakening their armor. This reduction stacks with other effects.- Weapon Degradation: If the target is wielding a weapon, it becomes slightly dulled, imposing disadvantage on attack rolls with that weapon until it is repaired or restored.
Scaling: - 5th level: The damage increases to 2d6 necrotic damage, and the AC reduction lasts for 10 minutes.- 11th level: The damage increases to 3d6 necrotic damage, and the AC reduction is applied to all armor worn by the target, including shields.
Flavor: As you reach out with a swipe or strike, you feel the cold, creeping touch of decay course through your hand, an invisible corrosive aura eating away at the target’s defenses. Where you touch, rust and rot spread in an instant, weakening armor and weapon alike. Your victim recoils as the rust spreads across their equipment, their movements becoming sluggish and uncertain.
Modifier Details: reduces AC by 1 for 1 minute

Description: You create a faint, distorted trace of someone who recently handled an object. A ghostly outline of their hands or footprints lingers faintly, fading slowly over the duration. This effect does not reveal the person’s identity but may suggest their presence.
Component Description (Material): a strand of hair or a fingerprint

Description:
You designate an area as both sanctified and desecrated for 5 rounds. It counts as both types of terrain simultaneously.

While active:- All Sacred Footing and Aura of Sanctity benefits for both Holy and Unholy terrain are active.- Enemies within the area must make a Wisdom saving throw or suffer from Paranoia (disadvantage on Insight, Investigation, and saving throws against fear effects).- You may cast spells that require either Holy or Unholy terrain as though the condition is met.

This creates strange divine interference, and at the start of your turn while concentrating on it, roll a d6:- 1–2: Radiant burst (each creature takes 1d6 radiant)- 3–4: Shadow pulse (each creature heals 1 HP if undead)- 5–6: No extra effect
Modifier Details: enemies within the area must make a Wisdom saving throw or suffer from Paranoia

Description: You leave behind a temporal echo of yourself, a fractured imprint repeating your most recent motion as if caught between moments. The echo is visually indistinguishable from you and can be interacted with, but it cannot act independently. If the echo is struck, it ruptures into a burst of destabilized thought, dealing 6d6 cognitive damage to all creatures within 10 feet. Even after the echo fades, sensitive observers may sense that time was briefly bent.
Modifier Details: deals 6d6 cognitive damage
Acquisition Note: Entities sensitive to time distortions may notice anomalies lingering after the spell ends.

Description: You consign a creature to an infinite cycle of royal petition and review, trapping their mind in an unresolvable appeal before unseen authority. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they are unable to act for 1d10 days, convinced they are endlessly submitting requests, attending hearings, and awaiting judgments that never arrive. To the victim, time appears to pass normally, yet every attempt to act is deferred to a higher review that is always pending.
Modifier Details: target must make a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You rewrite the body’s present state to match its intended form. The target regains all hit points and has all lost limbs and organs fully restored.

If the creature is suffering from mutation, deformity, or anomalous alteration, the spell attempts to return the body to its original, uncorrupted state, ending such effects where possible. This restoration does not resurrect the dead but fully repairs the living.
Component Description (Material): a medical report detailing the target’s injuries

Description: You leave a lingering cognitive echo in an object. At any point during the duration, you may mentally transmit a single message (up to 25 words) that will be heard by anyone within 30 feet of the object as if it were ringing.

You do not just alter a document—you bend reality itself to accept the forgery as true for a short period of time.

- When you modify a ledger, contract, record, or file, the spell imprints its existence into the fabric of the world, making it temporarily irrefutable.
- For the next 10 minutes, any creature reading or interacting with the altered document will believe it is correct, unless they succeed on a DC 18 Intelligence (Investigation) check.
- Even memories are briefly affected—a guard whose records have been modified will recall seeing you enter.
- After 10 minutes, the false record vanishes, and reality attempts to correct itself, leaving behind only confusion and a lingering sense that something wasn’t quite right.
Modifier Details: DC 18 Intelligence (Investigation) check to disbelieve the altered document

Description: You momentarily shift into a liminal state, allowing you to step through solid objects no thicker than 5 feet. If you would end your movement inside an object, you are ejected to the nearest open space, taking 1d8 impact damage.
Modifier Details: takes 1d8 impact damage

Description:
This spell reconstructs the ambient sensations, whispers, and tactile imprints of previous intimate engagements within a location.
 
While harmless, the effect warps local reality, causing phantom touches, faint breathing, and glimpses of past moments that linger in the space. This spell is illegally used in corporate surveillance and high- end brothel atmospherics.
 
- A creature charmed by you automatically fails the saving throw for this spell.
Scaling: At higher levels, this spell reveals actual words spoken during the previous encounter (3rd-level slot).

Foundry Tag: Requires Sight, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Requires Sight, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Description:
You gesture subtly, and a phantasmal breeze whispers through an area you can see within range. Curtains billow, candles flicker, papers shift, and cold air brushes the skin. Though entirely illusory, the sensation feels real—enough to distract, unnerve, or suggest a presence unseen.- The wind cannot cause harm or move objects heavier than 5 lbs.- Creatures within the area instinctively glance around. If they are unaware of you or expecting stealth, you may gain advantage on your next Charisma (Deception or Performance) or Dexterity (Stealth) check against them before the end of your next turn.

This 0-Cantrip is often used to haunt, mislead, or emphasize one’s words, and is popular among illusionists, tricksters, and dramatists alike.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on Charisma (Deception or Performance) or Dexterity (Stealth) check

Description:
The target’s body crackles with latent electrical energy, empowering their strikes. For the duration of the spell, the target deals 1d8 additional electric damage with weapon attacks and unarmed strikes.

Furthermore, if the target takes electric damage while this spell is active, they absorb the energy, regaining hit points equal to half the damage taken.
Modifier Details: deals 1d8 additional electric damage with weapon attacks and unarmed strikes

Description:
You speak a single, forbidden syllable: A sonic anomaly that cuts beneath thought, identity, and social constraint. The moment it leaves your lips, the word spreads like an invisible shockwave, slipping past armor, modesty, and mental fortitude.

Every creature within 60 feet of you who can hear the word, including yourself, must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be compelled to immediately strip all worn items, including clothing, armor, uniforms, accessories, magical attire, and identity- linked gear.

Affected creatures drop whatever they are holding and use their next movement and action (if able) to completely disrobe, regardless of current danger, weather, or social context. The compulsion lasts until the creature has removed all items or is incapacitated.

- The caster is not immune.
- Creatures immune to being charmed are unaffected.
- Deafened creatures or those in soundproof areas are unaffected.
- Constructs or creatures that do not wear gear are unaffected.
- If a creature removes magical protective gear, their AC may drop, and spell effects tied to those items end as usual.

Additional Effects
In addition to its physical effect, the power word unravels falsehoods.
- All illusions, glamers, disguises, and non- permanent magical concealments affecting creatures in range are immediately dispelled, as if targeted by dispel magic.
- Creatures under effects such as disguise self, invisibility, or illusion- based shapeshifting are revealed in their true forms for 1 minute.
Flavor: In Concord City, this spell is classified as a Class- 7 Disruption Event by A.S.T.R.A.L. It was banned after the Incident at the Ivory Banquet, where two boardrooms and an entire opera house experienced simultaneous disrobing. The name of the word remains unrecorded. Those who try to repeat it often bite through their tongues.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description: Your vision sharpens, highlighting enemy weak points. During the spell's duration, once per turn, your firearm attacks deal an additional 1d6 damage. Additionally, you ignore penalties from long- range firing and half- cover.
Scaling: Damage +1d6 per 2 levels above 2nd
Modifier Details: additional 1d6 damage

Description: Nonmagical food and drink within range are protected from spoilage and contamination for the duration. The spell affects a quantity of supplies sufficient for up to ten creatures.

You unleash a pulse of pure polarity—light and shadow intertwined and tearing at each other with violent intensity. This unstable radiance converges on a creature within range, attempting to shear their essence between illumination and oblivion.

The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 12d10 chiaric damage, pulled between the blinding burn of light and the consuming void of shadow. On a success, they take half damage.

If the target is reduced to 0 hit points by this spell, their form is torn into two halves—one vanishing in a beam of searing light, the other collapsing into inky residue.
Flavor: Some believe Prism Shear is not a spell at all, but a fragment of a failed cosmic division, invoked and weaponized by mortals who never should have touched it.
Modifier Details: Constitution saving throw

Description:
You seize a creature’s voice and turn it into a vessel for imposed declaration. Choose one creature within range. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it is compelled to immediately speak a declarative statement of your choosing, delivered with formal conviction as though the words were required of them by duty or oath.

While speaking, the creature rationalizes the proclamation as necessary and justified, believing the act to be appropriate rather than coerced. If the statement is factually untrue, the creature may attempt a Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns to break free of the compulsion.

If you bear visible symbols of authority, ritual office, or recognized legitimacy, the target has disadvantage on its initial saving throw, as the borrowed voice yields more readily to perceived mandate.
Anomaly Effect: - If overused, the spell may trigger an automated audit, requiring the caster to justify their actions to an unknown, bureaucratic force.
Modifier Details: has disadvantage on its initial saving throw

Description:
You conjure a shimmering blade of psychic energy in your hand, forged from your focused will. Choose any melee weapon form you are proficient with (no ammunition or thrown weapons). The Psyblade takes that shape but is weightless, emits faint mental whispers, and deals force damage instead of its usual type.

You can use your Wisdom modifier instead of Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls with this weapon.

The damage die starts at 1d4 and improves as you gain Psionic levels, following the Monk’s Martial Arts die:
Clarification:
Additional Properties:
- The blade vanishes if it leaves your hand, if you cast this spell again, or if you dismiss it (no action required).
- The Psyblade is magical, counts as a spellcasting focus, and can be used as the delivery method for Psionic Cantrips.
- When you take the Attack action, you may forgo one attack to cast a Psionic cantrip through the blade, targeting the same creature.
Table:
### Psyblade Damage Die Progression

| Psionic Level | Psyblade Damage Die |
|--------------- |---------------------- |
| 1–4 | 1d4 |
| 5–10 | 1d6 |
| 11–16 | 1d8 |
| 17+ | 1d10 |
Scaling:
As you level up, class features may allow you to:
- Infuse the Psyblade with lingering cantrip effects
- Channel spells as reactions or bonus actions
- Teleport with the blade
- Split the blade into dual- wielding versions
- Imprint psychic marks or leave instability in struck targets
Modifier Details: can use Wisdom modifier instead of Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls

Description:
You release a surge of distorted Still Time that fractures the instant of impact. Creatures caught in the area suffer 6d10 cognitive damage as their perception and motion fall out of alignment. Each creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, its thread slips slightly out of phase with the present moment, becoming Displaced and suffering disadvantage on attack rolls and Dexterity saving throws until the end of its next turn.

When invoked at higher power, the Still Time distortion deepens, increasing the damage as the moment is forced further out of sync.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw
Arcane Strain: Moderate Arcane Strain – Minor temporal instability, brief perception shifts, or momentary déjà vu effects.

Description: You place your hand near the target’s throat, heart, or the air above their skin—never touching, yet always too close. With a whispered invocation, you invite the attention of something that drinks the breath between pulses.- The target loses 2 hit points per round for the duration.- The caster gains 1 hit point per round (up to their maximum).- If the target is reduced to 0 hit points this way, they do not fall unconscious immediately.- For 1 round, they remain aware but paralyzed, their vision darkening as they feel a cold, unseen presence leaning in, whispering promises only they can hear.- At the start of their next turn, they fall unconscious unless stabilized by magical means.
Scaling:
At Higher Levels.
When cast using a 4th-level spell slot or higher, increase the damage by 1 HP per round and the healing by 1 HP per round for every level above 3rd.
Modifier Details: target loses 2 hit points per round

Description:
You summon a chunk of stone or brick from the earth beneath you, hurling it toward a target with magical force. The rocky projectile strikes with an explosive impact.- Ranged spell attack: Make a ranged spell attack against a target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 impact damage from the stone.- The target must make a Strength saving throw (DC equals your spellcasting ability modifier) or be knocked prone by the force of the impact.

- 5th level: The damage increases to 2d8 impact, and the range of the spell increases to 60 feet.- 11th level: The spell deals 3d8 impact damage, and the target is stunned until the end of their next turn on a failed saving throw.
Scaling: - 5th level: The damage increases to 2d8 bludgeoning, and the range of the spell increases to 60 feet.- 11th level: The spell deals 3d8 impact damage, and the target is stunned until the end of their next turn on a failed saving throw.
Flavor: You kneel, tapping the earth beneath you, and with a surge of magical force, rip a jagged brick or cobblestone from the ground. With a swift motion, you hurl it at your foe, the stone sailing through the air with unnatural speed and power. The target is struck with the weight and force of the earth itself.
Modifier Details: target must make a Strength saving throw (DC equals your spellcasting ability modifier) or be knocke

Description:
You tear open a wound in the flow of pyric force, releasing a violent burst of living heat. The rupture blooms outward in a sudden flare, scorching flesh and stone alike.

Those within the blast feel fire without flame, a burning that strikes faster than pain can form. Surfaces blacken, warp, or crack as the heat surges through them, leaving the area scorched and humming with fading warmth.
Modifier Details: deals fire damage without flame

Description: You call upon divine radiance to flare from the gilded surface of the marble beneath you. The bright light sears those standing within its glow, casting shadows in the wake of its divine fury.- Creatures within 10 feet of the gilded marble or any surface you choose to target must make a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 chiaric damage.- Creatures that fail their saving throw are also blinded until the end of their next turn, as the radiant light temporarily overwhelms their vision.
Scaling: - 5th level: The damage increases to 2d6 radiant, and the radius of the effect expands to 15 feet.- 11th level: The radius increases to 20 feet, and creatures that fail the saving throw are also paralyzed until the end of their next turn.
Flavor: You place the gold- plated coin on the floor, and radiant beams burst from the gilded marble surface, creating an intense, divine shine that floods the area. Enemies caught in its glow feel the burning light sear their skin, and their vision is blinded by the brilliance.
Modifier Details: take 1d6 chiaric damage

Description:
You invoke a catastrophic descent of raw pyric power, calling down Ragna’s fury in an uncontrolled discharge that ruptures the world’s tolerance for heat and force.

The eruption scours everything within its reach, overwhelming defenses and unraveling protective workings as blazing energy floods the area. When the light fades, the ground remains hostile and poisoned with lingering heat, a scar upon the land that warns all who approach that the fire has not finished remembering.
Scaling: - At 9th level, the explosion becomes a chain reaction, affecting an additional 30- foot- radius area every round for 3 rounds.
Modifier Details: overwhelms defenses

Description:
You awaken the latent fury sleeping within worked metal, calling a violent spark along iron veins, rails, or bound steel nearby. The metal answers with a sudden crack of lightning, leaping outward to punish those too close.

Creatures near the charged metal suffer the bite of the spark, and those clad in steel or gripping iron draw the wrath more fiercely, as the metal itself conspires to betray them. The air snaps and hums in the aftermath, carrying the scent of scorched stone and ringing iron.
Scaling: You channel a burst of static energy through nearby metal tracks, causing them to discharge a violent arc of electricity. The electric current surges through the rails, shocking any creature in proximity.- Creatures within 10 feet of any metal track or rail you target must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 electric damage.- If the target is wearing metal armor or wielding metal weapons, they take an additional 1d6 electric damage due to the conductive properties of the metal.
Flavor: You press your palm to the rail and a crackling surge of electricity courses through the metal, causing the nearby train tracks to hum with power. A jolt of lightning arcs from the tracks, discharging across the area and shocking any creatures foolish enough to be standing nearby. The sound of crackling energy fills the air as static builds.
Modifier Details: Creatures near the charged metal suffer the bite of the spark

Description: You briefly unshackle motion from hesitation. For the duration, you ignore the loading property of ranged weapons you wield, as your hands reset bows, mechanisms, or thrown implements with impossible speed. While this spell persists, you also gain advantage on initiative rolls, as your body reacts before thought can slow it.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on initiative rolls

Description:
You exist half inside reality, half outside of it. While active:
- You can pass through walls and barriers as long as they are not magical.
- You gain resistance to all physical damage.
- You leave behind a blurred, static afterimage that lingers for 6 seconds before catching up.
Modifier Details: gains resistance to all physical damage
Acquisition Note: The containment sigil must be notarized at an A.S.T.R.A.L.- approved office, costing 2,500 credits. Unauthorized usage may result in legal fines or containment nullification.

Description: You restore a destroyed or corrupted location (up to 1,000 square feet) to a prior state. This includes buildings, roads, and technological structures—but not living creatures. The restored state must have existed within the last 100 years, and anomalies within the area may resist reconstruction.

You invoke a sanctioned accord that authorizes a soul’s return from Morena’s domain. Choose one creature. If a binding covenant or recognized claim exists permitting the creature’s return, its thread is retrieved and rewoven, restoring it to life regardless of how long it has been dead.

If no such accord exists, the Loom refuses the request and the spell fails without effect. The magic does not bargain or plead. It simply checks whether return has been permitted, and acts accordingly.

You conjure a sealed, unearthly chamber, where indulgence flows like silk and no outside force may intrude.

- The 15- foot- diameter space is soundproof, protected from weather, and forbiddingly private.
- The caster can shape the interior at will, conjuring cushions, incense, and music that hums from the walls themselves.
- Those who rest inside awaken feeling like time itself curled around them, leaving them untouched.
Component Description (Material): a satin ribbon, bound with a secret never spoken aloud

Description:
Your form fractures slightly from this world, your outline flickering and bending at unnatural angles. When injured, reality itself offers you an escape—but not always a reliable one.

While this spell is active, each time you take damage, you may immediately teleport up to 20 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. This reaction does not cost an action or reaction.

You must have line of sight to teleport intentionally. If no valid space exists, the teleportation fails.

While this spell is active, you cast no reflection on reflective surfaces
Scaling:
At Higher Levels.
When cast using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, your controlled teleportation range increases to 30 feet.
Flavor:
In the city of Concord, Unstable Reality Surge is outlawed, for it was once used by the cult of the Silent Maw to unmake a senator in broad daylight. Witnesses swore they saw his shadow scream before vanishing entirely.

Those who cast Unstable Reality Surge often find that they dream differently afterward. They dream of corridors that stretch into infinity, doors that lead to places older than time.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher:
Reality trembles slightly, and for the next minute, shadows of your previous movements flicker half a second behind you. Enemies have disadvantage on opportunity attacks against you.
Component Description (Material): A shard of a broken mirror

Description:
When an external force attempts to bind your will, you invoke a writ of refusal anchored in Regal authority. You immediately reroll the saving throw against any effect that would impose charm, compulsion, or enforced obedience.

On a successful save, all such effects upon you end instantly, and you become immune to further attempts of that nature for 1 minute. If the source of the coercion is within 30 feet, the backlash tears through their mind, dealing 4d6 cognitive damage as the Veins of Lament echo their overreach.

This writ stands in defiance of magical contracts, psychic domination, and any force that claims the right to command without rightful standing.
Modifier Details: rerolls the saving throw against any effect that would impose charm, compulsion, or enforced obedien
Trigger: when forced to obey an order magically

Description:
You inscribe a layered seal of authority upon an object, threshold, or designated space, warding it against unauthorized passage. The binding resists mundane interference, refuses forced entry, and denies traversal through the Veil unless the intruder overcomes the seal’s authority.

Attempts to bypass the cipher through strength, skill, or teleportation are met with resistance, requiring significant exertion or force of will. If the seal is placed upon a creature, it constrains movement and escape, holding the subject within sanctioned bounds until the binding is broken or dispelled.
Modifier Details: requires significant exertion or force of will to bypass
Modifier Fixed Value: significant exertion or force of will
Component Description (Material): a security keycard or anomaly tag

Description: You sheath a creature in an invisible mantle of sanctioned protection, as though they were acting under royal favor. For the duration, the target gains resistance to nonmagical impact, piercing, and tearing damage, and receives a +1 bonus to AC. The protection feels less like armor and more like the world itself yielding slightly around the bearer.
Modifier Details: +1 bonus to ac

Description:
You extend a measured boon of sanctioned vitality to up to four creatures within range. Each gains temporary resilience equal to 3d8 plus your spellcasting modifier and a +1 bonus to Constitution saving throws.

The endurance is artificial and exacting. When the effect ends, each creature must either rest for an hour or suffer one level of exhaustion as the borrowed stability withdraws and the body reckons with the imposed strain.
Modifier Details: +1 bonus to constitution saving

Description:
You inscribe a concealed seal of Regal authority into the environment, binding the surrounding space beneath sanctioned stillness. The seal is invisible to ordinary senses and can only be perceived through specialized divination or Erudit scrutiny.

When a creature first enters the warded area, it must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, its thread is halted and diverted into a suspended state of recognition, aware of its surroundings but unable to move, act, or communicate. The creature remains contained until presented with a valid sigil of release, bound at the time of casting to you or a designated bearer.

Creatures that succeed on the saving throw pass through unhindered, though the seal remains dormant until triggered. Once activated, the seal cannot detain additional creatures.

Repeated use of such seals within the same region risks attracting higher Regal scrutiny, which may cause the seal to misapply its authority, detaining an unintended subject or manifesting a bureaucratic anomaly of Still Time.
Anomaly Effect: If the spell is cast too frequently within the same region, higher bureaucratic forces may initiate a dispute over procedural irregularities. This has a 25% chance of causing the glyph to malfunction, possibly detaining the wrong creature or triggering a random bureaucratic anomaly.
Modifier Details: creature must make a Wisdom saving throw
Component Description (Material): an embossed containment sigil, notarized and stamped with an A.S.T.R.A.L. seal
Acquisition Note: Official clearance required. Unauthorized use may lead to A.S.T.R.A.L. investigation.

Description:
You invoke a regent’s authority to halt the immediate unfolding of events. When a creature within range attempts an action, its execution is deferred by one full round as the moment is seized for review. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the action does not occur until the start of its next turn.

If the creature attempts to force the action through, it must expend both its bonus action and reaction struggling against the imposed intercession, accomplishing nothing in the process. Creatures accustomed to rigid hierarchies of command and obedience find the delay especially difficult to resist.
Modifier Details: target must make a Wisdom saving throw
Trigger: when a creature within range attempts an action

Description:
You assert the primacy of your own will against imposed dominion. When an external force attempts to bind, command, or observe you, this spell fractures the authority behind it.

Lesser bindings unravel instantly, their claims rendered void. Stronger compulsions find no easy hold, as your resolve hardens against them, granting you heightened resistance to all attempts to dominate or surveil your actions for a short time.
Modifier Details: grants heightened resistance to all attempts to dominate or surveil your actions
Trigger: when affected by an obedience or surveillance spell

Description:
You lift the emotional weight from a single cognitive affliction affecting a willing creature. Choose one of the following conditions currently affecting the target: Paranoid, Aroused, Frightened, Stunned, Compelled, or Charmed.

That condition immediately ends. The creature retains memory of the events that caused the condition, but the emotional and psychological burden associated with it is removed, allowing them to act without the influence of that trauma.

This spell does not remove physical conditions or non-cognitive effects.
Component Description (Material): a sealed envelope containing classified information

Description:
You temporarily erase all aromatic substances within a 50- foot radius centered on yourself. Foods instantly become tasteless, perfumes and deodorants lose their fragrance, and creatures develop noticeably unpleasant body odors or breath as natural smells dominate.

Creatures within the affected area become distracted by the sudden lack or distortion of familiar aromas. Any creature attempting Wisdom (Perception) or Wisdom (Insight) checks relying on smell within the spell’s radius does so at disadvantage.

When the spell ends, aromas slowly return to normal over the next minute.

Roleplay Suggestions:- This spell could disrupt diplomatic meetings or social gatherings.- Restaurants or perfumers may ban this spell outright, viewing it as destructive or offensive.

At Higher Levels (Scaling):- When cast using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the radius increases by 20 feet per spell level above 2nd.- At 5th level or higher, the spell can selectively erase stronger or more specific aromatics (such as poisons, toxins, or pheromones), possibly neutralizing their effects temporarily.
Flavor: A distorted A.S.T.R.A.L. directive warns about “the catastrophic consequences of categorical aromatic erasure.” The document abruptly glitches at the mention of “mint,” leaving you uncertain about the full implications of aroma removal.
Modifier Details: any creature attempting Wisdom (Perception) or Wisdom (Insight) checks relying on smell within the s

Description: The area is difficult terrain. At the start of each creature’s turn while inside the zone, it must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 4d10 impact damage on a failure. Teleportation and planar movement automatically fail within the fractured space.
Modifier Details: takes 4d10 impact damage on failure

Description:
Your body becomes partially unmoored from stable existence, flickering faintly between adjacent layers of the Veil. For the duration, you gain advantage on Dexterity saving throws.

Additionally, attack rolls against you are made with disadvantage unless the attacker possesses a means of perceiving true forms beyond illusion and distortion. Effects that rely on mundane sight or conventional perception struggle to track your shifting presence as reality fails to agree on where you fully are.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on Dexterity saving throws

Description:
You touch a surface and pull residual emotions, whispered words, and sensory echoes from its past. These are not spirits, but impressions left by heightened emotions—love, fear, guilt, rage.
 
- As long as you concentrate, you experience faded flashes of what transpired in the location over the last 24 hours (or up to 10 years for a higher- level cast).
- You may see blurred faces, hear indistinct voices, or feel the emotions of the past occupants.
- If a violent crime was committed here, the spell may temporarily create an echo, repeating the event as a flickering hallucination that others can witness.
Anomaly Effect: If overused in one place, the imprints might notice you back, becoming aware that they are being observed.
Component Description (Material): a glass shard from a broken window

Description:
You reach beyond the veil and call forth a flickering echo of a recently deceased creature. Instead of communicating directly, you witness fractured memories or emotional imprints they left behind.

When cast on a space where a humanoid died within the last 7 days, choose one of the following effects:

- Residual Vision. You and up to 5 willing creatures see a ghostly reenactment of the creature’s final moments, projected like a spectral recording. The scene is silent and incomplete, but may reveal useful visual details such as a killer's face, a hidden object, or a symbol drawn in blood.
 
- Lingering Instinct. You gain a skill or tool proficiency the deceased possessed (your choice, if known) for 10 minutes. You also gain advantage on Intelligence checks related to the creature's past profession or knowledge.

- Emotional Imprint. The space is saturated with the creature's strongest emotion at the time of death. For 10 minutes, anyone entering the space must succeed on a DC 13 Charisma saving throw or experience that emotion intensely (e.g., rage, fear, sorrow), which may affect social interactions, Insight, or Intimidation checks.

The echo fades once the chosen effect ends, or if dismissed early.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a DC 13 Charisma saving throw
Component Description (Material): a business card left behind by the deceased

Description: You focus the sound of your steps and amplify the resonant clatter of metal beneath you, creating a sharp, ringing clang that reverberates through the environment. The disruptive noise causes nearby creatures to lose focus and become disoriented.- Creatures within a 15- foot radius of the source must make a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 sound damage and have disadvantage on Perception checks for the next minute as they struggle to recover from the ringing.- The sound effects can be heard clearly through solid objects, and creatures within range must make a second saving throw if they are engaged in any concentration spells or abilities. On a failure, their concentration is broken.
Scaling: - 5th level: The radius increases to 30 feet, and the creatures now also suffer disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of their next turn, as their hearing and focus are affected by the noise.- 11th level: Creatures that fail their saving throw are also stunned for 1 round, as the sonic shockwave disorients them even further.
Flavor: You slam your feet against the metal grating, and the amplified clang reverberates across the battlefield. The sound is so intense it rattles the bones of your enemies, disorienting their senses and clouding their judgment. The metallic hum lingers in the air as they struggle to regain their composure.
Modifier Details: takes 1d6 sound damage

Description:
You draw lingering strain out of the target’s body and return it to the Weft, allowing the creature to stand renewed. The spell removes one level of exhaustion from the target, or two levels if cast at greater power.

If the target is unconscious or severely overworked, the sudden restoration also restores 4d8 hit points as breath, strength, and will are forcibly returned. The relief is immediate but not gentle, as vitality reclaimed too quickly always leaves a faint echo of strain behind.
Modifier Details: restores 4d8 hit points

Description:
You impose absolute order upon a creature whose existence has been bent, corrupted, or overridden. All effects that compel allegiance, dominate will, distort reality, or impose external control upon the target immediately end.

This spell removes all forms of magical mind control, possession, forced devotion, and imposed identity. Madness, paranoia, and supernatural trauma affecting the creature are fully cured. If the creature is possessed, overwritten, or controlled by another entity, that entity must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be violently expelled from the target at once.

When cast using a 9th-level spell slot, this effect instead applies to all willing creatures of your choice within a 30-foot radius.
Scaling: - At 9th level, this spell affects all willing creatures in a 30- foot radius.

Description:
You place your hand on a willing creature and channel a diagnostic pulse of divinatory energy. For a brief moment, their body becomes transparent to your senses, revealing hidden disruptions or irregularities within.

You detect the presence of any internal affliction, including:
- Natural or magical diseases
- Poisons or chemical imbalances
- Curses with a biological or physiological component
- Arcane mutations or lingering experimental side effects

You immediately learn the type of affliction present (e.g., “necrotic infection,” “alchemical toxin,” or “soul- linked corruption”). You do not learn the exact name or nature of the condition unless you succeed on a DC 13 Intelligence (Medicine or Arcana) check.

Creatures without any detectable affliction feel nothing. Afflicted creatures may feel a brief sting, or other harmless somatic response depending on the nature of their condition.

You tear open a sealed truth never meant to be carried by mortal thought and force it into the awareness of a creature within range. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, it takes 6d10 cognitive damage as forbidden understanding floods its mind and fractures its sense of self. For 1 minute, the creature suffers disadvantage on Intelligence and Charisma checks while its thoughts spiral around the implications of what it has learned.
On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and suffers no further effect. Some knowledge does not enlighten. It overwhelms.
Flavor:
> Some secrets are buried not to be forgotten, but to protect the minds of those too fragile to carry them 
> The truth doesn’t just set you free—it unravels you.
Modifier Details: target must make an Intelligence saving throw

Description: You attune your presence to the resonating echoes of the surrounding environment, causing your form to distort and flicker like a ripple in sound. In places with reflective or hollow surfaces, such as concrete tunnels or other echoing terrains, your form becomes harder to focus on, creating confusion and uncertainty.- The first attack against you each round has disadvantage, as your form fluctuates within the echoes, making it difficult for enemies to track your exact location.- While the spell lasts, you gain advantage on Stealth checks to hide in areas with echoing acoustics, such as tunnels, caves, or large, noisy chambers.
Scaling: - 5th level: The spell now grants advantage on saving throws against any abilities that would reveal your position or detect your presence, such as Divination or perception- based effects.- 11th level: Creatures within 10 feet of you that make an attack roll against you also take 1d4 sonic damage as they are disoriented by the reverberations.
Flavor: The sound around you warps, bending and distorting with each subtle movement. The rhythmic pulse of your steps creates ripples in the air, making it difficult for enemies to pinpoint you amidst the shifting echoes of the walls. In the oppressive silence of the concrete tunnels, your presence is felt, but never fully seen, and your enemies are left chasing after fleeting sounds, unaware of your true location.
Modifier Details: first attack against you each round has disadvantage

Description:
A sensory overload in the target’s pleasure- response cortex creates an overwhelming repulsion toward all forms of intimacy.
 
- The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become physically nauseated at the thought of intimacy.
- Disadvantage on Charisma- based rolls involving seduction, persuasion, or charm effects.
 
 The reverse of this spell, "Pleasure Numbness," removes all sexual or emotional distress responses for 24 hours, making the target indifferent to physical stimuli.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You send a steadying pulse through the Weft of Thought, re-threading frayed cognition and severing imposed patterns of fear, command, and suppression. The target’s mind exhales, slipping free from bindings it did not consent to hold.

When you touch a willing creature, the following effects occur:
	•	Ends effects that impose Charm, Fear, or externally forced obedience and suppression, provided they originate from enchantment, imposed will, or cognitive interference.
	•	Immediately ends mental domination, compliance enchantments, or artificial thought-locks imposed by authority, ritual, or anomaly.
	•	If the creature is unconscious due to mental overload, psychic pressure, or forced suppression, it immediately regains consciousness.

This spell does not remove curses tied to identity, oaths freely accepted, or enchantments sustained by active concentration. It does not erase memory. It restores agency.
Scaling: - At 5th level, the target gains advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws for 1 hour.

Description:
You seize a creature’s thread and forcibly pass it back through the Loom, overwriting identity, memory, and purpose. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, its former self is erased from conscious recall, replaced with a fully formed false identity sanctioned by higher recognition.

The creature believes this new life to be authentic and continuous, accepting fabricated history and status as truth. Attempts to restore the original thread fail, unless magic of the highest order is used to unravel and reweave what has been imposed.
Modifier Details: target must make a Wisdom saving throw

Foundry Tag: Modifies AC, Teleportation, Uses Bonus Action, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Modifies AC, Teleportation, Uses Bonus Action, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Description: The natural flow of the parquet wood beneath you guides your movements as you flicker momentarily between positions, destabilizing your form and reappearing in a more advantageous stance.- You gain advantage on your next Dexterity (Acrobatics) check or melee attack roll made before the end of your next turn.- If you are within 5 feet of a surface or object (like a wall or ledge), you can use your bonus action to teleport up to 10 feet in any direction within range. This teleportation does not provoke opportunity attacks.
Scaling: - At 5th level: The range of your teleportation increases to 15 feet.- At 11th level: You can also gain advantage on the first saving throw made against being restrained, paralyzed, or stunned during the turn the spell is cast.
Flavor: As you channel the natural flow of the parquet wood underfoot, you feel your body sync with the rhythm of the world around you. With a sudden flicker, your position changes, leaving only a brief blur behind before you land in a new, more favorable spot. The pulse of the earth beneath you supports your every movement, creating a seamless blend of natural agility and supernatural grace.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on next Dexterity (Acrobatics) check

Description:
You release a single guided strike that refuses to end with its first impact. Make a ranged weapon attack against a creature within your weapon’s normal range. On a hit, the strike deals its normal damage, then rebounds with unnatural precision.

After the initial hit, the strike leaps to up to two additional creatures of your choice, each within 15 feet of the last creature struck. Each additional target requires a separate ranged weapon attack roll. On a hit, an additional target takes half the damage dealt by the initial attack. The strike cannot leap to the same creature more than once.
Scaling: 1 target per level above 3rd
Modifier Details: requires a separate ranged weapon attack roll

Description:
You tear the warmth from a creature’s flesh as if unthreading heat from blood, forcing it to spill outward into the world in a violent frostbound surge.

Choose one creature within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw.
	•	On a failure, the creature is ravaged by unnatural cold as its body’s heat is ripped away, dealing severe cold damage. Its muscles stiffen and numb, leaving it unsteady and sluggish in its next strike.
	•	On a success, the creature resists the worst of the rimefold, suffering reduced cold damage and no lingering impairment.

If the target is in contact with metal, stone, or another heat-binding surface, the stolen warmth lashes outward. Creatures nearby are scorched by sudden frost as the displaced heat collapses into biting cold around them.

The air cracks and fogs instantly where the spell takes hold, and those who witness it swear they can see heat fleeing like pale ghosts from the victim’s skin.
Modifier Details: Constitution saving throw

Description: You realign the living patterns of up to six creatures, restoring harmony between form, function, and vitality. Each target regains 5d8 hit points and ends one condition affecting it: blinded, deafened, paralyzed, or poisoned. When used on constructs or augmented beings, the rite also resolves internal malfunctions as their systems are forced back into functional coherence.
Modifier Details: restores 5d8 hit points

Description:
You perform a cleansing rite that strips away imposed influence and coerced obedience, restoring a creature’s sovereign will. The spell removes all poison, disease, and dependency effects, and breaks long-term mental domination, conditioning, and subliminal control. Effects born of indoctrination, enforced loyalty, or imposed identity unravel and lose their hold.

At higher power, the rite can also sever cursed implants, binding artifacts, or devices that exert control over thought or behavior, restoring clarity and autonomy to the afflicted.
Scaling: - At 7th level, the spell also removes cursed cybernetic implants or mind- controlling artifacts.

Description:
You channel a precise surge of arcane force through a creature’s body, stilling thought, purging distortion, and re-aligning the spirit with its natural rhythm.
	•	All mind-affecting conditions are removed, including fear, confusion, domination, and mental overload.
	•	If the target has been warped by an anomaly, possession, or imposed alteration of will, it may make a Constitution saving throw to resist that influence for 1 hour.
	•	If the spell is forced upon an unwilling creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be stunned for 1 minute, its senses briefly drowned in silence.

The rite is taxing and invasive, briefly stripping the mind to its foundations before allowing it to rebuild cleanly.
Modifier Details: may make a Constitution saving throw to resist that influence

Class: Cleric, Wizard, Paladin, Druid, Sorceror, Bard
Description: You bind the life rhythms of two willing creatures into perfect concord, forging a shared cadence of motion, thought, and pain. While within 10 feet of each other, the bonded creatures gain advantage on attack rolls, resistance to cognitive damage, and the ability to communicate telepathically. When one creature takes damage, the other may use its reaction to absorb half of that damage, splitting the harm between them. The bond manifests as a steady, unseen pulse felt by both participants. If either creature becomes unwilling or unconscious, the accord immediately dissolves.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on attack rolls

Description:
You summon a single, spectral rat formed of condensed frost and arcane chill. It scurries off immediately, following an inexplicable instinct.- The rat automatically seeks the coldest location within a 200- foot radius, regardless of visibility or accessibility.- It can sense cold through barriers, insulation, or seals—even within a hermetically sealed container like a locked freezer.- As it moves, it leaves behind a trail of tiny frost- lined footprints, which last for 1 minute before sublimating.

The rat is entirely uninterested in combat and disappears instantly if initiative is rolled or hostilities begin nearby.
Flavor: It does not squeak. It does not stop. It does not care. It simply… finds the cold.

Description:
As your mind cracks under pressure, you unleash an anomalous pulse of rhythm and defiance—a stuttering cosmic melody that replays itself, over and over, across the battlefield. The air pulses in time with an unseen waltz. Reality blinks.

When you fail a saving throw, you may cast this spell to release a psychic backlash in a 20- foot radius centered on a point you choose within range.

Each creature in the area must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, they take 4d8 chiaric damage, and are staggered, unable to take reactions until the start of their next turn. On a success, they take half damage and suffer no additional effect.

Once per round for the duration (1 round), the melody echoes faintly again at initiative count 0. Creatures that failed their original save feel a tremor of déjà vu and take 1 cognitive damage as the rhythm loops.
Scaling: When you cast this spell using a 5th-level spell slot or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4th.
Flavor:
Something unknown has touched this magic, leaving behind traces of those who have cast it before.

The phantom ship *Elder’s Lament* was last seen adrift in the Void Sea, its captain having invoked Rotating Opus of Indignation in a desperate bid to escape the Hunt. The ship is still seen, sometimes, but never in the same place twice.

The very air around Rotating Opus of Indignation seems to recoil, as if reality itself wishes to avoid acknowledging its existence. It is a spell that should not be, yet is.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher:
“You hear a musical note that doesn’t exist in this octave. The ground pulses beneath your feet. One random creature (including possibly yourself) must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be forced to dance uncontrollably for 1 round, unable to take actions.”
Modifier Details: takes 4d8 chiaric damage
Trigger: After you fail a saving throw

Description:
You assert a Regal claim upon sanctioned stores maintained beyond the Veil. An item is drawn forth and manifested in an unoccupied space within range, bearing sigils of recognition from the Erelian Erudits.

The claimed provision may be a weapon, armor, or tool of notable value, a short-lived prototype bearing minor tesseral enhancement, or a sealed record such as plans, codes, or restricted writs. The item persists for a brief time before its claim expires and it is withdrawn from the Veil’s edge.

If the provision is squandered, destroyed, or unlawfully seized, the claim is marked as dishonored, and your standing suffers accordingly among those who track Regal obligation.
Component Description (Material): a requisition form stamped with an official corporate seal

Description:
You inscribe a rune onto a piece of paper, document, or surface. The rune hums with unstable magical nonsense, waiting for the next creature to read or open the marked object. When triggered, the rune unleashes a harmless but dramatic effect that startles, annoys, or mildly entertains.

The effect is randomly determined by rolling a d6:

The rune fades after being triggered. A creature that succeeds a DC 13 Arcana check while inspecting the document can spot the rune before triggering it.
Table:
d6 | Harmless Effect
--- |---------------- 
1 | A burst of confetti and a trumpet fanfare plays.
2 | The reader hears a magical raspberry blown directly into their ear.
3 | A tiny banner pops out: “Gotcha!”
4 | The paper insults the reader in a refined accent.
5 | Glitter explodes everywhere. It sticks.
6 | A small illusory bird flaps out and poops ghost ink on them.
Scaling: You can place one additional rune per spellcasting modifier (minimum of 1), but only one rune may be triggered at a time, and all runes share the same effect for the day.

Description:
