You press salt into a warded surface, whispered with a phrase of unbinding. For a fleeting moment, the salt dissolves the illusion of authority, allowing you to pass as legitimate in the eyes of magic.

When you touch a sealed, warded, or enchanted object such as a locked door, arcane seal, sigiled container, oath-bound barrier, or magically protected record, you temporarily override its binding logic and gain access.
	•	Simple enchantments (mundane locks with minor wards, apprentice seals, household charms) unravel automatically.
	•	Complex bindings require an Intelligence (Arcana) check based on the strength of the enchantment.
	•	This spell does not break purely physical locks without magical reinforcement.
	•	If the ward is part of a greater binding web (temple complex, vault network, royal archive), the disturbance echoes through the enchantment unless you suppress it.
Table:
| Binding Tier | Example Targets | Arcana DC |
|--------------|----------------|-----------|
| Tier I – Minor | Private lock, shop seal, apprentice ward | 10 |
| Tier II – Institutional | Guild vault, city archive, shrine seal | 13 |
| Tier III – Noble | Manor defenses, treasury doors, mage towers | 15 |
| Tier IV – Royal | Crown vaults, divine reliquaries, high temples | 17 |
| Tier V – Absolute | God-bound seals, ancient vaults, world-locks | 20 |
Scaling: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you gain advantage on the Arcana check for each slot level above 2nd.
Modifier Details: requires an Intelligence (Arcana) check based on the strength of the enchantment

Description:
You bind a spectral entity to a target, ensuring they are never alone again.
 
- Each night, the target must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 18) or be haunted by an invisible presence that whispers, shifts objects, and manifests in reflective surfaces.
- Each week, the haunting escalates, causing exhaustion if the target cannot escape it.
- The only way to remove the haunting is by offering someone else’s name in exchange.
Modifier Details: must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 18)
Component Description (Material): a contract written in the target’s blood

Description:
You conjure a sealed sanctum of focused labor in an unoccupied space within range. The space manifests as a quiet, furnished chamber containing desks, shelves, and arcane instruments suited to writing, calculation, and record-keeping.

The sanctum is soundproof and isolated from outside distraction. While within it, creatures may work, study, organize, or perform administrative or scholarly tasks without interruption. The sanctum lasts for the duration or until you dismiss it as an action, at which point it vanishes without leaving trace.

You conjure a thin line of crimson ink, written in your own blood, onto a surface within reach. The writing is in a language you know and can be as fine or elaborate as desired. You can erase the writing at will, but others cannot remove it by mundane means. The ink dries immediately and cannot be smudged. If used on a creature’s skin, it fades naturally in 24 hours.
Component Description (Material): a drop of your own blood

Description: You transmute corrupted or damaged blood within a creature. The target regains 2d8 + your spellcasting modifier HP and purges any diseases, toxins, or parasitic infections in their bloodstream.
Modifier Details: regains 2d8 + your spellcasting modifier HP

Description:
You inscribe an invisible sigil onto a creature or surface—an anomalous imprint that flickers just outside visible light. The mark is imperceptible to normal senses but can be perceived by you, and by others under the effects of an attunement protocol (such as *Reveal Transmission*, *Trace Anomaly*, or *See the Unseen*). 

You may choose one of the following effects for the mark: 
- It emits a subtle warmth or chill to those who touch it. 
- It causes a sense of unease or dread when handled. 
- It makes the marked object feel subtly *wrong* to the attuned. 

If placed on a creature, the mark persists until the duration ends or the target takes chiaric damage (1 HP or more). The sigil has no impact on gameplay unless revealed, but its presence may trigger paranormal sensors, bureaucratic alarms, or ritual interactions.
Flavor: A.S.T.R.A.L. monitoring stations can flag this anomaly for review if detected.
Component Description (Material): a drop of ink or blood

Description:
You unleash a brutal surge of temporal violence, wrenching time forward and backward in a single breath. Each creature affected must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes 6d8 temporal damage as its body is forced to relive moments of extreme strain in rapid succession, flesh and essence tearing under contradictory timelines. On a success, the creature takes half damage.

If a creature is reduced to 0 hit points by this effect, its body becomes locked in a frozen instant for one round. During this suspension, time refuses to advance for it. The creature cannot be healed, stabilized, or revived until the moment resumes and the frozen second is finally allowed to pass.
Flavor:
> "Time does not punish. It replays." 
> —Inscription found near the sealed rift in Hollowwatch.
Modifier Details: Constitution saving throw

Description:
You unleash a catastrophic unraveling that tears through a creature’s thread in the Loom, stripping away memory, discipline, and learned form in a single annihilating surge. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, it takes 15d10 cognitive damage as its mind is emptied, and it forgets all spells and abilities for 1 minute. On a success, it takes half damage and resists the deeper unraveling.

If a creature is reduced to 0 hit points by this spell, its thread is torn free entirely. Memories of its existence vanish from minds, archives, and Erudit registries alike. Only magic of the highest order or direct divine intervention can reweave what has been utterly scoured from the Loom.
Modifier Details: must make an Intelligence saving throw
Arcane Strain: Major Arcane Strain – Memory fragmentation or long- term reality instability may occur.

Description:
You impose a sovereign seal upon an area within a 60-foot radius, isolating it from the rest of reality. For the duration, no creature can enter or leave the area by teleportation, portals, or extradimensional movement.

The boundary is invisible and cannot be perceived or interacted with from outside. Creatures of overwhelming presence may attempt to destabilize the seal, testing its authority through sheer force of will.

You impose a suppressive seal upon the target’s flesh and spirit, stilling impulses of desire and severing the feedback between appetite, emotion, and will. Passion dulls, confidence falters, and the body resists engagement beyond necessity.

The afflicted creature struggles to project charm or allure, their resolve frayed by inner dissonance. If the target is held under enchantment born of desire or fascination, this seal shatters that influence outright, restoring clarity through enforced restraint rather than consent.
 
- The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or suffer from:
- Disadvantage on Charisma checks involving seduction or persuasion.
-- 2 penalty to attack rolls and morale checks as a side effect of psychological distress.
- Temporary reductions in Wisdom, Constitution, and Charisma by 1d3 points for the duration.
Flavor: Originally developed as a military- grade focus enhancement program, this spell is now illegally distributed in underground markets for controlling undesired urges.
Modifier Details: disadvantage on Charisma checks involving seduction or persuasion

Description:
You impose an arcane interdiction upon a structure or passage, cloaking it in the unmistakable aura of forbidden access.

A building, corridor, or enclosed area becomes subject to a temporary arcane closure. Entrances refuse to open, passages resist passage, and wards hum with dormant authority.
	•	Doors remain sealed, passages refuse traversal, and protective enchantments lapse into inert stillness.
	•	Creatures who encounter the sealed space instinctively accept the closure as valid unless they succeed on a DC 15 Intelligence check, dismissing it as illusion or trickery.

The closure appears official, ancient, and unquestionable, as though mandated by powers long established.

Repeated use of this rite in the same place risks attracting actual custodial forces, arcane caretakers who may unknowingly reinforce the interdiction rather than dispel it.
Anomaly Effect: - If cast too often, bureaucratic forces may send an actual maintenance crew, unknowingly reinforcing the spell.
Modifier Details: creatures must succeed on a DC 15 Intelligence check to dismiss the closure as illusion or trickery

Spell Name: Seam Ripper of the Living World
Description:
Reality was stitched together for convenience. You simply choose to pull at the thread.
You target a location within range and unravel the physical rules governing that terrain. For the duration, the area becomes Sutured Terrain:

- Matter Becomes Pliable – Stone flows like fabric. Walls bulge and flex. Ground can ripple like water underfoot. All movement is halved unless the creature succeeds a Strength saving throw at the start of its turn.
- Gravity Buckles – Gravity shifts every round. Roll 1d4:

Objects Drift Apart – Doors fall off frames. Windows become free- floating. Anchored structures must pass a DC 18 Constitution saving throw each round or begin to unravel into ambient material.
- Creatures Warped by Terrain – At the end of each round, any creature in the area must succeed a Constitution saving throw or gain a random minor mutation (DM’s discretion). Mutations last until the end of their next long rest.
Table:
| d4 | Gravity Effect |
|---- |--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | Gravity reverses. Non- anchored creatures fall upward. |
| 2 | Gravity pulls sideways. Creatures are dragged 10 feet to the left. |
| 3 | Gravity flattens. Jumping is impossible, all creatures feel compressed. |
| 4 | Gravity vanishes. Creatures float, gaining a flying speed equal to half their walking speed. |
Modifier Details: must pass a DC 18 Constitution saving throw
Component Description (Material): a surgeon’s needle dipped in your own blood

Description: You subtly alter the taste, aroma, and temperature of a small amount of food or liquid within range. You can make bland food flavorful, warm or chill it, and remove minor unpleasant qualities, but this spell does not neutralize poison, disease, or magical effects.

A binding ritual forces one or more undead entities into total compliance with the caster’s physical or emotional desires.

- The undead must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, they cannot resist the caster’s commands for the duration.
- If forced into pleasurable acts, they take 1d6 chiaric damage per instance of forced compliance.
- This spell is illegal under A.S.T.R.A.L. anomaly regulations and has severe ethical consequences.
Modifier Details: undead must make a Charisma saving throw
Component Description (Material): a drop of vitae from both caster and target

Description:
You inscribe a binding writ upon a willing creature’s body, asserting authority over the flow of life within them. The spell permanently alters the target’s fertility, as chosen at the time of casting: fertility may be enabled, suppressed, or set to follow a fixed cycle defined by the caster.

This alteration persists until the magic is deliberately revoked. Attempts to undo the effect through other magic or mundane means automatically alert the spell’s binding, triggering a restorative backlash that reasserts the original condition. Reversal of the writ requires a deliberate ritual process and a waiting period of two full days before the change takes effect.

In rare cases, the living pattern resists perfect order, producing unforeseen variations in flesh or lineage at the discretion of the world.
Anomaly Effect: In rare cases, biological data corruption results in spontaneous genetic mutations—some with unpredictable side effects.

Description: You attempt to forcibly disconnect a supernatural entity from the material world. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it loses its ethereal, invisible, or extraplanar traits for the duration. If it was bound to this plane via an anomaly, it must roll a d6 each round—on a 1, it is banished permanently.
Modifier Details: target must make a Charisma saving throw
Component Description (Material): a copper wire severed from an old telephone

Description: You disrupt a telepathic bond, magical connection, or mental influence. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they lose any telepathic abilities or magic item attunements for the duration.
Modifier Details: target must make a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You rip away a fragment of a target’s voice and presence, leaving behind only an echo.

- The target must make a Charisma saving throw (DC 16). On a failure, they become mute and their shadow begins acting independently.
- Each turn, their shadow makes an unintelligible whisper, causing 1d6 cognitive damage to the target.
- If the target dies while this spell is active, their shadow remains, endlessly repeating their last words.
Modifier Details: target must make a Charisma saving throw (DC 16)

Description: You invoke the name of something lost to time, letting its voice slip through your own. A creature of your choice within range hears a whisper from the void. It must make a Wisdom saving throw or become momentarily overwhelmed, causing it to miss the next verbal component it attempts to use before the start of your next turn. If the target does not cast spells, it instead hesitates, reducing its speed by 10 feet for one round.
Modifier Details: must make a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You sever a creature’s presence from bindings that rely on name, identity, or recognition. For the duration, the target gains the following effects:

• The target is immune to the Under-Contract condition.
• The target’s name cannot be magically spoken, written, recorded, or recalled.
• All attempts to bind, compel, or track the target through contractual, bureaucratic, or identity-based magic automatically fail.
• Any creature attempting to recall or reference the target experiences hesitation and cognitive interference.
• Ongoing effects that rely on legal, contractual, or imposed identity are suppressed.

The spell immediately ends if the target willingly identifies themselves, signs a binding agreement, or submits identifying information.
Flavor: This spell is illegal in all A.S.T.R.A.L.- regulated zones and may trigger automated containment review if detected.
Component Description (Material): a bureaucratic form with the target’s name scribbled out, stamped with an identity suppression seal
Acquisition Note: *See Blood Seal rules for alternative acquisition methods.*
Arcane Strain: Using a Blood Seal instead of the bureaucratic suppression mark inflicts Minor Arcane Strain.

Description:
Allows the caster to communicate with their own shadow. Gains advantage on persuasion and investigation check for the next hour. The shadow is sarcastic and unhelpful.
Additional Effects: Gains advantage on persuasion and investigation check for the next hour Gains advantage on persuasion and investigation check for the next hour
Modifier Details: gains advantage on persuasion and investigation checks for the next hour

Description: You focus a controlled vibration into the glass floor beneath you, causing a sudden concussive burst that shatters the surface. The shockwave ripples outward, damaging those within range.- Adjacent creatures (within 5 feet) must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they take 1d6 sonic damage and are deafened until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, they take half damage and are not deafened.- You suffer no ill effects from the glass beneath you, and you remain unharmed.
Scaling: - At 5th level: The radius of the blast increases to 10 feet.- At 11th level: The damage increases to 2d6.- At 17th level: The deafening effect lasts for 1 minute and can be dispelled by a creature using a Heal or Lesser Restoration spell.
Flavor: The druid channels the earth’s natural vibrations through the glass floor, using it as a conduit for explosive energy. The floor shatters like glass under the force of a wave, sending concussive shockwaves into any creature unlucky enough to be nearby. The spell’s precise vibration control makes it an effective tool for both damaging enemies and disrupting their balance, while the unique use of sound as a weapon provides an eerie, almost supernatural quality to the druid’s powers.
Modifier Details: takes 1d6 sonic damage and is deafened

Description:
You fracture the surface of your own cognition, rendering your thoughts unreadable and irregular. For the duration, you gain advantage on saving throws against mind-reading effects, telepathic intrusion, and charm magic.

Any attempt to probe your mind encounters only broken impressions, overlapping fragments, and contradictory echoes, as though your thoughts refuse to assemble into a coherent pattern under external scrutiny.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on saving throws against mind-reading effects, telepathic intrusion, and charm magic

Description:
You draw open a temporary refuge by folding the Veil inward, creating an extradimensional shelter just out of phase with reality. Each casting forms a distinct pocket shaped by abandoned intent and forgotten function, its interior manifesting as unfamiliar yet functional architecture.

The shelter offers safety and rest, sealed from outside intrusion. Yet the Veil is imperfectly bound. Subtle anomalies sometimes emerge within the space, leaving signs that something else briefly shared the refuge. When the shelter dissolves, faint impressions linger, as if the Veil remembers being disturbed.
Component Description (Material): a key from an unknown origin

Description: You subtly alter the perception of an indoor corridor or room, causing it to appear slightly longer, shorter, or misaligned. A creature attempting to navigate the affected area must make an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC or briefly hesitate, treating the terrain as difficult for one round.
Modifier Details: creature attempting to navigate the affected area must make an Intelligence (Investigation) check ag

Description: You whisper a fleeting phrase laced with unsettling intent, audible only to one creature within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or hear your voice echoing around them, causing mild disorientation. On a failed save, the creature has disadvantage on its next attack roll or ability check before the start of your next turn.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description: A nearly invisible strand of shadow stretches between you and a creature within range. For the duration, you always know the direction of the target as long as they remain within range. The thread vanishes if the target moves behind total cover or crosses a body of water wider than 5 feet.

You coat a surface or willing creature with a living unction that defies ordinary friction, spreading in a smooth, self-renewing sheen guided by the Weft rather than gravity. The substance is clean, safe, and responsive, adjusting itself to motion rather than resisting it.

If cast upon a surface, creatures moving across it must make a Dexterity saving throw or lose their footing as the ground flows subtly against their balance. If cast upon a willing creature, the unction enhances motion and flexibility for the duration, allowing seamless, fluid movement without strain.

Creatures affected by enchantments find the unction especially treacherous. Any Dexterity saving throw they make to cross or interact with the treated area is made with disadvantage, as divided will and yielding motion conspire against control.
Modifier Details: creatures affected by enchantments make Dexterity saving throws with disadvantage

Foundry Tag: Healing, Permanent Effects, Modifies AC, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Healing, Permanent Effects, Modifies AC, Grants Advantage
Description:
You weave an invisible suture in the air, connecting it to the target’s flesh and nerves. The thread does not cut, it unravels, pulling the skin away in delicate layers.

- The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, their flesh begins to separate from their body, piece by piece.
- Round 1: Their fingertips and lips begin to curl and peel, exposing raw flesh.
- Round 2: The skin along their arms and jaw pulls loose, hanging like discarded parchment.
- Round 3: Their entire face and chest are stripped, leaving only muscle and exposed nerves.

- The target takes 4d10 chiaric damage per round and suffers disadvantage on all Dexterity- based actions due to agony.
- If the spell lasts the full minute, the target is left entirely skinless, a raw, quivering thing, forced to exist in constant, unbearable sensation.

Skinless Condition: 
- The creature has disadvantage on all saving throws against pain, stun, or fear effects. 
- They are vulnerable to pyric and radiation damage. 
- They cannot benefit from healing spells other than *Reconstructive Audit* or *First Responder Algorithm* until ritually treated. 
- They are permanently disfigured, unless magically or surgically restored. 

End of the Spell: 
- The target may repeat the Constitution save at the end of each of their turns. On a success, the unweaving halts but does not reverse—what has been lost remains lost. 
- If the spell is cast multiple times on the same target, their skin may begin to regrow incorrectly, forming anomalous grafts that twitch and whisper in forgotten tongues.
Flavor:
> “Flesh can be carved. Bone can be broken. But to be skinless… is to be unprotected even from the wind.” 
> —Archivist Trellian Vahl, *On Nerves and the Ritual of Peeling*
Anomaly Effect: - If cast repeatedly, the peeled skin may attempt to reform itself into something separate, crawling and watching from the corners of the room.
Modifier Details: target must make a Constitution saving throw
Component Description (Material): a thread soaked in human blood

Description:
In the intimacy of flesh and fevered breath, the caster siphons the essence of slumber from their partner.

- After a shared act of passion, the target falls into an unnatural sleep for 1 hour per caster level.
- The caster awakens fully refreshed, as if they had slept those stolen hours.
- If the spell is cast repeatedly on the same victim, their dreams begin to fray, unraveling into visions of things best left unseen.
- If the target is asleep or unconscious, they automatically fail the saving throw against this spell.
- Some say the most powerful users of this spell never sleep at all, stealing rest from a hundred lovers.
Modifier Details: automatically fails the saving throw

Description:
You step sideways into a neighboring thread of time, a reflection of the world where moments were never chosen and events never fully occurred. In this half-real passage, the world appears hollow and distant, as though carved from memory rather than matter.

While veiled within this untaken timeline, you may move freely and unseen, untouched by sight, sound, or magic rooted in the waking world. Walls offer no welcome, voices carry no meaning, and nothing you do leaves a mark. You are a passing thought, not a presence.

When the spell ends, the abandoned timeline releases you, and you return to the world of consequence exactly where you began, as though you had never left at all. Some who use this magic swear they feel another version of themselves hesitate just out of reach, wondering why it was left behind.

You speak with a voice that does not belong to you—layered, shifting, and wrong.
 
One creature of your choice within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save:
- The creature flinches involuntarily, disrupting its focus.
- If it takes an action on its next turn, it must spend its bonus action first, if available.
- If it has no bonus action to use, its speed is reduced by 10 feet instead.

 A creature that succeeds on its saving throw is immune to this effect for 24 hours.
Modifier Details: must make a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You entangle the target’s mind in a knot of Still Time, halting the flow of cognition mid-process. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, their thoughts lock in place. They cannot take actions, bonus actions, or reactions, and they automatically fail Intelligence-based checks and saving throws for the duration.

At the end of each of its turns, the creature may repeat the saving throw. On a success, the knot loosens, thought resumes, and the effect ends.
Modifier Details: must make an Intelligence saving throw

Description: A veil of otherworldly clarity shrouds the target, severing their mind from chemical, magical, or emotional distortion.- The target becomes immune to alcohol, narcotics, pleasure- enhancing magic, and any enchantments that manipulate perception or judgment.- They remain unnaturally composed, able to see through illusions, resist temptation, and stay grounded even in the grip of hallucination or desire.- The target cannot be charmed, intoxicated, or seduced for the duration.
Flavor: A priest of A.S.T.R.A.L. once described the sensation as “staring into the abyss while the abyss begs you to blink.”
Modifier Details: target cannot be charmed, intoxicated, or seduced for the duration

Description:
You unleash a burst of targeted emotional disruption based on the black site experiment RAGE- 031. One creature of your choice within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or succumb to uncontrollable fury.

While affected, the target is enraged, experiencing an irrational compulsion to lash out. At the start of each of its turns, roll a d6 to determine the target’s behavior:

The rage lasts for 1 minute or until the target takes damage. A creature under this effect is considered charmed by the rage itself and cannot willingly end it early.


When cast with a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you may target one additional creature for each slot level above 3rd.
Table:
| d6 | Rage Behavior |
|---- |----------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | Attacks the caster with reckless focus |
| 2 | Screams at itself, attacking with disadvantage |
| 3 | Charges and punches the nearest wall or obstacle |
| 4 | Rips off parts of its clothing in a frenzy |
| 5 | Smashes the nearest object or piece of furniture |
| 6 | Rants at an unseen force only they can perceive |
Flavor: Survivors of Experiment RAGE- 031 recalled only the distorted audio loops and the overwhelming need to destroy something—anything. No test subject retained the ability to reason for hours afterward.
Modifier Details: target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You draw strength from the stability of concrete beneath your feet, solidifying your posture and ensuring that you cannot be easily moved. Your connection to the earth gives you exceptional resistance to being pushed or knocked down.

You gain advantage on Strength and Dexterity saving throws made to resist knockback or any effects that would push, pull, or topple you. This effect lasts until the start of your next turn.
Scaling: When you reach higher levels, the ability strengthens:- 5th level: You can now resist grapples and restraining effects with advantage as well.- 11th level: You also gain resistance to forced movement, such as telekinetic pushes or teleportation effects that seek to reposition you.
Flavor: A druid attuned to the urban landscape or the resilience of built environments uses concrete’s unyielding nature to channel an innate stability. They sense the deep foundation of the ground beneath them, drawing on its permanence to stand firm against any attempt to disrupt their position. The caster’s posture straightens as if they themselves have become part of the unmovable structure around them, ready to resist whatever forces attempt to unseat them.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on Strength saving throws made to resist knockback or any effects that would push, p

Description:
The soothing comfort of velvet carpet emanates from your being, gently reinforcing your body and spirit. The calming energy wraps around you, providing minor restorative benefits and a sense of calm resilience.

You gain 1d4 temporary hit points, which last until you take a short or long rest. This temporary hit points cannot stack and are replenished with each casting of the spell.
Scaling: - At 5th level: The temporary hit points increase to 2d4.- At 11th level: The effect of the spell extends to allies within 10 feet of you, granting them 1d4 temporary hit points as well.- At 17th level: The temporary hit points increase to 3d4, and the duration is extended to 1 hour instead of just being until your next rest.
Flavor: A druid who has learned to embrace the comfort of natural softness manifests a calming aura as they attune to the environment around them. The soothing nature of soft, plush materials like velvet becomes a channel for their connection to comfort, warmth, and rejuvenation. As they breathe in deeply, the tension in their body releases, replaced by a quiet, resilient strength, echoing the peaceful tranquility of the soft carpet beneath their feet.
Modifier Details: gain 1d4 temporary hit points

Foundry Tag: Permanent Effects, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Permanent Effects, Affects Objects, Grants Advantage
Description:
You carve a true name or concept into an object, binding it not just in matter, but in meaning.
 
- The engraving is permanent—it cannot be removed, altered, or counterfeited.
- If the object is stolen, broken, or lost, its owner feels its absence like a phantom limb.
- The item becomes "known" to reality—it cannot be easily forgotten, overlooked, or discarded.

- If used on a weapon or tool, its wielder feels more attuned to it, gaining advantage on rolls related to its use (e.g., Smith’s Tools, a specific sword, or a custom- built firearm).
Anomaly Effect: - If the engraving is too ambitious, the object may begin carving messages on its own, attempting to write its own story.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on rolls related to its use
Component Description (Material): a chisel made from an ancestor’s bone

Description:
You invoke an invisible tether of force that violently pulls a creature backward through the path it came.

Choose one creature within range. That target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be dragged up to 30 feet in a straight line toward the space it occupied at the start of its previous turn.

- If the target collides with a solid object or creature during this forced movement, it takes 6d12 void damage, and the object or creature it struck takes half as much.
- On a successful save, the target is not moved, but still takes half damage from the forceful retraction.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Strength saving throw

Description: You summon a spectral cooking utensil, such as a cast iron pan, a sturdy pot, or a well-balanced ladle. This summoned item can be wielded as a magical weapon. It bypasses resistance to nonmagical attacks. On a hit, the utensil deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage. The force damage increases as you gain levels: 1d4 force at 5th level, 2d4 force at 11th level, and 3d4 force at 17th level.
Modifier Details: deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage

Description: You weave a transient channel of arcane force into a willing creature you can see within range. Until the spell ends, the next time that creature makes a spell attack and hits, the attack deals one additional damage die of the same type as the spell (do not add ability modifiers twice). The effect then ends. If the attack misses, the spell persists until the end of the duration or until an empowered spell attack hits. This spell ends early if you cast another spell that requires concentration.
Scaling: Optional slot-based scaling: +1 die per slot level
Modifier Details: deals one additional damage die of the same type as the spell

Description: You create a 20-foot-radius sphere in which light, sound, and distant perception are smothered. Creatures within the sphere are blinded and deafened, and magical means of communication fail unless a creature succeeds on a Wisdom saving throw. The space feels hollowed out, as if sensation itself has been removed.
Modifier Details: requires a Wisdom saving throw

Description: Stimulating energy surges through the target. A creature you touch gains 10 feet of additional movement speed and temporary hit points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier for the duration.
Modifier Details: gains 10 feet of additional movement

Description:
Drawing upon the natural flow of the wooden environment around you, you call forth a sharpened splinter from the very floor or nearby wooden structures, launching it with precision and agility.

You make a ranged spell attack against one creature within 20 feet. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 tearing damage as a splinter of wood embeds itself in their body.
Scaling:
The spell’s damage increases when you reach higher levels:
- 5th level: 2d6
- 11th level: 3d6
- 17th level: 4d6
Flavor: Urban druids who specialize in interior architecture and sustainable urban environments often rely on wooden elements to channel their magic. When they cast this spell, small creaks and pulses of energy resonate through the wooden beams, and the floor beneath them seems to bend slightly before a sudden burst of sharp wooden projectiles is released.
Modifier Details: makes a ranged spell attack

Description:
You afflict a creature’s mind with a sudden deluge of irrelevant memories, false recollections, and intrusive ideas. Fragments of half-remembered lessons, imagined failures, and meaningless details flood their thoughts, crowding out focus and intent.

The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, it is mentally overwhelmed for the duration. While affected, the creature rolls 1d6 and subtracts the result from all attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws, as hesitation and confusion interfere with every decision.

The spell ends early if the target takes psychic damage, as sharp mental pain clears the cluttered thoughts through shock and forced clarity.

For the duration, the creature is visibly distracted, muttering, hesitating, or reacting a moment too late, as though trapped in its own spiraling inner monologue.
Modifier Details: must make an Intelligence saving throw

Description: A sudden burst of static energy surges through a target. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw or take 2d8 electric damage and lose all electronic communication for 1 minute. On a successful save, the target takes half damage and suffers no disruption.
Scaling: At Higher Levels. When cast using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 per level.
Modifier Details: must make a Constitution saving throw

Foundry Tag: Modifies AC, Creates Light, Difficult Terrain, Affects Objects
Unnamed: 5: Modifies AC, Creates Light, Difficult Terrain, Affects Objects
Description:
You blur at the edges, your body slipping into the seams of unreality. Shadows cling to you unnaturally, muting your presence, your weight, your warmth. You become a half- whisper in the world of form.

While the spell is active:- You gain resistance to all damage except chiaric and cognitive.- You cannot physically interact with creatures or objects (you pass through doors but cannot open them; you cannot attack or cast spells requiring material components).- You are considered heavily obscured to creatures relying on normal sight and invisible in dim light or darkness.- You can move through non- magical objects and walls as if they were difficult terrain, but you take 1d10 void damage if you end your turn inside a solid object.
Scaling: If you cast this spell using a 6th-level slot or higher, you may choose to phase through magical barriers (like Wall of Force) as long as you are not being directly observed.
Flavor:
Something unknown has touched this magic, leaving behind traces of those who have cast it before.

The phantom ship *Elder’s Lament* was last seen adrift in the Void Sea, its captain having invoked Shadow's Embrace in a desperate bid to escape the Hunt. The ship is still seen, sometimes, but never in the same place twice.

The very air around Shadow's Embrace seems to recoil, as if reality itself wishes to avoid acknowledging its existence. It is a spell that should not be, yet is.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: gains resistance to all damage except chiaric and cognitive
Modifier Fixed Value: resistance to all damage except chiaric and cognitive

Description:
You conjure a shimmering veil of pale green energy across the target’s skin, filtering their body from infection both mundane and magical.- The target becomes immune to contracting, carrying, or transmitting any disease for the duration.- Any disease currently affecting them is rendered dormant—its symptoms subside and it cannot worsen, but it is not cured.- While affected, the target emits a subtle aroma of antiseptic and burnt sugar. Creatures with keen smell may sense this aura.

If this spell is cast repeatedly on the same creature without curing the underlying disease, roll a DC 12 Constitution saving throw for the target after the third use in a day. On a failure, they gain one level of exhaustion as their immune system becomes paradoxically hypersensitive and destabilized.
Flavor: Healers who overuse this veil often report phantom fevers or hallucinated symptoms, as if their bodies are grieving the absence of illness.
Modifier Details: DC 12 Constitution saving throw

Description:
You carve out a pocket of Still Time, a suspended antechamber where motion halts but awareness remains sharp. Up to six creatures within range are drawn into an echoed copy of the location, existing slightly apart from the Loom’s forward weave.

Outside the chamber, time flows normally. Within it, no external force may intrude, and no action taken can affect the world beyond. Through a silent veil, you may observe reality as it unfolds, gathering knowledge while remaining untouched.

Lingering too long risks thinning your thread. When you return, reality may hesitate to recognize you, causing subtle distortions as the Loom struggles to reweave your presence.
Anomaly Effect: If you stay too long, external reality might forget you exist, causing minor distortions when you return.

Description:
You draw the target into a heightened alignment with Still Time, compressing effort and intention into a narrow, relentless focus. For the duration, the creature’s movements sharpen and reactions tighten, granting a +2 bonus to AC, advantage on attack rolls, and resistance to exhaustion effects as their body functions beyond its natural cadence.

When the effect ends, the compressed moment releases all at once. For 1 hour, the creature suffers disadvantage on all ability checks as their thread struggles to resettle into the Loom’s ordinary rhythm, leaving them drained and unfocused.
Modifier Details: +2 bonus to ac

Description: You slow your breathing and still your pulse, imitating the lifeless calm of a corpse. Until the spell ends, you appear dead to mundane inspection, such as touch or sight. A creature that physically examines you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom (Medicine) check to determine that you are still alive. This effect does not suppress your ability to move or speak, but any voluntary action ends the spell immediately.
Modifier Details: DC 15 Wisdom (Medicine) check to determine that you are still alive

Foundry Tag: Requires Sight, Summons Creature, Affects Objects
Unnamed: 5: Requires Sight, Summons Creature, Affects Objects
Description:
You conjure a magically reinforced stone ship at a point you can see within range. The ship appears instantly and gently settles without causing damage, regardless of its immense weight.- If summoned onto water:
The ship immediately sinks to the bottom without damage and remains indefinitely as a stable underwater structure.- If summoned onto land or any solid surface:
The ship appears safely resting on the ground, remaining for 1 minute before vanishing harmlessly. It can serve as temporary cover, shelter, or a temporary barricade during this time.

Creatures or objects caught underneath when the ship is conjured are gently pushed aside and take no damage.
Clarification:
- The ship cannot cause physical harm due to its magical weightlessness when conjured.
- Provides half- cover (+2 AC) to creatures aboard or behind it.
- Once summoned, the ship cannot be moved or manipulated by normal physical means due to its immense (though magically nullified) weight.
Table:
| Spell Slot Level | Ship Size | Capacity |
|------------------ |----------------- |----------------------------------- |
| 1st | Tiny (5 ft.) | Fits 1 Small creature comfortably |
| 2nd | Small (10 ft.) | Fits up to 2 Medium creatures |
| 3rd | Medium (20 ft.) | Fits up to 6 Medium creatures |
| 4th | Large (40 ft.) | Fits up to 12 Medium creatures |
| 5th | Huge (60 ft.) | Fits up to 30 Medium creatures |
Modifier Details: remains for 1 minute before vanishing harmlessly

Description:
You invoke an absolute decree of the Imperatum, striking directly at a creature’s place within existence. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it suffers 20d12 impact damage as the Loom recoils against its presence, and its name, likeness, and identity are erased from all records and memories of those who fail to recognize the severance.

On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and remains anchored within the Loom. The strike does not merely harm the body. It attempts to deny that the creature was ever meant to persist.
Modifier Details: target must make a Charisma saving throw

Description: A lance of discerning force tears through the target, briefly measuring its place in reality. The creature must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it takes 6d8 impact damage. If the creature is extraplanar or warped by anomalous influence, it also suffers disadvantage on its next saving throw before the end of its next turn. On a success, the creature takes half damage.
Scaling: At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 per slot level above 3rd.
Modifier Details: takes disadvantage on its next saving throw before the end of its next turn
Acquisition Note: Use of this spell without proper documentation may result in corporate retaliation.

Description:
You sever a creature’s thread from the Loom of recognition, unweaving their presence from all sanctioned records and registries maintained by the Erelian Erudits. Names, credentials, holdings, and proofs of standing dissolve as though they were never woven.

The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, they are treated as a non-entity by all institutions, authorities, and systems of recognition. Doors do not open for them, claims do not acknowledge them, and obligations cannot be formally applied, as their thread no longer appears in the Loom’s accounted weave.
Modifier Details: target must make an Intelligence saving throw

Description:
You impress a sovereign edict upon a constructed or augmented being, forcing its animating will to bow beneath your authority. The target must resist or become bound to your command, accepting your directives as lawful imperatives rather than coercion.

While bound, it obeys simple orders issued by your will, acting as though service were its original purpose. This magic cannot dominate minds that are fully self-aware and sovereign in their own right.

You summon the pest—an immortal, mildly sentient mosquito designated Pest- 1 in A.S.T.R.A.L. records. There is only one such mosquito in existence, and this spell temporarily returns it from its dormant state. The mosquito appears within 5 feet of the most important person in the area (DM’s discretion) and begins an aggressive buzzing and biting campaign.
- If Summon Pest is cast again: Pest- 1 is not duplicated. It simply relocates to the new target, abandoning the old one unless they are still within 30 feet of the new caster, in which case it harasses both.
- If no suitable target exists: Pest- 1 vanishes into non- space until summoned again.
- If the target becomes untargetable (e.g., invisible, banished): Pest- 1 immediately shifts to the next most “important” person in the room.
- If someone other than the original caster uses the spell: Pest- 1 recognizes this and updates its priorities accordingly, often begrudgingly.

Mechanical Effect:
The affected creature is considered distracted for 1 minute:
- Disadvantage on concentration checks.
- Cannot benefit from the Invisible condition.
- Any attack rolls made against the creature have advantage if they rely on sound or movement (e.g., tremorsense or blindsight).
Scaling: Casting this spell using a slot of 3rd level or higher causes Pest- 1 to gain memory of the target. The next time it’s summoned, it will return to the previous target unless they are farther than 1 mile from the caster.
Flavor: Pest- 1 is believed to predate Concord City. It has no known predator, no known prey, and no biological need to feed—yet it does. Repeated exposure to Pest- 1 has caused breakdowns in test subject behavior, often leading to social ruin, combat mistakes, or existential despair.
Modifier Details: has disadvantage on concentration checks
Component Description (Material): A feather from a random bird

Description:
You call forth a retinue of spectral servants bound to authority rather than flesh. These figures resemble robed scribes, stone-faced attendants, and faceless functionaries of the Imperatum, each moving with practiced efficiency and unquestioning purpose.

For the duration, the retinue can perform non-combat labor such as assembly, repair, organization, transcription, or logistical processing at a rate equivalent to ten skilled workers. They do not tire, complain, or improvise, acting only within the scope of their summoned mandate.

If threatened or forced into combat, the retinue dissolves instantly, their purpose complete or revoked, leaving behind only the faint sense that order briefly passed through the space.

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Uses Bonus Action
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Uses Bonus Action
Description:
You conjure a semi-sentient spectral engine of war that manifests as a massive, floating sling bound to unseen forces. Suspended in the air by arcane tension, it slowly swings back and forth, gathering momentum like a pendulum wound with memory and wrath. At its center hangs a heavy astral cradle, repeatedly loaded with conjured stones of condensed force.

The Pendulum of Ruin acts on your initiative, immediately after your turn. Each round, it selects a hostile creature within range and releases a thunderous cast, hurling its projectile with inevitability rather than aim. Its attacks are magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance.

The sling may be mentally guided with a bonus action, adjusting its focus or letting it act according to its own brutal instinct, always favoring the nearest hostile presence. The air around it thrums with lethal anticipation, and standing too close invites lingering harm from its unstable energies.

Though it has no wielder, the pendulum swings as if pulled by unseen ambition, each release echoing like a judgment cast across the battlefield.
Clarification:
Additional Rules:
- The artillery is immune to cognitive damage and charm effects.
- It vanishes if reduced to 0 HP or if your concentration ends.
- Enemies within 5 feet of the artillery at the end of their turn take 1d6 radiation damage from exposure to its lingering aura.
- You may mentally command it using a bonus action, or let it act independently according to its targeting protocol (attacking the nearest hostile creature).
Table:
| Spell Slot | Artillery Type | Damage | Traits |
|------------ |-------------------------- |--------------- |--------------------------------------------------------- |
| 3rd | Field Echo Turret | 3d8 kinetic | Stationary. Basic cannon. 60 HP. |
| 4th | Crawling Barrage Unit| 5d10 impact | Mobile. Can move 10 ft/turn. Fires twice per round. |
| 5th | Skyhammer Array | 6d12 radiant | Hovers. Can fire area bursts (10- ft radius). |
| 6th | Godmouth Obliterator | 8d10 chiaric | Semi- sapient. Ignores cover. 100 HP. Deals splash radiation. |
Scaling: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the artillery grows in size, intelligence, and destructive potential.
Quotes:
“You don’t fire it. You convince it to fire.”
—From a redacted A.S.T.R.A.L. containment memo.
Modifier Details: attacks are magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance

Description:
You invoke the Assassins of the Saint, a hidden order of executioners bound by ancient contracts. This spell marks a target for death, ensuring that they will never know peace again.

- At dawn the next day, the target awakens with a blood- red rose placed on their chest.
- From this moment, Assassins of the Saint will begin hunting them.
- The first assassin arrives within 1d6 days, with each subsequent hunter increasing in lethality.

 Counterplay & Removal:
- The mark cannot be dispelled through normal means.
- Only by making an offering of equal or greater worth (to the Saint’s order) can the mark be transferred to another soul.
Flavor:
The Saint’s Will is Absolute:
If the caster attempts to interfere, they too receive the mark of the blood rose.
Component Description (Material): a black candle, a silver coin, and the target’s written name on parchment

Description:
You call forth an unseen arbiter of order, a faceless force that demands the world be made tidy, complete, and properly recorded.

An invisible scriptor-spirit manifests within a room or similarly bounded space. Though unseen, its presence is undeniable: parchment rustles, quills scratch, and judgmental murmurs echo from nowhere.

While the scriptor presides:
	•	No creature may willingly leave the area until all demanded records are completed in exacting multiplicity.
	•	Any documents present must be copied in triplicate, a process that consumes time and patience.
	•	If no records exist, the spirit conjures an exhausting stack of forms.

If cast in an open or outdoor space, the scriptor first translates all creatures into a barren clerical demesne, a small featureless chamber filled with desks, parchment, and stiff chairs. None may leave until the task is complete or the spell ends.

Failure to comply provokes consequences known only to the spirit, which may include ominous warnings, inscrutable marks upon fate, or references to a record that cannot be erased.
Table:
| d4 | Consequence of Non- Compliance |
|---- |------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 1 | You receive a cryptic, ominous "Official Warning." |
| 2 | Your name appears on a mysterious "watchlist." |
| 3 | You owe inexplicable back taxes in a foreign city. |
| 4 | The invisible entity sighs and mentions your "permanent record." |
Scaling: When cast using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the paperwork increases exponentially, making escape progressively more difficult and tedious (triplicate at 4th-level, quadruplicate at 5th-level, etc.).
Modifier Details: failure to comply provokes consequences known only to the spirit

Description:
You invoke forbidden authority and rend open a violent passage between two points of the world that should never touch.

A vast, unstable rift opens between your position and another location you can see within range. The tear yawns wide, its edges writhing with warped light and fractured space, and remains open for the duration so long as your focus holds.
	•	Any creature may step through the rift as a movement, arriving instantly at the far side.
	•	All who pass must return through the same gate before it closes.

Those who fail to return are cast into the Between, a collapsing lattice of half-formed dimensions.

While lost between worlds:
	•	The creature cannot be targeted, perceived, contacted, or located by any means.
	•	After 1d6 hours, the world violently rejects them, expelling them into the nearest safe, unoccupied space near the destination.
	•	Upon return, they suffer 3d10 distortive damage, their bodies and minds wrenched by temporal and spatial dissonance.

If you willingly end the spell or lose consciousness, the gate snaps shut instantly, severing all remaining paths.
Modifier Details: after 1d6 hours, the world violently rejects them

Description:
You release a concussive burst of internal instability — the psychic scream of a soul sundered by power.

Each creature of your choice within 5 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they take 1d6 cognitive damage and are pushed 5 feet away from you. On a success, they take half damage and are not moved.

The psychic burst has no visible light, but those affected feel an echo of broken memory.
Table:
The spell’s damage increases as you reach higher levels:
| Character Level | Damage |
|-----------------|--------|
| 1st             | 1d6    |
| 5th             | 2d6    |
| 11th            | 3d6    |
| 17th            | 4d6    |
Modifier Details: takes 1d6 cognitive damage

Description:
You guarantee that your target contracts a non- lethal but incredibly annoying illness. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become afflicted for the duration. While afflicted, the target suffers one randomly determined symptom.

Roll a d10 on the Inconvenient Affliction Table to determine the effect:
Table:
| d10 | Inconvenient Affliction |
|----- |------------------------------------------------------ |
| 1 | Uncontrollable sneezing. Disadvantage on Stealth checks. |
| 2 | Randomly itchy elbows. Disadvantage on ranged attacks. |
| 3 | Cannot pronounce the letter "R." Verbal components sound ridiculous. |
| 4 | Burps loudly every time they speak. Disadvantage on Persuasion checks. |
| 5 | Excessive sweating. Must make a DC 10 Dex save to avoid dropping items. |
| 6 | One leg falls asleep. Movement speed is halved. |
| 7 | Hearing high- pitched ringing. Disadvantage on Perception (hearing). |
| 8 | Sudden lactose intolerance. Food- based spells fail. |
| 9 | One hand becomes clammy and wet. Disadvantage on sleight of hand. |
| 10 | Mild but persistent nausea. Disadvantage on Constitution saves. |
Scaling: When cast using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you may roll twice and apply both afflictions (reroll duplicates).
Flavor: Initially developed as an office prank by a rogue botanist- wizard, this spell became a controlled anomaly after an outbreak of contagious spoonerisms in Sector D.

Description:
You attune your balance and momentum to the structural rhythm of your surroundings—metal beams, concrete ledges, scaffolding, and rooftop catwalks. For the briefest moment, the world feels designed for your movement.

Until the start of your next turn, your long jump distance is doubled, and you do not need a 10- foot run- up to make a long jump.

This movement ignores difficult terrain caused by uneven platforms, narrow ledges, or slippery surfaces, as long as they are part of a constructed or elevated structure (e.g., catwalks, fire escapes, scaffolding, or city rooftop obstacles).
Flavor:
Surefooted Leap is commonly used by urban druids, parkour enthusiasts, and rooftop messengers trained by nature itself. They move not like people, but like whispers on the wind—navigating a city’s skeleton with the grace of a climbing vine or the leap of a fox from rusted girder to bent pipe.

When this spell is cast, there’s a moment where gravity seems to wait—just long enough for you to leap farther than you should.
Modifier Details: long jump distance is doubled

Description:
You seize the target’s thread at the Loom’s edge and jolt it toward survival, igniting instinct before thought can intervene. The creature’s body responds instantly, driven by primal momentum rather than deliberation.

For the duration, the creature’s speed increases by 20 feet. It gains advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) and Strength (Athletics) checks, and it automatically succeeds on the first check made to escape a grapple, binding, or restraint. The effect does not feel planned or controlled. When it ends, the creature may struggle to explain how they escaped at all, only that their body moved before fear could catch up.
Quotes: You won’t *think* about escaping—you’ll *already be gone.
Modifier Details: speed increases by 20 feet

Description:
You briefly attune to the narrative fabric of reality, gaining an uncanny sensitivity to dramatic structure, clichés, and meta- patterns. For the duration, you can sense the presence of common story tropes in characters, environments, and events within range.

You can detect things like:
- When a villain is about to monologue
- If a side character radiates “tragic backstory energy”
- Whether a room feels “too conveniently empty”
- If an ancient prophecy is about to become relevant
- When someone is definitely about to betray the party

This insight is incredibly annoying to others. NPCs may roll Insight to feel disturbed by your overly convenient genre awareness.

Meta Mechanic (once per casting):

If the DM becomes visibly annoyed by your observations—subtly or overtly—you may “break the fourth wall.” When this happens:
- You may ask the DM one question about the current narrative, twist, or character.
- The DM must answer, but may do so cryptically.
- Your character forgets the answer consciously, but retains it subconsciously.
- The player gains one “narrative gut feeling” that can be invoked at any time for advantage on a single Insight, Perception, or Investigation check.

This ability can only be triggered once per casting.
Clarification: Roleplay Guidance:- You might instinctively distrust someone because they “look like a redemption arc.”- You might refuse to read scrolls because “reading aloud always triggers curses.”- You might whisper “he’s the real villain” the moment you meet the king.
Blood Pact Effect:
You offered your past as part of the bargain—and now it isn’t entirely yours anymore.

Effect:
After the next long rest, the DM may change one detail about your character’s backstory.
You are aware that something has changed, but you don’t know what.

This change:
- Is permanent (unless changed again by future rests or spells)
- Can be minor or major (a name, a location, a relationship, a memory)
- This change is canon and ripples into the world.
- You can sense that something changed, but not what unless you investigate it.

You might feel déjà vu, confusion, strange memories, inconsistencies, or subtle emotional dissonance—but reality accepts the change without question.

Description:
You channel your arcane energy into pure style and physics- defying balance. As part of the spell, you perform an absolutely sick trick on a skateboard (or any similar celebrityl transport device). The trick defies gravity, logic, and taste. Everyone nearby feels its impact.

Roll a Performance (Charisma) check. Choose one of the following based on the result:- 15 or higher: A nearby crowd gathers. If 5 or more creatures visibly react with awe (DM’s discretion), you gain advantage on all Charisma checks and Charisma saving throws until your next long rest.- 10–14: The trick works, but only a few bystanders notice. You gain advantage on your next Charisma ability check.- Below 10: You still land it… barely. No benefit, but you avoid humiliation.

This spell grants no combat bonuses and immediately ends if cast in combat.
Clarification: - NPC reactions can be determined by a group Insight check or DM fiat.- Impressing elite socialites, hardened criminals, or nobles may require a higher Performance DC.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on all Charisma checks and Charisma saving throws

Description: You deliver a spectral strike that softens defenses. Make a melee spell attack against a creature. On a hit, the target takes 1d4 bludgeoning damage and has disadvantage on the next Strength check or Strength saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn.
Modifier Details: has disadvantage on the next Strength check or Strength saving throw

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Uses Bonus Action
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Uses Bonus Action
Description:
You conjure an ethereal tether and anchor it to a visible ledge or wall.
- You may instantly pull yourself up to 20 feet toward the anchor as part of the casting.
- For the spell’s duration, you or an ally may use a bonus action to pull themselves to the same anchor.
- If the anchor is removed or broken, the tether snaps.
Modifier Details: can instantly pull themselves up to 20 feet toward the anchor as part of the casting
Component Description (Material): a scrap of rope or wire

Description:
You launch a pulse of cursed energy in a straight 120- foot line, lashing through all creatures in its path. Each creature in the line must make a Constitution saving throw.

- On a failure, the creature becomes Cursed by the Lance for the duration and takes 2d8 chiaric damage. 
- On a success, the creature takes half damage and is not cursed.

While Cursed by the Lance:
- If the creature dies, its body detonates in a burst of radiant decay, dealing 2d6 chiaric damage to all creatures within 10 feet.
- Any creature killed by this explosion while also under the curse triggers a new detonation, potentially chaining across the battlefield.
- Cursed creatures emit a faint hum of inverted sound, and their veins glow faintly with impossible colors.
Scaling: When cast using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, the initial damage increases by 1d10 for each level beyond 7th, and the explosion damage increases by 1d6.
Flavor:
The rogue artificer Veyl Renshaw designed an enchanted device that could replicate Thallan's Consuming Lance. She activated it only once—her house was later found standing, but all its doors opened into an endless void.

To cast Thallan's Consuming Lance is to invite a presence that does not belong. The words taste of static, the gestures feel like echoes of hands not your own.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: 
A ghostly lance of light pierces the sky above you, flickering between real and not. For 1 round, you gain truesight in the direction the spell was cast.
Modifier Details: Constitution saving throw

Description:
You cast a handful of impossibly dry, crumbling soap into the air. It dissolves mid- flight, releasing microscopic flecks of cleansing hatred that scour body, soul, and shame alike.

Each creature in a 20- foot- radius sphere centered on you must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 11d4 biologic damage as the soap sears away impurities—real or imagined—with unrelenting force. On a successful save, they take half damage.
- Affected creatures are coated in a stinging residue for 1 minute. During this time, they emit a foul, antiseptic stench, suffer disadvantage on Charisma checks, and cannot benefit from healing spells unless they succeed on a Constitution saving throw at the start of their turn.
- Creatures without skin (undead, constructs, oozes) are immune to this spell.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly. One creature hit by the spell experiences a childhood memory of being forcefully scrubbed clean, even if they never had one.
Modifier Details: takes 11d4 biologic damage on failed save

Description:
You call upon the maddening echoes of the Craven Opal, forcing the minds of your enemies to grasp fragments of truths too vast to bear. Choose up to three creatures within range. Each must make an Intelligence saving throw.
- On a failure, a creature takes 5d8 cognitive damage and has(disadvantage on Intelligence and Wisdom saving throws) until the end of its next turn.
- On a success, it takes half damage and suffers no other effect.

While concentrating on this spell, you may use your action on subsequent turns to target the same or different creatures within range (up to three again). Each new wave of insight echoes the original effect—forcing a new save, new damage.
Scaling: When cast using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the initial damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4th.
Flavor:
Thallan was a scholar obsessed with knowledge not meant for mortals. When he finally beheld the Eye of the Craven Opal, his mind shattered, and his thoughts scattered into the void. Now, his name lingers in whispers, carried by unseen hands that claw at the minds of those who dare to reach for forbidden truths.

Legend says that Thallan's Hand of Craven Opal was first cast by the exiled wizard Iosephine Blackquill, who sought to trap a god within a single word. The spell succeeded—but the god did not remain silent for long.

The ancients who first uttered Thallan's Hand of Craven Opal did not record its effects, only their warnings: 'It does not unmake. It remembers. And it will remember you, too.'
Anomaly Effect: When you cast this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher, you hear your own name whispered backwards. Make an Intelligence saving throw (DC 15). On a failure, you are Stunned until the end of your next turn as impossible knowledge surges through your mind.
Modifier Details: disadvantage on Intelligence and Wisdom saving throws

Description:
You conjure an arcane seal of forgotten syllables—a 15- foot- radius circle inscribed with runes that shimmer like powdered saffron. The circle centers on you and moves with you. For the duration:

- Allied creatures within the circle gain a +5 bonus to damage on all weapon and spell attacks targeting enemies outside the circle. This damage is impact type and manifests as reverberating glyphs striking in tandem with the blow. 
- If this spell was cast using your final remaining spell slot, this bonus increases to +10, and the runes emit a low harmonic tone audible only to allies. 
- Enemies who enter or begin their turn within the circle must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or suffer disadvantage on all melee attacks until the start of their next turn. 
- Any creature affected by a Condition (such as Paranoid, Compelled, or Frightened) takes an additional 1d6 cognitive damage when hit by attacks made from within the circle.
Scaling: When cast with a spell slot of 8th level or higher, the radius increases by 5 feet per level, and the cognitive damage increases by 1d6 for each level above 7th.
Flavor:
Legend says that Thallan's Saffron Truth was first cast by the exiled wizard Iosephine Blackquill, who sought to trap a god within a single word. The spell succeeded—but the god did not remain silent for long.

The ancients who first uttered Thallan's Saffron Truth did not record its effects, only their warnings: 'It does not unmake. It remembers. And it will remember you, too.'
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: 
A shimmering sigil appears in the air above the circle. While it remains (1 round), all critical hits made from inside the circle deal maximum damage.
Modifier Details: additional 1d6 cognitive damage

Description:
- Any creature attempting to teleport, phase shift, or enter the area via extradimensional travel must succeed on a Charisma saving throw.
- On a failed save, they are forcibly bounced back to their point of origin and take 4d6 void damage as the firewall briefly stabilizes their reality—violently.
- Creatures that attempt to cross into the firewall without magical travel feel a pressure in the air but suffer no immediate effect unless the area is layered with other wards.
- The firewall emits a low- frequency pulse only detectable to those attuned to arcane signals. To most, it appears as empty space.
Notes: Missing a bit of info. and Is it a wall or a zone?
Flavor: Firewall protocols were originally adapted from Thaumatech incident logs and reverse- engineered from a failed dimensional quarantine. The area is considered mildly anomalous during the spell’s duration.
Modifier Details: creature attempting to teleport, phase shift, or enter the area via extradimensional travel must suc

Description:
You target a door, wall, or secure entryway within range that you or an ally has previously passed through. For the duration, you and up to five willing creatures can step through a flickering illusion of that entrance, appearing on the other side as if you’d bypassed its barriers.
- The entrance must be non- magical, or you must succeed on a DC 20 Arcana check to bypass magical locks.
- The illusionary gateway remains open for 10 seconds after the first creature steps through, then vanishes.
- Any creature attempting to follow after that must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw or be displaced—emerging up to 30 feet away in a random nearby location.
- Surveillance systems or magical wards detect your passage as a security anomaly, triggering local alerts unless suppressed beforehand.
Modifier Details: must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw
Component Description (Material): a key that no longer fits any lock

Description:
You draft a supernatural contract, binding the signee to a deal they cannot escape—either willingly or through legal loopholes within reality itself.
 
- The contract must specify clear, finite terms (e.g., “deliver the encrypted drive,” “never disclose my presence,” “report back monthly”). 
- A creature may willingly sign the contract or be coerced through a failed Wisdom saving throw.
- If the signee violates the terms, they begin to hemorrhage lifeforce, suffering 1d6 bleeding damage per hour until the contract is honored or the caster dissolves it.
- The contract cannot be undone by mundane means. It can only be nullified through a corporate tribunal, anomaly override, or higher- order reality arbitration.
Clarification: If the contract is poorly worded or vague, it will self- interpret using its own logic, often to the caster’s detriment. Corporate legal departments recommend all terms be double- verified before casting.
Anomaly Effect: - If poorly worded, the contract may self- interpret, leading to unintended consequences that the caster did not foresee.
Modifier Details: failed Wisdom saving throw
Component Description (Material): a quill, blood, and a binding sigil

Description:
You place a consuming need inside a creature, but it is not hunger for food—it is for something worse.
 
- The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they develop an insatiable hunger for a specific, forbidden thing, chosen randomly from the list below:
 
- The hunger cannot be resisted forever—every 24 hours without consuming the object, they take 2d6 cognitive damage.
- If they give in, they feel no satisfaction, only greater hunger.
Table:
| Roll | Side Effect Description |
|------ |--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | Flesh of the Living – The target craves human flesh. They can resist, but only barely. |
| 2 | Dead Voices – The target wants to chew on books, scrolls, and paper, consuming knowledge instead of food. |
| 3 | Their Own Body – The target begins biting and gnawing at their own hands, arms, and lips when no one is looking. |
| 4 | Glass and Metal – They find themselves chewing on shards of glass and rusted nails, feeling neither pain nor damage—but never satisfaction. |
| 5 | Forgotten Names – The target whispers the names of those who have been erased from history, consuming their legacies with each repetition. |
| 6 | Shadows – They try to eat lightless spaces, their mouth opening wider than it should when they stand in darkness. |
Anomaly Effect: - If multiple creatures are cursed in the same location, they begin to crave each other, driven to a shared consumption.
Modifier Details: target must make a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You conjure an unnerving mask over your face, obscuring your true identity.

- You gain advantage on Deception checks, but all social interactions become deeply unsettling.
- Creatures who see you for more than 1 minute must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 15) or develop a deep fear of you for the next 24 hours.
Modifier Details: creatures who see you for more than 1 minute must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 15) or develop a de
Component Description (Material): a mask that has never been worn

Description:
Your eyes become vessels of an unknowable force—an unblinking, paradoxical void that reflects nothing and sees everything. You focus this unnatural stare on a single creature within range.

The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or become Obsessed with your gaze. While obsessed, the creature:
- Cannot avert its eyes from you and cannot willingly move more than 30 feet away.
- Suffers disadvantage on Wisdom and Intelligence checks and saving throws as its thoughts loop in recursive terror.
- Takes 2d6 cognitive damage at the start of each of its turns as your gaze frays its mental barriers.

The creature may attempt another saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On a success, the effect ends.
Scaling:
At Higher Levels.
When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each level beyond 4th. At 6th level and above, the creature is also Frightened of you for the first round after failing.
Flavor:
The Veil Market traders whisper of an artifact that contains The Infinite Stare—a single black page that cannot be burned, torn, or marked. Those who read it vanish, leaving behind only the scent of ink and decay.

The Elders of the Concord Spire refuse to acknowledge The Infinite Stare, calling it 'a mistake in the fabric of the world.' Yet, the forbidden archives tell another story.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher:
Your eyes glow with ink- black light, and any creature that can see you must make a Wisdom saving throw or be unable to lie until the start of your next turn.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Charisma saving throw

Description:
Chaos hangs in the air, swirling with possibilities. You embrace the whimsy of chance, drawing power from fate’s unpredictable nature.

When you cast The Jester’s Wild Gambit, you surrender yourself to fate’s hands. Every time you make an attack roll or ability check, you may roll twice, taking the higher result. On a natural 20, the effect is magnified—doubling the result of your roll. But on a natural 1, something chaotic occurs. The DM may choose an unpredictable effect, such as disadvantage on your next roll, an unintended event that causes a distraction, or a minor but inconvenient consequence.
Scaling: At 5th level, fate’s unpredictability expands. You may force one enemy within 10 feet of you to reroll a successful saving throw once per turn, manifesting the whims of fortune upon your foes.
Flavor:
Legend says that The Jester's Wild Gambit was first cast by the exiled wizard Iosephine Blackquill, who sought to trap a god within a single word. The spell succeeded—but the god did not remain silent for long.

The ancients who first uttered The Jester's Wild Gambit did not record its effects, only their warnings: 'It does not unmake. It remembers. And it will remember you, too.'
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a roll of 15 or higher, you feel a slight disturbance in reality, as though an unseen force shifts in your favor. The outcome may feel more certain, but a lingering ripple remains in the air.
Modifier Details: can roll twice, taking the higher result
Component Description (Material): A playing card dipped in blood

Description:
You conjure a vast, spectral maw beneath you—its jagged teeth grinding the threads of reality. The air trembles with a silent hunger, and all creatures of your choice within a 30- foot radius must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or suffer the following effects:

- Their maximum hit points are reduced by 2d10. This reduction lasts until they complete a long rest. 
- They become Frightened of you until the end of your next turn. 
- Their healing is halved for the duration of the spell.

A creature that fails this save by 10 or more is also afflicted with the Altered condition for 1 minute, their features subtly reshaped by the hunger of the void.

While the spell is active, the ground pulses like a throat, and creatures who begin their turn within the area take 1d6 chiaric damage as spectral teeth snap at their soul.
Scaling: At 9th level, the radius increases to 60 feet.
Flavor:
A beggar on the streets of Concord claims to have been a king before uttering the words of The Maw That Hungers. When asked for details, his mouth moves, but no sound emerges.

Some claim that The Maw That Hungers does not work the same way twice. The more one casts it, the more it adapts—learning, watching, waiting.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Constitution saving throw
Component Description (Material): A shard of bone gnawed by an unknown entity

Description:
You infuse the Veins of Lament into a kiss, channeling raw pain through the moment of contact. When you touch a creature with your lips (or simulate the act with a somatic gesture), your magic delivers a pulse of internal anguish.

The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 1d3 chiaric damage plus 3 damage per caster level (maximum 1d3 + 20). On a successful save, the creature takes half damage.

This kiss leaves no visible wound, but the target feels as if something delicate within them cracked.
Flavor: Practiced in secret by jaded lovers, dark poets, and those who trade beauty for brutality, the Kiss of Wounding is both a spell and a statement.
Modifier Details: target must make a Dexterity saving throw

Description:
You strip power from a target’s true name, making them vulnerable.

- The target has disadvantage on all saving throws against effects that explicitly name them in their casting, inscription, or invocation (such as through a name spoken aloud, signed in a document, or used in a ritual).
- Any creature who speaks the target’s name aloud can sense the direction (not exact location) of the target within 1 mile, provided the target is on the same plane.
- This effect ends if the name is burned, rewritten, legally nullified, or exchanged through sanctioned rites (as negotiated through an A.S.T.R.A.L. broker or similar agent).
Modifier Details: has disadvantage on all saving throws against effects that explicitly name them in their casting, in
Component Description (Material): a legal document dissolving the target’s identity, signed in willing blood
Acquisition Note: True name etchings must be obtained through sanctioned rites or eldritch brokers.

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Grants Advantage
Description:
