You conjure a colossal spectral spear—a jagged relic made from the reigns of long- erased monarchs and the regrets that bound them. The spear surges forward in a straight line up to 120 feet long and 5 feet wide.

All creatures in the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 6d10 chiaric damage. A creature reduced to 0 hit points by this spell has its soul torn from memory and imprisoned within a spectral throne—unable to be resurrected or contacted by any means short of a *Wish* or *Sovereign Accord*.

Success: A creature takes half damage and is not affected by the soul imprisonment effect.

While affected by this spell:
- The area traversed by the spear echoes faintly with overlapping royal decrees in unknown tongues. 
- Any creature standing within 10 feet of the line for the next minute suffers disadvantage on Charisma checks, as their voice seems small and forgotten. 
- You may sense the presence of monarchic or noble bloodlines within range for 1 round, as they resonate with the spear’s memory.
Table: The Veil Market traders whisper of an artifact that contains The Spear of Forgotten Kings—a single black page that cannot be burned, torn, or marked. Those who read it vanish, leaving behind only the scent of ink and decay.
Scaling:
At Higher Levels. 
When cast with a 9th-level spell slot:
- The damage increases to 8d10 chiaric.
- You may designate up to two lines extending from you in separate directions.
- Any creature slain by this spell also causes one creature of your choice within 30 feet to become Frightened of you for 1 minute (Wisdom save negates).
Flavor: The Veil Market traders whisper of an artifact that contains The Spear of Forgotten Kings—a single black page that cannot be burned, torn, or marked. Those who read it vanish, leaving behind only the scent of ink and decay.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: 
A ghostly coronation takes place in silence at the end of the line—an echo of an ancient ruler briefly manifesting. All who see it must succeed a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or fall prone in reverence or horror.
Modifier Details: takes 6d10 chiaric damage on failure
Component Description (Material): A rusted crown from a long- dead ruler

Description:
You invite a spirit back into the world of the living, but only for a limited time.
 
- The summoned spirit returns in physical form for 24 hours.
- They remember everything from their death and are aware that this is temporary.
- At the end of the spell’s duration, the spirit knows who summoned them—and may not be happy about it.
Component Description (Material): a candle made from human tallow, burned over the target’s unburied remains
Acquisition Note: True Name Etchings are highly dangerous and must be carved into the spirit’s remains before summoning. Without both a Blood Pact and True Name Etching, the spirit returns malformed, vengeful, or hollow.

Description:
You grant a creature the blessing of the Ticking Man, a timeless entity whose debts must always be paid.
 

- The target gains +1 to AC and saving throws while the spell is active.
- At any time within 24 hours, the Ticking Man may call in the favor, forcing the target to perform one action or request, no matter the cost.
- If the request is refused, the target immediately ages 2d20 years and loses the benefits of the spell.
Modifier Details: +1 to ac
Component Description (Material): a pocket watch with no hands

Description:
You draw corrupted strands of the Tesserac Weft together, weaving them into a semi-sentient construct of scrap, forgotten designs, and abandoned intent. The construct hauls itself into being as a towering assemblage of warped metal, bound memories, and tesseral tension. It obeys your spoken commands without hesitation, sharing your initiative and acting immediately after your turn.

The construct’s blows land with crushing tesseral force, and its unstable core occasionally vents Everbright energy in violent arcs. Though formidable, its mind is flawed. At the start of each of its turns, the Weft strains against its form, and on a moment of instability it lashes inward, wracking the construct with cognitive backlash and erratic behavior.

At higher invocations, you reinforce the weave, granting the construct greater endurance and deadlier precision as more strands of the Weft are forced into alignment.

 Mech Stats 
Medium or Large Construct, AC 17, HP 85 
Speed 30 ft., Climb 15 ft. 
STR 18, DEX 10, CON 16, INT 3, WIS 10, CHA 3 
Saving Throws: Con +6 
Damage Immunities: Poison, Psychic 
Condition Immunities: Charmed, Frightened, Poisoned 
Senses: Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 10 
Languages: Understands the caster's commands, but cannot speak 

 Actions

Force Slam. *Melee Weapon Attack:* +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. 
Hit: 3d10 force damage.

Wrist Cannon (Recharge 5–6). 
The mech fires an unstable arcane round in a 30- foot line that is 5 feet wide. 
Each creature in the line must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 16), taking 4d8 radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

Interpose Shield (1/turn). 
When a creature within 5 feet is hit by an attack, the mech can intercept the blow, reducing the damage by 2d10 + its Constitution modifier.
Scaling:
At Higher Levels. 
When cast using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the mech gains an extra 25 hit points, its AC increases by 1, and it deals +1 damage with all attacks per slot level above 6th.
Anomaly Effect: At the start of each of its turns, roll a d6. On a roll of 1, the mech glitches, taking 1d8 cognitive damage and gaining the *Paranoid* condition until the end of its next turn.
Modifier Details: +8 to hit
Component Description (Material): a broken radio and a strip of military- grade tape

Description:
You plant a subtle psychic thread into the mind of a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on an **Intelligence saving throw** or become **Seeded** for the duration.

While Seeded:
- You can hear **surface thoughts** (what they are actively thinking).
- You know the creature’s **emotional state** and any strong **intent** involving you or your allies.
- As a bonus action on your turn, you may:
- Ask the GM one **yes/no question** the creature knows the answer to.
- Whisper a **single word or phrase** into their mind, which they perceive as their own thought.

A creature that succeeds on the save is immune to your Thoughtseed for 24 hours. 
This spell has **no visual or audible effect** and cannot be detected by mundane means.
Modifier Details: creature is immune to Thoughtseed for 24 hours

Description:
You bind a willing creature’s thread to a prepared point of safety within range. At any moment while the binding persists, the creature may sever its current position and snap instantly along the Loom to the designated location.

The recall may be invoked only once per casting. The passage leaves a faint disturbance in the surrounding Weft, making repeated use increasingly noticeable to those who watch for sudden absences and returns.

With a sharp pose and a surge of magic, you conjure a flawless fit. The target’s attire realigns into immaculate tailoring—custom, luxurious, and sharp as their mood.
- While affected, the target:
- Gains advantage on Charisma (Performance, Intimidation, or Persuasion) checks made to impress, seduce, or intimidate.
- Radiates undeniable confidence and poise.
- Exudes a faint supernatural polish—fabric shimmers, collars hold, and hems never fold.

When cast on an unwilling creature, they must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or:
- Their clothing constricts for a moment, imposing disadvantage on their next Strength or Dexterity- based check or save.
- They suffer 1d4 cognitive damage from the sudden discomfort and embarrassment.
Scaling: If cast using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the outfit is also instantly cleaned, mended, and altered into any cut, color, or fashion style desired—from tactical opera- wear to battlefield haute couture.
Flavor: In Concord City, it’s often used in high- fashion duels and rogue theater circles.- Casting Tight before a date or duel is considered polite.
Modifier Details: gains advantage on Charisma (Performance, Intimidation, or Persuasion) checks

Description: The target’s short- term memory floods with useless data. The affected creature must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, they roll 1d6 and subtract it from all attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws for the duration. The effect ends early if the target takes cognitive damage.
Modifier Details: must make an Intelligence saving throw

Description:
You release a parasitic curse woven from the hunger of haunted moss. Choose a point within range. Any number of creatures within a 15- foot- radius sphere centered on that point must make a Constitution saving throw.

On a failed save:
- For the duration, the next 45 hit points of healing the target would receive are stolen. Instead of regaining hit points, you regain that amount instead, and the target receives nothing.
- Each time this transfer occurs, spectral moss visibly spreads along the target’s skin or clothing.
- While affected, targets have disadvantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration on spells.

On a success:
- The creature is unaffected, but faint lichen- like patterns momentarily crawl across their skin before vanishing.

The spell ends early for a creature when it has had 45 HP of healing siphoned, when it drops to 0 hit points, or if you lose concentration.
Scaling:
At Higher Levels. 
When cast using a 9th-level spell slot:
- The siphon cap increases to 90 hit points per target. 
- You may choose up to two separate areas to apply this effect simultaneously (each requiring concentration to be maintained independently).
Flavor:
> “They say the moss grows where the dead still think of life—and that Tortak found a way to make it drink instead.” 
> — Folk saying, forbidden in Concord’s inner districts
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: 
A spectral overgrowth of bioluminescent moss blooms within the area. All creatures inside the sphere must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you until the start of your next turn.
Modifier Details: has disadvantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration on spells

Description:
You touch the target's flesh, feeling their breath flutter like candlelight in the abyss.
 
- The target must make a Constitution saving throw (- 2 penalty). On a failure, they drown instantly, as if submerged in unseen waters.
- Those who survive describe a vision of something immense, watching from beneath the surface.
- Those who fail leave behind no corpse, only a damp outline where they once stood.

 Some whisper that victims of this spell are not truly dead, but merely waiting… elsewhere.
Modifier Details: target must make a Constitution saving throw with a -2 penalty

Description:
You gesture toward a nearby stray creature—be it a cat, pigeon, squirrel, rat, or even a raccoon—and imbue it with trace amounts of venomous energy. For the duration, the creature becomes semi- magical and mildly dangerous in unpredictable ways.- Choose or roll for a nearby unclaimed beast CR 0 or lower within range.- That creature becomes your semi- loyal venomous companion for the spell’s duration.- The creature cannot speak, but it understands your intent and can take basic commands (such as “harass,” “follow,” or “bite”).

While venomous, it gains:- A bite or claw attack that deals 1d6 toxic damage in addition to normal damage.- Creatures damaged by it must succeed a Constitution saving throw or suffer disadvantage on their next attack roll or skill check (your choice).

If reduced to 0 HP or dismissed, it explodes in a puff of neon- green spores and glittery hair, imposing disadvantage on Perception checks in a 5- ft radius until the end of the turn.
Table:
| d6 | Quirk While Poisoned |
|---- |--------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | It glows faintly green and refuses to blink. |
| 2 | It purrs aggressively when attacking. |
| 3 | Its venom smells like bubblegum. |
| 4 | It sings very faint lullabies only the caster can hear. |
| 5 | It develops a temporary monocle. |
| 6 | It explodes into confetti upon vanishing. |
Flavor: This spell is commonly used by street witches, dumpster mystics, and one particularly infamous pastry chef in Neon Mire.
Modifier Details: must succeed a Constitution saving throw

Description: You tap the ground, sending a ripple of energy through the surface. A creature standing on solid ground within range must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or momentarily lose balance, forcing it to make its next attack roll or Dexterity check with disadvantage. If the terrain is loose or unstable, the effect is automatically successful.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw

Description:
You exhale a slow, intoxicating breath, sending whispers of carnal truth and romantic echoes drifting into the target’s aura. The magic doesn’t extract facts—it invites revelation.

A silken mirage unfolds, where the subject’s soul steps forward, cloaked in glimmering honesty. Faces, forms, emotions, and gestures emerge from the haze—some familiar, some unknown—each representing a truth they’ve always carried.

The spell may reveal one or more of the following aspects, depending on your focus:

Gender Identity: The target's inner identity manifests briefly as a mirage, affirming their true self. This may reveal identities such as Male, Female, Nonbinary, Transgender, Cisgender, Two- Spirit, Genderfluid, Agender, or something uniquely celebrityl and beyond typical categories.

Romantic Inclination: You awaken a subtle echo within the target’s spirit, reflecting their romantic nature. This might indicate whether they identify as Hyper- Romantic, Romantic, Demi- Romantic, Grayromantic, Aromantic, or something entirely undefined.

Sexual Orientation: A fleeting, sensory dreamscape gently unfolds around the target, reflecting their deepest attractions. They may acknowledge, embrace, or gently dismiss these visions. The spell doesn't impose new desires; it simply illuminates those already present.

Position Preference: A subtle sensation reveals the subject's preferred intimacy position, such as Top, Bottom, Versatile, Vers- Top, Vers- Bottom, Side, or an unclassified, uniquely celebrityl preference.

Fetish Affinity: A delicate hum pulses into the subject’s subconscious, drawing forth hidden motifs of pleasure—those they cherish, those they deny, and those that whisper only in dreams. This may reveal affinities such as Bondage, Impact Play, Sensory Deprivation, Exhibitionism, Voyeurism, Power Exchange, Pet Play, or something undocumented by common texts.

Kink Role Alignment: A soft whisper coils through the subject’s awareness, coaxing insight into their innate position within dominance- submission dynamics. They may momentarily experience themselves in a state of control, surrender, or fluidity. Whether they resist, acknowledge, or embrace this vision, the spell merely opens a door—it does not push them through it.

The experience is not invasive—it is affirming, deeply celebrityl, and laced with quiet reverence. The subject may resist this unveiling with a Wisdom saving throw; on a success, the vision remains fragmented, and only a vague sense of their essence slips through.

This spell does not coerce. It does not judge. It simply illuminates what was already burning softly beneath the surface.
Clarification:
This spell respects the target's autonomy and does not alter or coerce identities or preferences; it merely reveals truths already held.

Always ensure sensitive handling of these revelations at your table, prioritizing player comfort and respectful roleplay.
Component Description (Material): a shard of polished obsidian kissed once, a rose petal burned to ash, and a silk ribbon tied and untied around a drop of honeyed wine

Description:
You project a metaphysical failsafe that prevents involuntary memory alteration and data corruption within a 10- foot radius sphere.

- Any attempt to erase, fragment, or overwrite memories in this area automatically fails.
- Spells like Memory Fragmentation, Compliance Protocol, and A.S.T.R.A.L. Redaction are nullified within the field.
- Digital and paper records within the field are temporarily immune to deletion by magical or technological means.
Scaling:
- At 7th level, the radius extends to 30 feet, covering an entire room.
- At 9th level, the effect becomes permanent in a fixed location, creating an anti- redaction zone.

Description:
A pulse of low- frequency nerve stimulation targets a muscle group or sensitive area, triggering a sharp jolt of sensation akin to a goosebump- inducing touch.

- The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or experience an involuntary twitch, shiver, or sharp intake of breath, losing their next reaction.

This spell is often misused in underground establishments, enhancing paid experiences or delivering unexpected sensations.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Constitution saving throw

Description:
You rewrite the obedience of matter itself, compelling solid substance to part and flow aside like water under command.
	•	Choose a solid, nonmagical wall, vault, or reinforced surface no more than 5 feet thick. You open a 10-foot-long, 5-foot-wide passage, the material folding away in rippling bands of pale, unstable light.
	•	The passage shimmers faintly, semi-translucent, as though reality itself is uncertain it should exist.
	•	The opening seals itself after 1 round unless you maintain concentration, in which case it remains for the spell’s duration.
	•	The passage cannot be formed through living creatures or organic matter.

Warded or Enchanted Surfaces:
If the surface is protected by magic, make an Arcana check (DC = 10 + the level of the ward). On a failure, the spell collapses harmlessly, leaving the surface untouched.
Scaling: When cast using a 6th-level spell slot or higher, the maximum surface thickness increases by 5 feet for each slot level above 5th.
Modifier Details: make an Arcana check (DC = 10 + the level of the ward)

Description:
By touching a creature or object, you tug loose concealed threads from the Loom, allowing buried truths to bleed outward as telepathic murmurs. Fragments of memory, history, and intent spill into the minds of all creatures within 30 feet, carried on overlapping whispers that cannot be fully silenced.

If the target is a creature, it must make a Wisdom saving throw or be compelled to speak truthfully for the duration, unable to withhold or disguise what the unsealed threads reveal. The whispers fade when the spell ends, but what was heard cannot be forgotten.
Modifier Details: must make a Wisdom saving throw
Acquisition Note: Repeated use may attract corporate counter- infiltration specialists or trigger a data breach investigation.

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Grants Advantage
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Grants Advantage
Description:
You conjure a shroud of arcane interference that fouls the act of observation itself. Sightlines blur, divinatory senses slip, and memory refuses to hold what should have been seen.

For the duration, the following effects apply within the area:
	•	Divination magic used to observe or record the area suffers disadvantage, as visions fracture into static impressions, echoes, or false overlays.
	•	Magical sensors, scrying eyes, familiars used as observers, and similar effects fail to relay clear information, instead looping old images, fading to darkness, or presenting distorted scenes.
	•	Any creature attempting to observe the area remotely through magic must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be unable to recall what they perceived, retaining only vague impressions or missing time.

This spell does not render creatures invisible. Direct, unaided sight functions normally, but any attempt to watch, record, or verify events through supernatural means becomes deeply unreliable, as though reality itself refuses to be properly witnessed.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You loosen your presence from all forms of recorded recognition, rendering your thread unreadable to systems that rely on ordered perception. For the duration, the affected creature cannot be perceived by divinatory constructs, ritual sensors, or Erudit-maintained observation wards.

Any attempt to observe the creature through indirect or automated means produces static impressions, looping moments, or blank intervals in perception. Creatures relying on direct sight, sound, or touch remain unaffected and can interact normally.

If the creature deliberately interacts with a warded or observed mechanism, the system may register disruption or error, but never a discernible identity.

You align your body, mind, and bladder with an invisible thread of inevitability. For the duration, cosmic forces ensure your next strike is absurdly accurate—but only under strangely specific conditions.

Until the spell ends, your next attack roll automatically hits a target of your choice if any of the following are true at the time of the attack:
- You are actively urinating.
- You are within 5 feet of someone who is.
- You are standing inside a bathroom, restroom, washroom, or similarly designated facility—regardless of plumbing status.

If none of these conditions are true, you must spend an action miming the act of relieving yourself (DMs are encouraged to enforce social consequences) to activate the effect. Once the effect is used, the spell ends
Scaling: When cast using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you may choose two attacks within the duration to benefit from this effect instead of one.
Modifier Details: automatically hits a target of your choice

Description:
You intrude upon a creature’s decision-making, threading foreign intent through its thoughts until choice and command become indistinguishable. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw or be compelled to follow your spoken orders as if they were its own decisions.

While affected, the creature genuinely believes its actions are self-directed. A creature already under another compulsion effect automatically fails its initial saving throw. At the end of each of its turns, the creature may attempt a new Intelligence saving throw to reclaim control.
Modifier Details: must make an Intelligence saving throw
Component Description (Material): a high- ranking corporate ID badge, registered to the caster
Acquisition Note: Use of unauthorized compulsion spells within corporate zones is classified as Executive Manipulation Fraud.

Description:
You invoke a forbidden remnant of ancient magic, drawing from the chilling legacy known as Utallash’s Pursuit of Ceramic. Choose one creature within range; 
it must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the target suffers the following effects:
- The target takes 4d8 distortive damage as their identity begins to fracture, leaving a hollow, ceramic
- like resonance within their being.- They become a “Husk”: For the next hour, the target struggles to remember their own name, imposing disadvantage on all Charisma- based checks and saving throws.

Marked by the Void (Caster’s Consequences):
Every time you cast this spell, you become subtly marked by the magic. The mark grows stronger with repeated uses. You experience minor, unsettling phenomena:
- Your reflection occasionally blinks or moves independently.
- Your shadow periodically misaligns or lags behind your physical form.

After you cast this spell 3 or more times, you gain disadvantage on Charisma saving throws until you complete a long rest, as the magic slowly erodes your own identity.

These effects are cumulative but subtle enough to be hidden from casual observers—though those familiar with the Void’s whispers will recognize your affliction immediately.

Removing the Mark:
A Greater Restoration or similarly potent cleansing magic removes all accumulated marks from you, resetting the count.
Flavor:
Something unknown has touched this magic, leaving behind traces of those who have cast it before.

During the Calamity of Shrouded Stars, Utallash's Persuit of Ceramic was unleashed upon a battlefield, reducing an entire legion to nameless husks. Those who survived swore they heard their own names whispered by the void.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: target makes a Charisma saving throw
Component Description (Material): currant leaves or a little vial of paint

Description:
You momentarily slip your thread beneath the Veil of recognition, causing the Loom to lose sight of your movement. For the duration, divination magic, scrying effects, and tracking rites cannot perceive you.

If you were already being observed or followed, the last recorded position of your thread remains fixed, as though you never moved beyond that point. Any attempt to reestablish your trail through magical tracking fails unless the caster succeeds on an Intelligence saving throw against your spell save DC, as the Erudits’ registries refuse to update your position.
Modifier Details: the caster succeeds on an Intelligence saving throw against your spell save DC
Trigger: when targeted by surveillance or tracking

Description:
You loosen your thread from the Loom and allow it to drift unpredictably across the Veil. At the start of each of your turns, roll a d6 as the Veil resettles around you:
	•	On a 1–2, the Veil thickens defensively, granting you resistance to all damage.
	•	On a 3–4, your thread snaps sideways through the Veil, teleporting you 30 feet in a random direction.
	•	On a 5–6, the Veil obscures your presence entirely, rendering you invisible until the start of your next turn.

Each shift feels uncontrolled, as though the Veil itself is deciding how best to accommodate your unstable presence.
Modifier Details: grants resistance to all damage

Description:
You partially displace your body across the Veil, causing your presence to blur between states of recognition. For the duration, you move through crowds without drawing notice unless actively sought, slipping past attention as though your thread refuses to catch the eye.

You may pass through fragile barriers such as glass, light fencing, or unreinforced doors, and you leave no trace upon pressure-sensitive wards, ritual scans, or motion-detecting enchantments. To the world, you are not invisible, but unregistered, and therefore unseen.
Modifier Details: no notice unless actively sought

Foundry Tag: Modifies AC, Creates Light, Uses Bonus Action
Unnamed: 5: Modifies AC, Creates Light, Uses Bonus Action
Description: Your body shimmers with ethereal light, briefly phasing between reality and the unknown. For the duration, you can dim or brighten your own appearance at will, causing yourself to glow faintly (shedding dim light in a 5- foot radius) or become shadowed (light sources within 10 feet of you appear dimmer). This effect ends if you fall unconscious or dismiss it as a bonus action.
Modifier Details: does not affect saving throws

Description:
You seed a false proclamation directly into the telepathic currents that carry shared thought and rumor. For the duration, a prepared message manifests simultaneously in the minds of creatures within range, presented as an authentic announcement, warning, or authoritative declaration.

The message feels real and unchallenged unless a listener succeeds on an Intelligence (Investigation) check (DC 15), sensing contradictions or unnatural gaps in the thought-stream. Attempts to trace the source encounter only dissipating echoes in the Weft, as the originating thread slips away before it can be followed.
Modifier Details: requires an Intelligence (Investigation) check with DC 15

Description:
By touching a prepared focus, you weave a brief message of up to twenty-five words directly into the Loom and guide it along hidden telepathic channels toward a specific recipient. The message arrives instantly as a clear thought in the recipient’s mind, spoken in your mental voice.

The transmission leaves no physical trace, no record within Erudit registries, and no lingering signature once delivered. If the recipient is fully severed from telepathic or planar connectivity, the dispatch fails harmlessly, the thread dissolving before it can reach its destination.

You utter a single word carried directly through telepathic channels, comprehensible only to the intended target. To all others, the sound fractures into meaningless distortion or incomprehensible murmuring. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or obey the command on its next turn, as though compelled by a direct imperative of thought.

If the saving throw succeeds, the target senses that its will was briefly tested, though it gains no knowledge of the source. Observers may recognize that magic was invoked, but cannot discern the content or intent of the command. When cast at greater power, the utterance leaves no sound, sign, or lingering trace beyond the shared thought itself.
Scaling: When cast using a spell slot of 3nd level or higher, the spell becomes entirely undetectable to anyone except the caster and target—leaving no sound, no visual components, and no magical trace behind.
Modifier Details: target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You raise a bastion of folded Veil in a 30-foot radius centered on yourself. For the duration, the area becomes warded against external interference.

Forced teleportation, extradimensional travel, scrying, and mind-altering effects originating from outside the area automatically fail. Attempts to manipulate reality within the ward also fail unless the source of the effect originates from a creature of higher level than you.

You allow your veins to forget their purpose, turning your circulatory system into something liquid, shifting, and unknowable.
 

- For the duration of the spell, your blood is no longer entirely inside you—it slithers beneath your skin, forming moving patterns, and drips onto the floor in places you haven't stepped.
- You gain the following benefits and consequences:
- Your body is semi- fluid, allowing you to squeeze through gaps no wider than 1 inch.
- You are immune to poison and diseases, as your blood is no longer organic.
- You take half damage from slashing attacks, but piercing attacks deal double damage as your body cannot hold shape properly.
- Each time you cast a spell, your blood reacts violently, causing you to lose 1d6 HP per spell level.
- If you reach 0 HP while this spell is active, you do not die—instead, your blood scatters across the room, and your body must be reassembled manually.
 

 Anomaly Effect:
- If used too frequently, your blood refuses to return to your body, forming an independent entity that watches you, waiting for an opportunity to replace you.
Anomaly Effect: - If used too frequently, your blood refuses to return to your body, forming an independent entity that watches you, waiting for an opportunity to replace you.
Modifier Details: takes half damage from slashing attacks
Component Description (Material): a drop of your own blood, mixed with ink

Description:
With a deliberate and hypnotic performance, you begin a sensual dance designed to captivate a single creature of your choice within range. As part of the performance, you must remove one article of clothing per round. Each round the dance continues:
- The target must make a Wisdom saving throw, suffering a 
– 1 penalty for each round you’ve maintained the dance.
- On a failed save, the target becomes Charmed and fully fixated on your every movement, unable to willingly look away or take hostile actions against you for the duration.
- If the target is already Charmed by you when this spell is cast, the duration extends by 1 additional minute.

The spell ends early if the caster is interrupted, breaks concentration, or voluntarily stops dancing.
Flavor: “Influence consultants” claim this spell’s real magic lies not in the removal of garments, but in the power of anticipation.
Modifier Details: suffers a -1 penalty to Wisdom saving throws
Component Description (Material): an article of clothing to be removed

Description:
You ironically invoke nature in the concrete- dominated Concord City, spontaneously causing lichen and moss to rapidly grow and spread in a 30- foot radius around you, covering surfaces and structures alike.

Choose one creature within range (including yourself). If the target is willing, it gains a friendly affinity with this newly grown environment: the lichen subtly helps them navigate and move effortlessly, granting them advantage on Dexterity checks within the area.

An unwilling creature must make a Wisdom saving throw or become cursed by the aggressive lichen growth, which actively attempts to hinder and trip them, imposing disadvantage on Dexterity checks and saving throws within the affected area.

The effect lasts for up to 1 hour or until dispelled.

Additionally, while within the area of the spell, affected creatures occasionally notice flickering glimpses of a serene, lush, and seemingly para- dimensional park hidden within reality. They never fully access this realm, experiencing only a tantalizing vision of what Concord City might have been.
Flavor: A heavily censored A.S.T.R.A.L. document refers cryptically to experiments involving “environmental stabilization anomalies,” mentioning a phenomenon that “defied categorization—neither boon nor curse—but rewrote fate itself.”
Modifier Details: must make a Wisdom saving throw
Component Description (Material): a small piece of lichen or moss

Description:
You awaken the buried will of nature beneath stone and ruin, commanding life to reclaim what was taken from it.

Twisting vines, iron-hard moss, and thorned growth burst from cracked stone and worked ground, spreading across the area.
	•	The terrain becomes choked with overgrowth, hindering movement.
	•	Creatures caught within must struggle against binding plants that seize limbs and armor alike.
	•	You may will the growth to crawl across walls or floors, extending its grasp.
	•	Constructs and creatures of purely inorganic nature are especially vulnerable, as the living world rejects their presence.

The spell transforms ruin into a battlefield where stone remembers it was once soil.
Modifier Details: creatures caught within must struggle against binding plants that seize limbs and armor alike

Description:
You touch a patch of resilient green—be it moss growing through cracked pavement or ivy clinging to concrete—and draw strength from its quiet persistence.

Upon casting this spell, a subtle green glow briefly pulses around your feet and chest. For the next minute:- You gain resistance to poison damage.- You have advantage on Constitution saving throws made to resist or recover from exhaustion.

This effect represents the restorative essence of hardy flora—clinging life that thrives in adversity, even in forgotten corners of the city.
Flavor: Verdant Renewal is a hallmark 0-Cantrip of druids who walk the forgotten arteries of modern sprawl—those who find nature not in forests, but in the weeds in gutters, moss in subways, and roots beneath asphalt. These druids believe resilience is nature’s truest gift—and draw upon it to steady their own breath in moments of fatigue and illness.
Modifier Details: has advantage on Constitution saving throws made to resist or recover from exhaustion

Description:
You invoke a judgment long forgotten, compelling reality itself to erase the target. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, they take 10d10 chiaric damage, and all creatures within 30 feet must make a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened for 1 minute.

If the target is reduced to 0 HP by this spell, their body disintegrates into nothing, and their existence is stricken from minor recorded history (casual memories fade, but deep records remain).
Scaling: At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 per slot level above 6th.
Modifier Details: target must make a Wisdom saving throw
Component Description (Material): a relic of an erased historical figure

Description: All creatures in range are cured of diseases, infections, and parasitic infestations. If a creature is actively suffering from a necrotic or viral- based curse, this spell grants advantage on saving throws against it for the next 24 hours.
Modifier Details: grants advantage on saving throws against necrotic or viral-based curses for the next 24 hours

Description:
You press your lips to the target’s skin—cheek, brow, hand, or lips. They do not cry out. They do not resist. There is no flash of light, no scream, no struggle.

There is only absence.- A creature with 150 hit points or fewer instantly dies.- After 1d10 rounds, their body crumbles into dust, leaving no corpse, no remains, and no residue.- They cannot be resurrected by any means—not by Wish, True Resurrection, or divine intervention. Their soul is simply gone, erased from all known realms.- Creatures immune to necrotic damage or undeath are not immune to this spell. It bypasses resistance and death saves.
Flavor: Some say the victims of this spell are not destroyed—but taken. To where, none dare ask.

Description:
You conjure an illusory wall up to 5 feet wide, 10 feet tall, and 1 inch thick made entirely of bad visual gags and slapstick nonsense. Rubber chickens, cream pies, banana peels, and invisible panes of glass loop endlessly in magical animation. The wall occasionally emits groan- worthy puns, honking horns, or laugh tracks from nowhere.

The wall is not solid and can be walked through—but creatures who approach it often don’t realize it’s fake.

When a creature comes within 5 feet of the wall for the first time on a turn or starts its turn near it, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become incapacitated with uncontrollable laughter until the start of its next turn.

A creature that investigates the wall (as an action) may make another Wisdom saving throw. On a success, it sees through the illusion—the wall disappears for that creature only.

The spell ends early if you lose concentration or dismiss it.
Flavor: Originally created by court jesters- turned- clerics as divine protest theater. Still banned in several religious institutions due to a pie- related incident during communion.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
When a spell is cast nearby, you instinctively deflect attention by projecting a spectral version of yourself into a nearby corner. The illusory duplicate radiates an aura of intense social discomfort, misdirecting attention away from you.

For the duration, creatures within 30 feet must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw when they attempt to target you with an attack, spell, or ability. On a failure, they ignore you entirely, unable to focus on your presence unless you directly harm them or force a saving throw.

This is not invisibility—you remain visible, but socially unnoticeable in a way that feels wrong. Even allies may forget you’re present.
Flavor: A fragment of a partially erased A.S.T.R.A.L. log refers to this phenomenon as a “psycho- social misdirection glyph.” It notes one incident where an entire strike team entered a room, saw the caster, and left convinced it was unoccupied—twice.
Anomaly Effect:
When you cast this spell, roll a d6 to determine a subtle anomaly that persists while the spell is active:
1.People ignore you, even if you scream.
2.Your voice echoes from the wrong direction.
3.Your reflection disappears in all mirrors.
4.Written text about you vanishes or becomes unreadable.
5.Animals refuse to come near you.
6.Anyone who sees you forgets details about your appearance within seconds.
Modifier Details: creatures within 30 feet must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw
Trigger: when a spell is cast nearby

Description:
You brush your fingers gently across the target’s skin, and for a moment, their body opens itself to your senses—like a lover whispering its deepest discomforts.

You detect any sexual or intimacy- related ailments currently affecting the creature, whether physical, psychological, emotional, or arcane. This includes but is not limited to:
- Venereal diseases, mundane or magical
- Hormonal imbalances, arousal anomalies, arcane sterility
- Psychological blocks, trauma- induced aversions, or compulsions
- Magically- induced attractions, false bonds, or enchantments
- Anomalous intimacy curses (e.g., “can’t touch anyone,” “always fall in love,” etc.)

Each issue is revealed in poetic, metaphorical terms, such as:

“The heart is sealed with wax,” or “There is a hunger with no source.”

With a DC 12 Wisdom (Medicine or Insight) check, you may interpret one affliction literally.

An afflicted creature feels a brief sensation of heat or shiver, depending on the nature of the ailment. Some report hearing a whisper from within themselves.
Anomaly Effect: Some psychological conditions may emerge for the first time only after this spell is cast. These effects often manifest as soft compulsions or disturbing dreams.

Foundry Tag: Modifies AC, Creates Light, Uses Bonus Action
Unnamed: 5: Modifies AC, Creates Light, Uses Bonus Action
Description:
You remotely manipulate the fastenings of a creature’s outfit—zippers slip, laces untie, clasps flick open or shut, or corsets snap with whispered precision.
- You may don or doff any simple clothing as a bonus action, including on a willing creature within range.
- If cast on an unwilling creature, they must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have part of their clothing shift, loosen, or fall apart (purely cosmetic, unless specified).
- Subtle castings require a Wisdom (Insight) check against your spell save DC to notice the alteration.

At Higher Tiers:
- If the caster is level 5 or higher, the spell can affect light armor.
- At level 10, it can affect medium or heavy armor, but doing so requires a Strength (Athletics) check (DC 15) to undo or resecure the armor manually.
Scaling:
Scaling (At Higher Levels):
The spell’s damage increases when you reach higher levels:- 5th level: 2d6- 11th level: 3d6- 17th level: 4d6
Flavor: Druids of the Concrete Vein speak of the city’s pulse as if it were a living thing. Where others see decay, they find breath. Vital Pulse is their heartbeat—an echo of nature breaking through the scars of civilization.
Modifier Details: unwilling creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw

Description:
You flood a localized area with an excess of arcane weave, forcing incompatible strands of reality to occupy the same space. Within this zone, magic does not fail because it is suppressed, but because it is overwritten by too many simultaneous truths.

Spells unravel before they can fully resolve, ongoing enchantments flicker and lose coherence, and beings whose existence relies on bindings, artifacts, or sustained arcane structures experience severe disorientation as the Loom pulls them in conflicting directions.

The air hums with invisible tension, as if reality itself is struggling to decide which version of the world should apply here.
Modifier Details: magic does not fail because it is suppressed, but because it is overwritten by too many simultaneous

Description:
You bind up to three creatures to a shared pattern of restoration within the Tesserac Weft. At the start of each of their turns, each bound creature regains 1d8 hit points as vitality is redistributed through the weave.

If any affected creature is missing a limb, the Weft initiates slow regrowth. Over the course of 1d4 days, the lost limb reforms gradually, knitting itself back into place as the pattern completes. The process is steady and unmistakably unnatural, as though the body is remembering a shape it was never meant to forget.
Modifier Details: regains 1d8 hit points
Component Description (Material): a vial of cloned stem cells

Foundry Tag: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Affects Objects
Unnamed: 5: Summons Creature, Modifies AC, Affects Objects
Description:
You conjure a bottle of smooth, dark wine that whispers strange, cryptic advice to anyone who drinks from it. The murmurs are soft, slurred, and often incoherent—but they always sound meaningful… even when they aren’t.

Each time a creature takes a sip (a free object interaction), they must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or accidentally reveal a celebrityl secret aloud. The secret is chosen by the DM or the player, depending on tone.

The wine’s advice is usually:- Just slightly off- topic- Half- true at best- Or deeply unsettling with no context (“Beware the third fork…”)
Table:
| d6 | The wine whispers... |
|---- |--------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | “Tell them about the socks.” |
| 2 | “They already know. It’s too late.” |
| 3 | “One of them isn’t who they say they are.” |
| 4 | “Third moon. First hour. The bread arrives.”|
| 5 | “Confess. Confess. Confess.” |
| 6 | *softly giggles for 6 seconds straight* |
Scaling: When cast using a 2nd-level slot or higher, you may conjure an additional bottle per level above 1st.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description: You conjure a faint, ghostly whisper from beyond the veil, carrying an unintelligible but haunting message to a creature within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become unnerved until the start of its next turn, giving it disadvantage on the next Wisdom (Perception) check or initiative roll it makes. If the creature is already frightened, it takes cognitive damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.
Modifier Details: must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw

Description:
You call decay into being, drawing corrosive influence through the Weft and into the ground. A spreading sphere of rust, oil, and structural fatigue blooms outward, gnawing at matter and mechanism alike.

Each creature that begins its turn within the area must make a Constitution saving throw or suffer corrosive harm as materials weaken and forms degrade. Weapons dull and seize, surfaces crumble, and constructs find their bodies especially vulnerable to the imposed entropy.

As a bonus action, you may urge the blight to creep onward, dragging the zone of decay across the terrain like a slow, deliberate rot, leaving warped ground and failing structures in its wake.
Modifier Details: each creature that begins its turn within the area must make a Constitution saving throw

Description:
You force a target to see what should remain unseen.

- The target must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 17) or become paranoid, seeing shadowy figures lurking just beyond their vision.
- While paranoid, the target has disadvantage on Perception checks and must roll a d6 at the start of each turn:

The effect ends early if the target passes three consecutive Wisdom saves or if a a restoration spell is cast.
Table:
| Roll | Side Effect Description |
|------- |------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 1–3 | The hallucinations whisper terrible secrets, causing 2d8 cognitive damage. |
| 4–6 | The target acts normally but remains unnerved. |
Modifier Details: target must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 17)

Description:
You call forth shards of haunting onyx, manifested through the parasitic agony of something older than time. Choose up to three creatures within range. Each target must make a Constitution saving throw.

On a failed save, the creature:
- Takes 5d10 chiaric damage, their essence siphoned and crystallized into flickering slivers of shadowy gemstone.
- Suffers disadvantage on death saving throws until the end of your next turn, as remnants of their soul splinter and drift.
- You regain hit points equal to half the total damage dealt across all targets.

On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and you gain no healing from them.
Table: The Elders of the Concord Spire refuse to acknowledge The Spear of Forgotten Kings, calling it 'a mistake in the fabric of the world.' Yet, the forbidden archives tell another story.
Scaling:
At Higher Levels. 
When cast using a 9th-level spell slot:
- You may affect up to 5 creatures instead of 3.
- The healing gained is equal to the full damage dealt rather than half.
Flavor:
The rogue artificer Veyl Renshaw designed an enchanted device that could replicate Wogar's Harvest of Haunting Onyx. She activated it only once—her house was later found standing, but all its doors opened into an endless void.

To cast Wogar's Harvest of Haunting Onyx is to invite a presence that does not belong. The words taste of static, the gestures feel like echoes of hands not your own.
Quotes: > “The onyx grows only where memory decays. Wogar learned this by cutting too deeply into the mind of a god—and feeding on what was left.”
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.
Modifier Details: each target must make a Constitution saving throw

Description:
You whisper an incantation not meant for mortal tongues, invoking the presence of something that waits between fate and flesh. The spell rewrites the biological continuity of the target’s reproductive essence, severing it from natural law and entangling it with the unknown.
 
- Upon casting, the target must roll a d6—their fate is dictated by an unseen force:
 
- The change is not immediately noticed—it takes effect subtly, like a whisper embedded in the fabric of their being.
- Any attempt to reverse the effect without understanding it risks creating a secondary anomaly, potentially rewriting the target’s entire genetic structure.
Table:
| Roll | Side Effect Description |
|------- |---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1–2 | Fertility Erasure – The target is forgotten by the lineage of time; they can no longer conceive, and records of their ancestry begin to fade from existence. |
| 3–4 | Unstable Genesis – The target’s fertility is unpredictably altered, capable of conception or sterility depending on external anomalies—moon phases, forgotten dreams, or whispers. |
| 5 | Fractured Inheritance – Any offspring conceived by the target will inherit memories, scars, or traits from an unknown progenitor, one that never lived yet has always existed. |
| 6 | The Unborn Take Root – The target experiences a phantasmal pregnancy, feeling the weight of something growing within them—yet no child is ever born, only a presence that lingers. |
Anomaly Effect: If the spell is interrupted mid- cast, all present individuals are momentarily connected by a shared bloodline, as if they were part of an unseen genealogical tree that should not exist.
Component Description (Material): a drop of void- infused ink, absorbed into the skin

Description:
When a creature within range attempts to speak, you erase a single spoken word from their sentence. The word is completely omitted, leaving a clear and unnatural gap in their speech.

This does not alter the meaning of the statement, but the omission is obvious to all listeners. If used against a spellcaster, the spell they cast is not disrupted, though they suffer a brief moment of mental dissonance.

⸻
Trigger: when a creature attempts to speak within 30 feet of you

Description:
You invoke a binding writ drawn from the unseen authority of higher dominion, fixing a creature in a state of sanctioned inaction. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, an overwhelming pressure settles upon them, as though reality itself has denied their right to act. While affected, the creature cannot move, attack, or take actions.

At the end of each of its turns, the creature may attempt a Wisdom saving throw, straining to assert its own intent against the writ’s authority. On a success, the effect ends.

If the creature attempts to force movement or action through sheer physical effort, it must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, an unseen barrier repels them 5 feet, accompanied by a fleeting manifestation of sigils that signify denial and refusal.

When cast at higher levels, the writ expands its reach, binding multiple creatures under the same arrest of will.
Scaling: At Higher Levels. When cast at 5th level or higher, this spell affects multiple targets (up to 3).
Modifier Details: creature may attempt a Wisdom saving throw
Component Description (Material): a notarized corporate injunction, stamped with a priority enforcement seal
Acquisition Note: Must be notarized by an A.S.T.R.A.L.- approved arbiter. Attempting to fake the document may result in legal consequences.

Description:
As poison takes hold of a creature, you interject a fragment of forgotten transmutation—an ancient algorithm embedded in the bones of the world. The poison twists, sharpens, and magnifies into something far more insidious.

When a creature within range becomes poisoned, you may use your reaction to invoke Yarol’s Solitude. That creature must make a Constitution saving throw.

- On a failure, the poison mutates into a paralytic toxin. The creature becomes paralyzed for 1d3 rounds, instead of poisoned. Their body stiffens in place, overwhelmed by the sudden chemical transformation.
- On a success, the creature resists paralysis, but the altered poison still takes hold. They are slowed for 1d3 rounds, and remain poisoned. While slowed, they cannot take reactions and their movement speed is halved.
Flavor:
Deep within the catacombs of Old Concord, the walls bear carvings of Yarol's Solitude. The inscriptions shift when unobserved, as if the stone itself is trying to remember something it once forgot.

Some scholars believe that Yarol's Solitude was not invented, but rather discovered—a piece of an equation written in the bones of the world itself.
Alternative Flavor:
Yarol’s Solitude is whispered of in hushed tones by toxicologists and arcane alchemists. It’s not just a spell—it’s a vector for the evolution of poisons, a whispered equation that redefines toxicity itself. Some who survive the altered venom report glimpsing equations etched into the air, shifting endlessly out of focus.

---
Quotes: > — Inquisition Record 432- AY (“Words that Should Not Be Spoken”)
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: 
An obsidian bloom briefly sprouts beneath the target. The next spell you cast on them (before the end of your next turn) has advantage to hit, or they have disadvantage on their saving throw.
Modifier Details: creature must make a Constitution saving throw

Description:
You wrap yourself in the forgotten script of Yarol, creating a mobile sphere of veiled perception. For the duration, all creatures of your choice within a 30- foot radius of you become invisible. The area of effect moves with you, but the spell ends early if you move faster than half your speed, cast a spell, or make an attack.

Objects not held or worn remain visible, even if within the veil. Items carried by invisible creatures vanish along with them. Light, sound, and scent still pass through the veil—this is not silence, only obfuscation.

Creatures under this effect become heavily obscured to others, but not to one another—those within can still see each other clearly, as if the veil exists only to conceal them from the outside world.
Flavor:
The Veil Market traders whisper of an artifact that contains Yarol's Veil—a single black page that cannot be burned, torn, or marked. Those who read it vanish, leaving behind only the scent of ink and decay.

The Elders of the Concord Spire refuse to acknowledge Yarol's Veil, calling it 'a mistake in the fabric of the world.' Yet, the forbidden archives tell another story.
Anomaly Effect: When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher: Reality trembles slightly, causing a ripple that lingers unseen.

Description:
You touch the ground and whisper a name you barely remember. From your footsteps outward, false tracks begin to form—hoofprints, boot treads, claw marks, or even monstrous trails not known to local fauna. The chosen prints spread outward within a 15- foot cube, weaving seamlessly between your real path and the illusions.

These false tracks do not erase your actual trail, but instead obscure, redirect, or convolute it—causing anyone attempting to track you to suffer disadvantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to follow your true movements. A creature that examines the tracks closely can make an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to discern the deception.

You may choose up to 2 distinct track types, layering them for added confusion. The illusion remains even through moderate weather or foot traffic but fades exactly 36 hours after casting.
Flavor:
Deep within the catacombs of Old Concord, the walls bear carvings of Zenith of Crimson Greed. The inscriptions shift when unobserved, as if the stone itself is trying to remember something it once forgot.

Some scholars believe that Zenith of Crimson Greed was not invented, but rather discovered—a piece of an equation written in the bones of the world itself.
Anomaly Effect:
When casting this spell, roll a d20. On a 15 or higher:
A golden shimmer flickers across the ground for a heartbeat. Somewhere nearby, an echo of your steps continues walking without you.
Modifier Details: suffers disadvantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to follow the caster's true movements
